title: Bias- and Frequency-Dependent Optimization of Gate Length for Efficiency in GaN-on-SiC HEMTs With a Fit Predictive Model
authors: Jiongjiong Mo, Beibei Lv, Xinran Chen, Xuran Zhao
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: The efficiency of GaN-on-SiC high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs) is limited by the combined influence of the trap-induced current collapse, knee-voltage walkout, and parasitic capacitances. While the gate-length scaling is known to affect these mechanisms, the optimum geometry for maximum power-added efficiency (PAE) under the realistic bias and frequency conditions has remained unclear. Here, we investigate the gate length impacts on efficiency. Comprehensive DC, pulsed-IV, small-signal, and large-signal measurements reveal a nonmonotonic dependence of efficiency, from which a bias-frequency regime map for gate-length optimization has been established, and a predictive model that links PAE to small-signal f<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</sub> and applied bias was introduced. The model reproduces measured data with high accuracy and offers a compact route to efficiency prediction from routine RF characterization. Together, the regime map and predictive model provide a physically grounded framework for bias- and frequency-aware design of GaN HEMTs for millimeter-wave power amplification.

title: Hierarchical Multi-Domain Collaboration Based on DRL: An Intelligent and Generalized Architecture for Multi-Satellite Collaborative Communications
authors: Yuanzhi He, Biao Sheng
abstract: Multi-satellite collaborative communications (MSCC) is emerging as a cornerstone of 6G satellite networks to ensure ubiquitous coverage and high quality of service (QoS). However, the non-stationary channel conditions, limited link resource constraints, highly dynamic topology, and rapidly increasing problem scale pose significant challenges to traditional optimization methods. Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers a promising solution, yet current algorithms are tailored to specific problems and lack a holistic perspective. To bridge this gap, this paper presents a comprehensive survey and proposes an intelligent, generalized architecture for multi-satellite collaboration. First, we synthesize a set of algorithmic design principles along the dimensions of problem type, observability, and system scale, providing a systematic guide for algorithm selection. Subsequently, guided by these principles, we establish a cloud-edge-terminal hierarchical federated DRL framework. This architecture leverages geostationary earth orbit (GEO) satellites as intermediate aggregators and global context providers, bridging the gap between the ground cloud’s massive computing power and low earth orbit (LEO) edge’s real-time inference needs. This design effectively mitigates partial observability and feeder link congestion, facilitating the resolution of communication optimization issues spanning from the physical layer to the network layer. We propose a hierarchical multi-satellite collaborative framework where LEO agents, guided by GEO-aggregated global context, employ the multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) based algorithm to dynamically optimize semantic modality selection and access control. Simulation results validate that this approach effectively manages multi-level image and text semantic transmission, enhancing system adaptability. Finally, we outline future directions, including communication-computing synergy and embodied intelligence, to guide the evolution of autonomous satellite networks.

title: Performance Analysis of Hybrid-Electric Propulsion Systems for Regional Commuter Aircraft
authors: Gustavo Muller Hauck, Cleverson Bringhenti, Mauricio Andrés Varela Morales, Jesuino Takachi Tomita, Antonio Bruno De Vasconcelos Leitao, Franco Jefferds Dos Santos Silva, Konstantinos Kyprianidis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Aviation plays a fundamental and valuable role in the modern world, yet it faces significant challenges, including high fuel costs and substantial environmental pollution. These issues have prompted the aviation industry to establish emission standards and develop less-polluting propulsion systems, such as those utilizing synthetic fuels, fuel cells, and electrification. Among these, electrification holds promise as a potential solution for reducing emissions in commuter aircraft, despite the mass limitations posed by batteries. In this study, a methodology was developed and implemented within in-house software to simulate the performance of a commuter aircraft with a hybrid-electric propulsion system. The analysis focused on key metrics like fuel economy and climb time to cruise altitude. The EMB-120 Brasilia was chosen as the base aircraft for this research. Its long-standing use by the Brazilian Air Force (FAB), the authors' extensive familiarity with its performance, and the availability of experimental data for lift and drag coefficients made it an ideal model for our simulations. To evaluate the performance of the hybrid propulsion system and compare it with the standard case, a gas turbine was utilized as the primary engine. Mathematical models were developed for the PW118 gas turbine, which powers the real aircraft that was considered the standard case, and for the PT6A-68C, which was suggested as a substitute for the hybrid system. To evaluate the aircraft's performance, a standard mission was simulated on a short-range route of 926 km (500 NM), flying at an altitude of 7,620 m (25,000 ft), a common mission that is used by Brazilian Air Force. For hybrid simulations, battery packs were tested with specific energies ranging from 0.125 kWh/kg to 0.750 kWh/kg, in multiples of the initial value. Batteries with 0.250 kWh/kg were considered the current state-of-the-art, while the 0.750 kWh/kg packs represent an extreme upper bound associated with far-future technological developments. The results demonstrated significant performance gains depending on the chosen battery technology and the power split, the hybrid system achieved fuel savings of up to 18% to 19% and reduced climb time by 17% to 25%.

title: Lightweight Privacy-Preserving and Fault-Tolerant Truth Discovery for Mobile Crowdsensing Systems_supp1-3694180.pdf
authors: Xiaofen Wang
abstract: As a paradigm for encouraging users to contribute data spontaneously, mobile crowdsensing (MCS) has received considerable attention recently. It is crucial to evaluate the truthfulness of MCS data by proper truth discovery mechanisms. Although recent truth discovery schemes can determine truthful information, they either provide limited privacy preservation or have heavy computation and communication overheads. Moreover, most of them are not resilient to malicious faults and active attacks. To tackle the above problems, we propose two fault-tolerant and privacy-preserving truth discovery solutions. Our first scheme is mainly used for scenarios with a relatively stable number of users, where participants do not frequently join or leaves. Integrating ring signature with the perturbation technique, we design an anonymous and privacy-preserving truth discovery scheme, namely RsAnonTD, which can achieve privacy preservation and resist active attacks. To address the challenge with dynamically changed workers, we devise a multi-client inner product functional encryption scheme with a lightweight zero-knowledge proof protocol (namely McFeKDeTD) for defending against active attacks. The security analysis shows that both schemes can preserve the privacy of sensory data, weights, and estimated truths while resisting active attacks, thereby guaranteeing fault tolerance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our designs achieve superior performance than other schemes in terms of accuracy, convergence speed, and system overheads. For example, compared with the state-of-the-art approach RPTD-II, which has a security level comparable to ours, our proposed schemes, RsAnonTD and McFeKDeTD, reduce the computational overheads approximately by 98% and 69%, respectively.

title: Anchor-Driven Compact Gaussian Splatting for Dynamic Scene Reconstruction
authors: He Huang, Qi Yang, Mufan Liu, Wenjie Huang, Yiling Xu, Zhu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Existing 4D Gaussian Splatting methods typically rely on per-Gaussian deformation from a canonical space to target frames, which overlooks the strong redundancy among spatially and temporally adjacent Gaussian primitives and leads to suboptimal efficiency. To address this limitation, we propose ADC-GS++, an anchor-driven compact Gaussian splatting frame work for efficient and high-quality dynamic scene reconstruction. Specifically, ADC-GS++ organizes Gaussian primitives into an anchor-based canonical representation, enabling attribute sharing across local regions. To efficiently model dynamic scenes, we introduce a static–dynamic decomposition mechanism and further employ a coarse-to-fine deformation strategy driven by dynamic anchors at multiple granularities. In addition, a unified rate-distortion optimization is adopted to achieve a balanced trade-off between storage efficiency and reconstruction fidelity. Furthermore, a temporal significance-based anchor refinement strategy is employed to dynamically grow and prune anchors, allowing robust adaptation to complex and large-scale motions. Extensive experiments on multiple real-world dynamic scene datasets demonstrate that ADC-GS++ significantly improves rendering speed over deformation-based approaches by 300% 700%, while maintaining competitive rendering quality. More over, ADC-GS++ achieves a more favorable rate-distortion trade off, resulting in substantially reduced storage consumption across different bitrate settings.

title: Truthful and Trustworthy IoT AI Agents via Immediate-Penalty Enforcement under Approximate VCG Mechanisms
authors: Xun Shao, Ryuuto Shimizu, Zhi Liu, Kaoru Ota, Mianxiong Dong
abstract: The deployment of autonomous AI agents in Internet of Things (IoT) energy systems requires decision-making mechanisms that remain robust, efficient, and trustworthy under real-time constraints and imperfect monitoring. While reinforcement learning enables adaptive prosumer behaviors, ensuring economic consistency and preventing strategic manipulation remain open challenges, particularly when sensing noise or partial observability degrades the operator’s ability to verify actions. This paper introduces a trust-enforcement framework for IoT energy trading that combines an α-approximate Vickrey–Clarke–Groves (VCG) double auction with an immediate one-shot penalty. Unlike reputation- or history-based approaches, the proposed mechanism restores truthful reporting within a single round, even when allocation accuracy is approximate and monitoring is noisy. We theoretically characterize the incentive gap induced by approximation and derive a penalty threshold that guarantees truthful bidding under bounded sensing errors. To evaluate learning-enabled prosumers, we embed the mechanism into a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment reflecting stochastic generation, dynamic loads, and heterogeneous trading opportunities. Experiments show that improved allocation accuracy consistently reduces deviation incentives, the required penalty matches analytical predictions, and learned bidding behaviors remain stable and interpretable despite imperfect monitoring. These results demonstrate that lightweight penalty designs can reliably align strategic IoT agents with socially efficient energy-trading outcomes.

title: SE-Blackboard: A Shared-State Architecture for Multi-Agent Software Engineering Pipelines
authors: Erxi Liu, Qiang Zhu, Xinru Dong
abstract: Multi-agent large language model (LLM) systems are increasingly used for software engineering tasks, yet the role of communication architecture in these systems remains poorly understood. Most existing approaches rely on message passing, where agents communicate through sequential natural language summaries, potentially introducing information loss across pipeline stages. This paper introduces SE-Blackboard, a modular framework that applies a shared-state Blackboard architecture to multi-agent software engineering pipelines.We conduct controlled experiments on 50 SWE-bench Lite issues, comparing three communication architectures: message passing, shared-state blackboard, and a hybrid design. Our results show that communication architecture significantly affects information preservation and intermediate pipeline performance. The blackboard configuration improves Coder-stage information fidelity (IFS) by 62% compared with message passing (0.586 vs. 0.362), which translates into a 27.5 percentage-point improvement in correct file targeting (82.6% vs. 55.1%). However, once the correct file is identified, all configurations converge to a ∼22% conditional resolve rate, revealing a downstream bottleneck in LLM diff generation. As a result, the overall resolve-rate improvement is modest (16% vs. 12%) despite large upstream gains. These findings highlight communication architecture as an important design dimension for multi-agent software engineering systems and suggest that improvements in upstream information quality translate into task-level gains only when downstream generation bottlenecks are addressed.

title: LAS: Reducing Redundant Memory Access in ANNS via Adaptive Learned Indexing
authors: Yi Wang, Huan Liu, Jianan Yuan, Jiaxian Chen, Tianyu Wang, Chenlin Ma, Rui Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search (ANNS) has become a key paradigm for large-scale high-dimensional data retrieval. As a typical ANNS technique, Inverted File with Product Quantization (IVFPQ) provides a promising solution by significantly reducing storage costs through PQ compression. However, IVFPQ suffers from severe redundant memory access issues during retrieval. This paper proposes LAS, a novel ANNS accelerator specifically designed to reduce redundant memory access in IVFPQ. LAS presents two core techniques : dimensionality reduction component and adaptive learned index forest (ALIF). The dimensionality reduction component filters out a large number of non-neighbor vectors in the early retrieval stage. ALIF improves the accuracy of neighbor prediction through a multi-segment indexing design, further reducing redundant accesses caused by prediction errors. Meanwhile, ALIF employs an adaptive search strategy based on query-cluster relationships to dynamically adjust the search range of different clusters. Experimental results demonstrate that LAS achieves significant improvements in retrieval efficiency and effectively reduces redundant memory access in IVFPQ compared to representative works.

title: Energy- and AoI-Aware Hierarchical Personalized Federated Learning for Distributed Edge Systems with Selective LLM Module Exchange
authors: Faranak Solat, Joohyung Lee
abstract: Distributed edge systems require federated learning (FL) frameworks that can jointly support timely model updates, user-level personalization, and communication-efficient coordination. However, conventional hierarchical federated learning (HFL) methods mainly focus on reducing communication overhead and often struggle to maintain model freshness and local adaptability in multi-tier environments with heterogeneous data and resource constraints. To address these challenges, we propose an Energy- and Age-of-Information (AoI)-Aware Hierarchical Personalized Federated Learning (EA-HPFL) framework. The proposed method introduces two key ideas: 1) selective module exchange, in which clients communicate only lightweight adapter, head, or other trainable sub-module updates instead of full model parameters; and 2) deterministic AoI- and energy-aware aggregation, in which freshness and energy indicators guide client- and edge-level aggregation. This design enables efficient hierarchical coordination while preserving local personalization under non-IID data distributions. Experimental results on WESAD, HAR, and Amazon-EN show that EA-HPFL provides a favorable trade-off among communication efficiency, update freshness, and personalized performance across diverse edge settings. In particular, the proposed framework maintains competitive accuracy while substantially reducing the overhead associated with full-model exchange, and the revised TinyLlama-based Amazon-EN evaluation further confirms its effectiveness in an LLM-oriented setting. These results demonstrate the practical value of AoI-aware coordination and selective sub-module exchange for scalable and adaptive FL in real-time intelligent edge systems.

title: Rigor Over Hype: What Foundation Model Should Mean in Computational Science
authors: Youngsoo Choi, Siu Wun Cheung, Youngkyu Kim, Ping-Hsuan Tsai, Alejandro N. Diaz, Ivan Zanardi, Seung Whan Chung, Dylan Copeland, Coleman Kendrick, William Anderson, Xiaolong He, Traian Iliescu, Matthias Heinkenschloss
abstract: The term foundation model is rapidly entering scientific machine learning, yet its meaning in computational science remains inconsistent. Models are increasingly labeled foundational without evidence of broad generalization across physical systems, geometries, boundary/initial conditions, tasks, or operating regimes. We propose a definition of foundation models tailored to computational science and identify core and desirable characteristics, drawing inspiration from classical foundational methods (FEM/FVM/FDM) that achieve wide reuse through problem-independent structure and principled enforcement of governing equations. We argue that scientific foundation models face unique barriers relative to language and vision models, including field-valued data, mesh and geometry dependence, heterogeneous physics, expensive adaptation, limited benchmarks, and strict requirements for interpretability and physical consistency. To make claims measurable, we introduce a reviewer- and author-friendly checklist for reporting training breadth, generalization axes, adaptation cost, extrapolation protocols, and scientific consistency checks. We also highlight a constructive hybrid path: local learning with global physics enforcement.

title: Detecting Hallucinations in Arabic Large Language Models Using Automatic Fact-Verification Signals
authors: Mohammad Alhawarat
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: In many Natural Language Processing tasks like Arabic Large Language Models have shown great performance. But they often produce hallucinations - fluent but not factually correct responses - which are very dangerous in many critical applications in education / healthcare / decision support systems. Though hallucination detection is a well-studied topic for LLMs, Arabic is still mostly understudied despite the appearance of more and more Arabic-capable models. In this paper an automated detector for hallucinations in Arabic LLM outputs is presented based on fact-verification signals. In our approach we use three complementary detection mechanisms: Logical consistency/semantic similarity analysis with sentence embeddings and ensemble classification against Arabic Wikipedia - Arabic Natural Language Inference (NLI). We test two Arabic-capable LLMs (Jais-13B-chat & Meta-LLaMA-3-8B-Instruct) on Arabic question-answering datasets (ARCD and TyDiQA for both training/validation and for cross-dataset testing). Experimental results show that in hallucination detection with both NLI and retrieval signals F1-scores are higher than single-signal baselines. Ablation studies show that NLI is most important to detect accuracy whereas retrieval consistency is very important to complement that evidence. It is the first systematic benchmark for Arabic LLM hallucination detection & offers suggestions for better - tuned Arabic NLP systems.

title: A Vision Transformer-Based Hybrid Encryption Watermarking Algorithm for Medical Image Security
authors: Yu Fan, Jingbing Li, Uzair Aslam Bhatti, Yenwei Chen
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Unauthorized access and tampering of medical images are key problems in medical image security. To address these concerns, this research introduces a Vision Transformer (ViT)-Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT)-based digital watermarking framework incorporating Logistic Map chaotic encryption and a hash-based authentication mechanism function to improve watermark robustness and confidentiality. First, the ViT model is used to extract global features from medical images, and DCT frequency domain transformation is applied to enhance feature representation. A perceptual hash function is then employed to generate a feature vector, and the original watermark undergoes chaotic encryption using the Logistic Map. The resulting encrypted watermark is fused with the image feature descriptor using an exclusive-OR (XOR) operation, generating a logical key that is securely stored for subsequent authentication and integrity verification. During the extraction process, the same feature analysis is performed on the test image to obtain a corresponding descriptor, which is then decrypted using the stored logical key through an XOR to recover the watermark. The extraction effectiveness is assessed by the normalized correlation (NC) coefficient. Experimental evaluation confirms that the proposed scheme provides excellent resistance to both conventional and geometric attacks. Moreover, since the watermark embedding process does not directly modify the medical image content, the original image quality remains unaffected. Overall, this algorithm holds promising potential for ensuring medical image copyright protection and data integrity assurance.

title: Vision Foundation Models Pretrained on Skin Lesion Images Can Classify Oral Lesions Too!
authors: Vedatroyee Pal, Rajarshi Bandyopadhyay, Samarup Bhattacharya, Sanjoy Saha, Tapabrata Chakraborti
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recognizing the critical limitations imposed by data scarcity in Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma (OSCC) detection, this work demonstrates how the domain transferability of Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) provides a powerful solution. We present the first work to employ a VFM, Google's Derm Foundation, pretrained on dermatology images, as a frozen feature extractor for OSCC detection. By training only a shallow Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) classifier, we achieve high-performance with minimal computational load. This lightweight pipeline, validated across multiple datasets, offers robust performance comparable to fully trained Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), establishing an efficient paradigm for early screening in resource-constrained environments. An additional contribution of the work is the combination of multimodal data (image and non-imaging) into an integrated pipeline with improved results.

title: Retina Diseases Identification With OCT Images Using Transfer Learning
authors: Asha Joseph, K Rajakumari
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) is a non-invasive imaging technique widely used to diagnose retinal diseases such as choroidal neovascularization, diabetic macular edema, and drusen, where delayed intervention can cause irreversible blindness. Manual interpretation of OCT scans is time-consuming and subject to inter-observer variability, highlighting the need for automated solutions. This study introduces a clinically oriented deep learning framework employing transfer learning with a ResNet-50 backbone. A two-phase fine-tuning strategy, anatomically realistic data augmentation, and patient-level partitioning were applied to ensure robust training and prevent data leakage. The model achieved 98.21percentage accuracy, with weighted precision, recall, and F1 scores above 98 percentage, and macro AUC of 0.996. Cross-device validation confirmed strong generalizability, while Grad-CAM provided interpretable visual explanations. With rapid inference time of 6.4 millisecond per image, the framework offers an efficient, explainable solution for large-scale retinal screening, enhancing diagnostic reliability and reducing clinician workload.

title: Optical Limit Cycles Induced by Nonlinear Bifurcation in Silicon Microring Resonators
authors: Menglong He, Abdou Shetewy, Kambiz Jamshidi
abstract: In this paper, the dynamics of optical limit cycles induced by free carrier and thermal nonlinearities have been investigated via mathematical modeling and numerical simulation. Besides, the experimental observation of a thermo-optic oscillation has been demonstrated with oscillation frequencies up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 ~ M H z}$</tex>.

title: Analysis of Hard and Soft Switching Loss Imbalance Induced by Parameter Dispersion in Parallel Connected SiC MOSFETs
authors: Dimitrios Deldimos, Andrea Piccioni, Fausto Stella, Gianmario Pellegrino
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Parallel operation of SiC MOSFETs is essential in high-current converters since individual switches are limited in current handling capabilities by maximum die size and packaging constraints. However, device-to-device parametric variations lead to unevenly distributed switching currents, thus resulting in unbalanced switching losses and additional thermal stress. This paper presents an experimental investigation of how variations in the intrinsic parameters of the devices can influence loss sharing during hard and soft switching. A population of device samples was tested at datasheet conditions, and switching loss imbalance was investigated on paralleled pairs. Results reveal that even modest parameter mismatch can lead to significant imbalance in both soft and hard-switching transitions. These findings highlight the importance of dispersion-aware design practices for reliable parallel operation in high-current converters.

title: A Multi-Modal Deep Learning Framework for Smart Nutrition Monitoring and Dietary Management
authors: Mangali Sharath Chandra, Gedipudi Abijith, Kotha HarshVardhan, E.Ravi Kumar, Mohammad Irshadulla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: A novel multi-modal deep learning architecture is proposed for the smart nutrition monitoring and dietary management. It merges food recognition, physical activity tracking and hydration analysis that are housed in one health monitoring system. Food identification and nutritional intake estimation are done through a ResNet50-based convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on the Food-101 dataset, which allows the automated analysis of dietary patterns from food images. Moreover, the framework includes the MET-based approach for estimating the energy expenditure from physical activities and personalized hydration targets based on body weight and exercise duration. The key metabolic health indicators, body mass index (BMI), basal metabolic rate (BMR), recommended daily calorie intake and net calorie balance, will be computed and visualized continuously. The system is realized with a Flask backend incorporating a PyTorch inference pipeline and features an interactive dashboard for the real-time monitoring of nutrition, activity and hydration metrics. This framework by unifying different health modalities and reducing the need for manual data entry promotes the datadriven, personalized dietary management and lifestyle awareness.

title: Cross-Attention Fusion of Structural and Deformation-Based Mri Features for Alzheimer's Disease Classification
authors: Shijia Zhang, Xiyu Ding, Brian Caffo, Junyu Chen, Xinyi Zhang, Hadi Kharrazi, Zheyu Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate assessment of Alzheimer's disease (AD) using neuroimaging is important for understanding disease progression and supporting clinical research. Structural MRI (sMRI) is widely used for this purpose, but most deep learning approaches primarily rely on intensity-based features, which may not fully capture subtle morphological variations. Jacobian determinant maps (JSM) provide complementary information by describing localized brain deformations; however, conventional multimodal fusion strategies such as early or late fusion may not fully capture the interactions between these modalities. To address this, we explore a cross-attention fusion approach that explicitly models the relationship between sMRI intensity and JSM-derived deformation features for AD classification. Using data from the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI), we compare cross-attention with pairwise self-attention and four baseline fusion methods. The proposed approach achieved mean ROC-AUC scores of 0.903 (0.033) for AD vs. cognitively normal (CN) and 0.692 (0.061) for mild cognitive impairment (MCI) vs. CN under five-fold cross-validation. The proposed approach achieved the best performance among fusion strategies and achieved higher or comparable performance with only 1.56 million parameters. These findings suggest that cross-attention fusion may serve as a potentially useful approach for integrating structural and deformation-based MRI features in AD classification tasks.

title: A Deep Learning Approach to Political Sentiment Analysis Using Natural Language Processing
authors: Fuad Hailat, Riyad Alshalabi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Political life within the Arab world experienced significant changes amid the period 2014-2015, which altogether impacted citizens political states of mind and introductions. With the fast extension of web get to and social media stages, Arab citizens progressively voiced their suppositions and political opinions straightforwardly on stages such as Facebook and Twitter. This computerized engagement produced a tremendous volume of information that mirrors open estimation over the Arab locale. In reaction, this ponder proposes a methodological system for analyzing political estimations communicated by Arab citizens amid this period, utilizing profound learning strategies inside characteristic dialect handling to empower a more exact understanding of open supposition patterns and their association with major territorial political occasions. The paper aims to classify political sentiments in posts on different texts in Twitter into three main classifications: positive, negative, and neutral, tracing chronological development and linking it to political events, and regional developments affecting Arabic public opinion. Different algorithms were used such as CNNs, LSTM, BERT, SVM, Multinomial Naive Bayes and Logistic Regression. The examination of the test results appears significant contrasts in how each classifier takes care of Arabic political substance. Multinomial Bayes conveyed the foremost reliable execution, coming to an exactness near to 0.47, which proposes that probabilistic approaches can still capture important opinion designs successfully.

title: Carbon-Neutral-Agriculture: Renewable-Energy Powered IoT Systems and Resource Optimization
authors: Molumuri Mohan Rao, Mohd Basit Mohiuddin, Panjala Srinu, A. Satyanarayana, J Madhavan, R Dinesh Kumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The need to sustainable agriculture is increasing, which requires the use of intelligent farm systems, which consume less energy, less water, and less carbon. The study demonstrates a smart farming architecture that uses renewable energy to implement an automated irrigation system and farm management using IoT. The proposed system is based on the combination of a wireless sensor network, weather predictions data, and farmers with a cloud made IoT platform to monitor and make decisions in real-time. Solar energy and energy storage provides power to sensing, communication and actuation units and eliminates the reliance on the traditional power grid. The data collected are processed with edge and cloud analytics to manage water pumps, irrigation and lighting systems with intelligent actuators. The experimental outcomes can prove that there is a high degree of increase of the use of resources, such as lower grid power consumption, optimality of water distribution, and high efficiency of real-time work of the system. The architecture is scalable in order to implement deployment in addition to constant surveillance of the environment, thus fitting precision agriculture applications. The proposed solution leads to the growth of climate resistant and energy efficient smart farming based on the integration of renewable energy, Internet of Things, and automated control systems.

title: Enhancing ECG Signal Compression Robustness Using Noise-Aware Deep Convolutional Autoencoders in IoT-Assisted Remote Healthcare
authors: Alyaa Ali Neamah, Mustafa Ismael
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: High-efficiency compression of the ECG signal is crucial for minimizing transmission bandwidth and power consumption in healthcare and Internet of Things (IoT) systems. In this work, the proposed Deep Convolution Auto encoder (Deep CNN AE) model consists of three stages: encoder, noise channel, and decoder. This allows for efficient signal compression and reconstruction, enabling the model to learn concise representations while maintaining its resistance to noise, under varying noise channel conditions, experiments were conducted using real ECG signals from the MIT-BIH database. The proposed model achieved minimal noise during signal reconstruction and high accuracy, thus outperforming other models (Baseline, Quantized, and Denoise). This model achieved PRD values as low as 1.7%, which is considered very low, along with high PSNR values of up to 41.81 dB and correlation coefficients exceeding 0.99 and compression ratio of 4, this demonstrates the model’s ability to retain the ECG signal shape during retrieval. The experimental findings confirm that incorporating deep convolutional feature extraction with noise-aware latent representations substantially enhances compression robustness and reconstruction quality. Consequently, the proposed Deep CNNAE represents a promising solution for low-power, bandwidthconstrained ECG monitoring in next-generation remote healthcare and wearable biomedical systems Keywords-ECG signal compression, Deep convolutional auto encoder, Noise-aware learning, Biomedical signal processing, IoT-Based remote healthcare.

title: Large Language Models in Cybersecurity AI-Driven Threat Detection and Prevention
authors: Deepika Sharma, Anurag Shrivastava, Bharti Sahu, Ronak Agrawal, Sorabh Lakhanpal, Ankam Sreejyothsna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The Large Language Models (LLMs) have been made considerably more efficient, and the AI helps to identify and eliminate the possible threats to the safety of the cyberspace. The intrusion identification, the automation of vulnerability tests, and the use of complex attack patterns are helpful in identifying a vulnerability and assist in bettering the assault intelligence, which is used in the localization of complex patterns of attacks. However, the significant threats of the implementation of ML-based applications, such as adversarial attacks, data privacy, and model bias, are to be addressed in the relevant manner. LLMs in Cybersecurity: Overview of recent advances The article concerns the future of using LLMs in cybersecurity in terms of how the powerful models can be used to prevent threats before they occur, detect anomalies and facilitate the process of automated response. It also brings out some of the problems and ethical issues of introducing LLM to security systems. By enlisting the strength of the progress of AI, organizations will be able to improve their cyber defense and minimize exposure to the ever-changing character of cyber threats. The authors of the study believe that LLAMs have potential solutions to cybersecurity but more research is needed to reduce vulnerabilities and maximize their use in practice.

title: Obscuration to Clarity: Bone Suppression for Enhanced Localization in Pneumothorax Segmentation of Chest Radiographs
authors: Ananya Shukla, Amog Rao, Siddharth S., Rina Bao
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Chest radiography (CXR) is a primary modality for assessing cardiopulmonary conditions, but its effectiveness is limited by anatomical obstructions (e.g., ribs, clavicles) that hinder accurate pneumothorax segmentation, boundary delineation, and severity estimation. While deep learning-based bone suppression improves soft-tissue visibility, its utility for precise pixel-wise localization remains underexplored. This study investigates the downstream application of bone suppression for pneumothorax segmentation, integrating it as a preprocessing step to mitigate bony obscuration. We evaluate its impact across CNN and Vision Transformer models on two public datasets, where models trained on bone-suppressed CXRs significantly outperform (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex>) non-suppressed counterparts, achieving up to 17 % improvement in Mean Average Surface Distance (MASD), 4.9% in Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC), and 5.9 % in Normalized Surface Dice (NSD), alongside a 9.5% gain in Matthew's Correlation Coefficient (MCC). These results demonstrate bone suppression as an architecture-independent enhancement for pneumothorax localization, improving the reliability of automated CXR interpretation.

title: Predicting Longitudinal Brain Change from Baseline 3D MRI and Detecting Abnormal Atrophy Using Deep Learning
authors: Linh N. N. Le, Evan Fletcher, Duygu Tosun, Jinyi Qi, Audrey P. Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is highly heterogeneous, with characteristic regional changes in patterns of cortical volume. This study aims to predict future longitudinal brain volume changes in older individuals with various stages of clinical diagnoses, from healthy to dementia, using predictions from baseline MRI. We test two deep learning (DL) approaches predicting longitudinal atrophy: (i) a unimodal model using baseline MRI scans from a single time point as predictor and log-Jacobian atrophy map as target, and (ii) a multimodal model including imaging and non-imaging data. We then developed a normative model using mixed linear regression, based on the longitudinal brain changes predicted by the DL model, to detect deviations in the clinical patient population. This preliminary result using the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset demonstrated the efficacy of the proposed framework to detect abnormal brain regions with high atrophy rate related to dementia.

title: 800 V On-Board Charger with Reduced DC-Link Capacitance via Embedded Active Power Decoupling
authors: Qishan Pan, Guanjiang Liu, Dejun Zhang, Haoyu Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: On-board chargers (OBCs) face challenges from bulky DC-link electrolytic capacitors, oversized isolation transformers, and efficiency loss in the isolated DC/DC stage over wide battery voltage ranges. Conventional active power decoupling (APD) circuits can reduce DC-link capacitance requirements. However, they typically rely on additional external switches and energy storage components dedicated to a single function. To improve, this work introduces a two-stage 800V OBC architecture with reduced DC-link capacitance via embedded active power decoupling. The proposed design employs an integrated APD unit, thereby lowering capacitance demand, minimizing transformer volume, and enhancing the performance of the isolated DC/DC stage under wide gain conditions. A 3.3kW prototype, supporting an output voltage range of 600–900V, achieves a peak full-load efficiency of 95.96%, while consistently maintaining high performance across the entire operating range.

title: Indirect MPC with Adaptive DC-Link Voltage Control for a CHB-based Shunt Active Power Filter
authors: Christopher Curtis, Joseph Fourcaudot, Saige Niemi, Darian Smith, Apparao Dekka, Deepak Ronanki
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The fixed dc-link voltage operation of a shunt active power filter (SAPF) produces high current ripple and affects its ability to compensate the grid harmonic currents under light loading conditions (i.e., operating at low modulation indices). In this paper, an indirect model predictive control (MPC) is proposed for a cascaded H-bridge (CHB) based SAPF to compensate the grid harmonic currents. In addition, an adaptive dc-link voltage control philosophy is proposed to adjust the net dc-link voltage depending on the harmonic current magnitude. Through the proposed method, CHB-based SAPF always operates at higher modulation indices, resulting in an improved performance and produces less current ripple, irrespective of the harmonic current magnitude. Also, the redundancy switching state selection algorithm is developed to equally distribute the net dc-link voltage between each H-bridge module in a CHB. Simulation studies are presented to study the performance of the proposed indirect MPC with adaptive dc-link voltage control philosophy for a CHB-based SAPF with current and voltage source type harmonic loads.

title: Identifying Optimal nnU-Net Configuration for Cerebral Microbleed Segmentation
authors: Junmo Kwon, Sang Won Seo, Hwan-ho Cho, Hyunjin Park
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The nnU-Net has become the de facto standard in medical image segmentation. While the generalizability of nnUNet has widely been studied across various domains, we question the optimality of the automated nnU-Net configuration specifically for cerebral microbleed (CMB) segmentation. We identified that the standard nnU-Net setup, particularly the recommended input patch size, is primarily optimized for the heterogeneous nature of multi-organ segmentation, resulting in suboptimal performance for CMB segmentation. To address this limitation, we propose a patch selection algorithm based on a local minima search that fully leverages the imaging characteristics of CMB lesions. Experiments on two independent cohorts demonstrate that our approach improves the overall performance without altering the segmentation backbone, highlighting the important role of preprocessing. Our code is available at https://github.com/junmokwon/CMBPreprocessing

title: AI-Driven Clinical Decision Support for Laser Tattoo Removal: A Review and Framework
authors: Sarah Abdul Zahra Kadim, Omar Nameer Mohammed Salim, Ghaidaa A. Khalid, Esraa M. Hashem
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Laser removal of tattoos is a complex clinical protocol affected by several interacting parameters, such as pigment composition, skin phototype, laser parameter choice and inter-individual variability in the immune clearance process. It might be that clinically-oriented “one-size-fits-all” dosing guidelines are not sensitive enough to make sure that all patients with rare diseases experience a similarly safe and efficient therapy. With recent developments in the field of artificial intelligence (AI), better choice of parameters, better prediction and management of outcomes and complication especially post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation are possible. This survey specifically covers the AI-assisted applications that are related to laser tattoo removal, including robotic and automated designs for intervention delivery, sensor-based strategies to optimize parameters, predictive methods to estimate treatment outcomes, and physics-informed simulations. This need for a hybrid CDSS system is based on the shortcomings reported in previous work such as fragmented data sets and restricted generalization over various phototypes and populations, insipid integration into clinical workflow. As a result, it is proposed to synthesize a conceptual hybrid model in which predictive AI modules are combined with safety guardrails (rule-based) as well as explain ability outputs to ensure safer, personalized treatment planning. Future lines include implementing standardized artificially annotated databases, external validation and enhanced clinical alignment for integration of real life setting in dermatology practice.

title: Non-Active Power Control of Single Phase Dual Active Bridge for improved performance
authors: Amrithesh Kumar, Jayakumar P
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Dual Active Bridges (DABs) are widely used in various power electronics applications. Controlling the nonactive power associated with active power flow will be highly beneficial for the effective utilization of the converter. In this paper, strategies for managing the inductor current and nonactive power in a single-phase DAB are attempted. Non-active power flow control using single phase shift (SPS) modulation, dual phase shift (DPS) modulation, were presented. The triple phase shift (TPS) modulation technique for the control of active and non- active power without compromising efficiency or increasing system complexity is found to be more beneficial.

title: A 6.78-MHz kW-Level 15-cm Air-Gap Misalignment-Tolerant Capacitive Wireless Power Transfer System for EV Charging Employing Roadway-Vehicle DC-DC Converters
authors: Asif Mushtaq Bhat, Ayush Dixit, Sreyam Sinha
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In capacitive wireless power transfer (CWPT) systems for EV charging, coupling variations arising from roadway-to-vehicle coupler misalignment or varying vehicle ground clearance leads to significant change in output power and increased losses. This paper introduces a roadway-vehicle DC-DC converter (RVDC) based CWPT system that mitigates this issue by using two DC-DC converters, one on the roadway-side and one on the vehicle-side. These converters control the input and output DC link voltages of the system during coupling variations to maintain a constant output power while ensuring a resistive loading of the inverter and thereby reducing losses. A comprehensive design methodology for the proposed RVDC based CWPT system is presented. A 6.78-MHz, 15-cm air-gap, 2.2-kW RVDC-based CWPT prototype suitable for EV charging is designed and built. Experimental results with 1-kW output power demonstrate that the inverter loading phase and output power remain constant for up to 50% lateral misalignment between roadway and vehicle-side coupler plates.

title: Research and Practice of the Application of Digital Twin Technology in the Intelligent Inspection System of Hydropower Station
authors: Ligui Wu, Liming Yang, Qiangwen Zong
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Hydropower station inspection is an important part of hydropower station operation. In this paper, the digital twin technology is applied to the intelligent inspection system of the Three Gorges Power Station. Firstly, the realistic three dimension (3D) model scene of the Three Gorges Power Station was realized through digital modeling technology. Secondly, on the basis of the three-dimensional digital model, combined with the image monitoring system of the Three Gorges Power Station, the three-dimensional visualization, model and video fusion, intelligent recognition, virtual reality and other technologies are comprehensively used to realize the functions of intelligent online inspection, intelligent early warning, virtual training and other functions, and meet the requirements of hydropower station inspection.

title: Brain-CLIP: An Individual-Preserving Contrastive Framework for Bidirectional Cross-Modal Brain Connectome Translation
authors: Yaokai Yang, Nan Zhao, Zeyu Zhang, Jing Zhang, Lu Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Understanding the complex relationship between structural and functional brain networks is essential for uncovering the mechanisms that govern brain organization. However, most existing studies model this relationship in a unidirectional manner, either predicting functional connectivity (FC) from structural connectivity (SC) or vice versa, while very few approaches enable true bidirectional translation between the two modalities. To address this gap, we introduce Brain-CLIP, a novel framework inspired by the multimodal contrastive learning paradigm exemplified by CLIP, in which heterogeneous modalities are aligned in a shared latent space through semantic correspondence. Similarly, Brain-CLIP aligns SC and FC in a common embedding space to achieve bidirectional translation. Furthermore, we propose an Individual-Preserving CLIP Loss that maintains precise subject-level alignment between SC and FC representations, enabling the model to capture individual-specific connectomic patterns rather than merely population-level correspondences. Comprehensive evaluations on two large-scale datasets, HCP and ADNI, demonstrate that Brain-CLIP substantially outperforms existing methods. (Code is available here: https://github.com/BrainX-Lab/Brain-CLIP)

title: AgesAI: An Intelligent Cybersecurity Framework for Advanced Threat Detection and Mitigation
authors: Ianjot Singh, Manpreet Singh, Vikas Kamra, Sweta Srivastava, Ishpreet Singh
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: A sudden increase in cyber threats such as a zero-day exploit and advanced persistent threat (APT), has demonstrated some serious shortcomings in conventional signature-based cybersecurity solutions especially the failure to keep up with the changes in attack forms. The paper introduces AgesAI, which is an intelligent cybersecurity system conceptualized as an unsupervised machine learning, Large Language Models (LLMs), and reinforcement learning agent that allows the system to detect and mitigate threats dynamically and based on situational factors. The framework uses Isolation Forest-based anomaly detection to detect the deviations of the usual network behavior, and the purpose of the LLMs is to reason semantically of the contextual threat description as well as high-level security decision support. To promote a dynamic role of security policy composition based on perceived states of the system and response to threats, reinforcement learning is included. The paper provides an elaborate architectural design, theoretical statements and algorithmic workflow that shows the interface between the detection, reasoning and adaptive control layers. Analytical analysis and reference comparison shows that an integrated method like the one can provide a great deal of accuracy in detection, a low false-positive rate, and responsiveness relative to systems based on unchanging rules and regulations. The suggested framework illustrates the possibility of integrating learning, reasoning, and adaptation as a single cyber security framework that will provide a scalable and privacy-aware base of intelligent security solutions deployed by enterprises and clouds in critical infrastructure scenarios in the future.

title: Predicting Future Land Use Patterns in Kurnool District, India using Cellular Automata and Artificial Neural Network Models
authors: Sunandana Reddy Machireddy, Keerthan Kumar Mallipeddi, Sathish Kumar Reddy Baasireddy, Umesh Kodidala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The focal point of this study is the concept of land use and land cover (LULC) changes in Kurnool district, India, using Cellular Automata (CA) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) models in geographic information system environment during the period of 2019-2025. The satellite images were used to study and categorize the land into those categories like water bodies, vegetation, cropland, and dry land using Landsat 8 satellite images. The analysis of changes in 2019-2022 and the forecasting of 2025 were performed using GIS software (QGIS and ArcGIS) and MOLUSCE plugin using Cellular Automata (CA) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) models. The results indicated that the outputs produced a minor change in the water bodies, increase in the cropland areas and dry lands and generally a good match between the simulated and the real 2025 maps (71% accuracy, Kappa = 0.623). This work will help the policy makers and planners to understand land transformation and make better decisions to ensure sustainable development.

title: Automated Prediction of Paravalvular Regurgitation Before Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation
authors: Michele Cannito, Riccardo Renzulli, Adson Duarte, Farzad Nikfam, Carlo Alberto Barbano, Enrico Chiesa, Francesco Bruno, Federico Giacobbe, Wojciech Wanha, Arturo Giordano, Marco Grangetto, Fabrizio D'Ascenzo
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Severe aortic stenosis is a common and life-threatening condition in elderly patients, often treated with Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI). Despite procedural advances, paravalvular aortic regurgitation (PVR) remains one of the most frequent post-TAVI complications, with a proven impact on long-term prognosis. In this work, we investigate the potential of deep learning to predict the occurrence of PVR from preoperative cardiac CT. To this end, a dataset of preoperative TAVI patients was collected, and 3D convolutional neural networks were trained on isotropic CT volumes. The results achieved suggest that volumetric deep learning can capture subtle anatomical features from pre-TAVI imaging, opening new perspectives for personalized risk assessment and procedural optimization.

title: Extending the Input Voltage Range of an Always-Dual-Path Hybrid DC-DC Converter Using Phase-Shift Operation
authors: Katsuhiro Hata, Shinsaku Tanaka, Toru Ashikaga, Yasuhiro Rikiishi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a method to extend the input voltage range of an always-dual-path hybrid (ADPH) DC-DC converter using phase-shift operation with soft charging through a sub-inductor. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve a regulated 12 V output even when the input voltage drops from the nominal 48 V to 40 V at a rated load of 8 A. Furthermore, the impact of duty-cycle and phase-shift combinations on efficiency is analyzed, and practical design guidelines are discussed to improve overall performance.

title: Integrating Multi-Modal Fundus Photography for Precision Diagnosis in Ophthalmology
authors: Fan Xiao, Junlin Hou, Yiqian Xu, Ruiwei Zhao, Haidong Zou, Lina Lu, Yi Xu, Juzhao Zhang, Rui Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: In recent years, multi-modal fundus imaging has become crucial for comprehensive retinal assessment, since different imaging modalities provide complementary information about retinal structures and lesions. As two different imaging tools for diabetic retinopathy (DR) grading, color fundus photography (CFP) and infrared fundus photography (IFP) are highly-correlated and complementary in clinical applications. CFP offers rich color information, where as IFP captures pathological changes from deeper retinal layers. This complementarity is especially valuable in eyes with media opacities, such as cataract or vitreous haze, where CFP quality often degrades. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study to explore a novel multi-modal deep learning framework that effectively fuses information from CFP and IFP for more accurate and robust DR grading, particularly under such challenging conditions. Specifically, we construct a dual-stream architecture Cross-Fundus Transformer (CFT) to fuse the ViT-based features of two fundus image modalities. Moreover, we adopt both the single-modality and multi-modality supervisions to maximize the overall performance for DR grading. Extensive experiments on a clinical dataset consisting of 1,713 pairs of multi-modal fundus images demonstrate the superiority of our proposed method. Code will be released for public access.

title: A Computationally Efficient Method of Power Module Junction Temperature Estimate Considering Ripple Elements for Yearlong Reliability Simulation of Power Electronics Converters
authors: Shahabuddin Khan, Purushottam Khadka, Siraj Ahmed Shahnewaz, Liwei Wang, Buck Brown, Zheyu Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The reliability of power electronic converters is strongly influenced by the power module, where junction-temperature magnitude and cycling accelerate the dominant aging mechanisms in semiconductor devices. Consequently, accurate junction-temperature estimation is essential, and electro-thermal modeling is widely applied on the basis of the power-device loss model and the thermal impedance of the module packaging and cooling system. Previous studies have focused on developing various models to improve accuracy, while few studies emphasize computation efficiency, which is equally important considering reliability assessment with a yearlong mission profile. This paper proposes a computationally efficient algorithm to compute the junction temperature of the power module with transient ripple components. First, a decoupled framework is introduced where the loss model, dependent only on junction temperature, is separated from the thermal model, eliminating the need for co-simulation. Second, an algorithm to decouple the average temperature through a one-dimensional thermal-resistance model and the ripple component through a thermal-impedance model, along with an adaptive simulation-time-resolution setting is proposed. Finally, a case study of a T-type power module for solar applications demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method, achieving a normalized root mean square error of 1.51% and a simulation time speedup of 700 ×.

title: A 22-kW Three-Phase Wireless Charging System Capable of Independent Phase Control
authors: Hyeon-Young Lee, Yeon-Jun Park, Min-Seok Chae, Jun-Sik Hwang, Seojin Kim, Hyeon-Gyu Choi, Euihoon Chung
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a new three-phase wireless power transfer (WPT) system designed to address the efficiency degradation caused by coil misalignment. The proposed system independently controls the dc-link voltage and duty cycle of each primary-side inverter phase, this enables each phase to deliver power at an independently optimized level that reflects the phase-specific coupling variations under misalignment. Since the optimal dc-link voltage varies significantly across phases under misalignment, a systematic optimization process is essential. To this end, this study presents a coil modeling technique, a mathematical analysis method, and a numerical iteration–based optimization procedure. Simulation results verify the validity of the proposed mathematical model, and a 22-kW hardware prototype experimentally demonstrates the effectiveness of the method, achieving 96.74 % efficiency under aligned conditions and 95.17 % efficiency under misaligned conditions. Furthermore, the strong agreement between the simulated and measured waveforms confirms that the proposed design procedure provides a systematic and highly reproducible methodology for high-performance WPT system development.

title: Distilling Expert Surgical Knowledge: How to Train Local Surgical VLMs for Anatomy Explanation in Complete Mesocolic Excision
authors: Lennart Maack, Julia-Kristin Graß, Lisa-Marie Toscha, Nathaniel Melling, Alexander Schlaefer
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recently, Vision Large Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated high potential in computer-aided diagnosis and decision-support. However, current VLMs show deficits in domain specific surgical scene understanding, such as identifying and explaining anatomical landmarks during Complete Mesocolic Excision. Additionally, there is a need for locally deployable models to avoid patient data leakage to large VLMs, hosted outside the clinic. We propose a privacy-preserving framework to distill knowledge from large, general-purpose LLMs into an efficient, local VLM. We generate an expert-supervised dataset by prompting a teacher LLM without sensitive images, using only textual context and binary segmentation masks for spatial information. This dataset is used for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and subsequent Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) of the locally deployable VLM. Our evaluation confirms that finetuning VLMs with our generated datasets increases surgical domain knowledge compared to its base VLM by a large margin. Overall, this work validates a data-efficient and privacy-conforming way to train a surgical domain optimized, locally deployable VLM for surgical scene understanding.

title: Self-Supervised Vision Transformer for Surgical Phase Recognition in Endoscopic Submucosal Dissection
authors: Gyanateet Dutta, Aya Hammad, Thomas Archer, Qi Dou, Noor Mohammed, Sharib Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Surgical phase recognition is critical for context-aware interventions, but deep learning models require large, costly annotated datasets. This work presents DINO-Endo, an efficient framework pairing a self-supervised DINOv2 Vision Transformer (ViT-S/14) backbone with lightweight temporal models. Through sequential domain adaptation—pretraining on unannotated porcine videos followed by human endoscopic videos—our approach produces highly discriminative features that enable simple temporal decoders to achieve state-of-the-art performance. We demonstrate 89.5% accuracy on patient dataset and 90.0% on complex porcine dataset, outperforming existing methods while using 14-times fewer parameters than foundation models like V-JEPA2. Our comprehensive evaluation reveals that powerful SSL features reduce temporal modeling complexity, with dataset characteristics determining optimal decoder architecture. This work establishes that domain-specific self-supervised pretraining combined with efficient temporal heads achieves state-of-the-art results while reducing annotation dependency and computational requirements for clinical deployment.

title: BrainTAP: Brain Disorder Prediction with Adaptive Distill and Selective Prior Integration
authors: Zhenyu Lei, Aiying Zhang, Song Wang, Han Fan, Jundong Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Predicting clinical outcomes from brain networks in largescale neuroimaging cohorts such as the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study requires effectively integrating functional connectivity (FC) and structural connectivity (SC) while incorporating expert neurobiological knowledge. However, existing multimodal fusion approaches are shallow or over-homogenize the inherently heterogeneous characteristics of FC and SC, while expert-defined anatomical priors are underutilized with static integration. To address these limitations, we propose Brain Transformer with Adaptive Mutual-Distill and Selective Prior Fusion (BrainTAP). We introduce Adaptive Mutual Distill (AMD), which enables layer-wise information exchange between modalities through learnable distill-intact ratios, preserving modalityspecific signals while capturing cross-modal synergies. We further develop Selective Prior Fusion (SPF), which integrates expert-defined anatomical priors in an adaptive way. Evaluated on the ABCD dataset for predicting attentionrelated disorders, BrainTAP achieves superior performance over state-of-the-art baselines, demonstrating its effectiveness for brain disorder prediction.

title: A Multi-Scale Linear-Time Encoder for Whole-Slide Image Analysis
authors: Jagan Mohan Reddy Dwarampudi, Joshua Wong, Hien Van Nguyen, Tania Banerjee
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We introduce Multi-scale Adaptive Recurrent Biomedical Linear-time Encoder (MARBLE), the first purely Mamba-based multi-state multiple instance learning (MIL) framework for whole-slide image (WSI) analysis. MARBLE processes multiple magnification levels in parallel and integrates coarse-to-fine reasoning within a linear-time state-space model, efficiently capturing cross-scale dependencies with minimal parameter overhead. WSI analysis remains challenging due to gigapixel resolutions and hierarchical magnifications, while existing MIL methods typically operate at a single scale and transformer-based approaches suffer from quadratic attention costs. By coupling parallel multi-scale processing with linear-time sequence modeling, MARBLE provides a scalable and modular alternative to attention-based architectures. Experiments on five public datasets show improvements of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 9 \%}$</tex> in AUC, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0. 3 \%}$</tex> in accuracy, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 \%}$</tex> in C-index, establishing MARBLE as an efficient and generalizable framework for multi-scale WSI analysis.

title: Modified Mantis Shrimp Optimization-Based Global MPPT for Photovoltaic Systems under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Nisha G Poothullil, Abdul Saleem
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) techniques are essential for enhancing the efficiency of photovoltaic (PV) systems under dynamically changing environmental conditions. Under partial shading conditions (PSC), PV arrays exhibit multiple peaks in their power-voltage (P-V) characteristics, posing a significant challenge for conventional MPPT techniques to accurately locate the Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP). Although traditional techniques perform well under uniform irradiance, they often fail under PSC due to their inability to distinguish between local and global peaks. To address this, a Modified Mantis Shrimp Optimization (MSO) algorithm is proposed in this paper. Inspired by the predatory and sensory behavior of mantis shrimps, the algorithm effectively balances exploration and exploitation to ensure rapid and accurate tracking of the GMPP. The proposed MSO algorithm demonstrates improved tracking performance with faster convergence, reduced power oscillations, and high efficiency. MATLAB-based simulation results under various partial shading scenarios validate the superiority of the proposed approach when compared with conventional P&O, PSO, and GWO techniques. The MSO algorithm exhibits excellent dynamic response and robustness, making it a promising solution for real-time MPPT in PV systems under non-uniform irradiance.

title:
A 91%, 1,113A/inch
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    , 12-52V Input Voltage Fibonacci Hybrid Converter with 5 Flying Capacitors for Point-of-Load Conversion
authors: Hua Chen, Young-Seok Noh, Minxiang Gong, Arijit Raychowdhury
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-phase Fibonacci hybrid converter with Gallium Nitride (GaN) switches for high step-down ratio, high power efficiency, and high current density. The proposed converter achieved 13:1 step-down ratio from input voltage to inductor-side switching nodes. This architecture reduces the converter area and alleviates the stress caused by short switch on-time, thereby minimizing switching transition loss. The equivalent output resistance in dual-phase interleaving operations is nearly half of that in a single-phase Fibonacci hybrid converter. Through the Fibonacci mathematical sequence, the proposed converter operates over 12-52V input voltage to 0.8-2V output voltage. The proposed converter demonstrates 91% peak efficiency at 500kHz under 1A load current and 1,113A/inch<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> current density at 12A load current for a 52V to 1V conversion ratio.

title: High-Performance FCS-MPC for a New Five-Level Voltage Source Inverter
authors: Ali Azimi Bizaki, Apparao Dekka, Deepak Ronanki
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The efficacy of finite control-set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) for multilevel inverters (MLIs) is contingent upon the precision of the system’s mathematical models. Particularly, the prediction accuracy is interlinked with the accuracy of mathematical models, which further influence the harmonic performance and switching frequency of MLIs with FCS-MPC methods. This research proposes an enhanced FCS-MPC approach for a novel five-level voltage source inverter (5L-VSI). The new 5L-VSI has lesser flying capacitors (FCs) and possesses sufficient redundant switching states to provide flexible regulation of FC voltages and inverter currents. Additionally, the discrete-time (DT) models of the 5L-VSI’s currents and FC voltages are formulated using Heun’s discretization method to enhance the performance of the proposed FCS-MPC. The proposed Heun’s-based DT models minimize the discretization error in the predicted control variables, which further results in the reduction of switching frequency and total harmonic distortion in the output currents of MLIs. Through simulation studies, the performance of the proposed enhanced FCS-MPC is exhibited under steady state and transient scenarios. It is further analyzed in conjunction with the existing FCS-MPC method to demonstrate its benefits.

title: Prediction of Crop Yield Using AI Approach
authors: Megavath Aravind Kumar, Swarup Nandi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Agriculture remains vital to both the economy and food security of India, but accurate crop yield forecasting remains hampered by climatic variability, soil heterogeneity, and limitations in traditional estimation methodologies. Traditional methods are mainly based on historical records, which hardly consider real-time environmental conditions and thus cannot produce reliable predictions. This paper, therefore, proposes a hybrid machine learning-based framework for crop yield prediction using multi-source agricultural data. The given framework combines weather parameters, soil nutrient data, and historical data of crop yield, collected from Medak district. Individual models such as Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) are trained and evaluated to assess their predictive capabilities. A hybrid ensemble model has been further developed in order to address the limitations of single model approaches by synergistically integrating the advantages of these techniques. To validate the generalizability of our findings, we utilized the standard metrics of $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ score, Mean Absolute Error(MAE), Root Mean Square Error(RMSE), and accuracy besides $\mathbf{k}$-fold cross-validation to ensure robustness. The experimental results indicate that the proposed hybrid ensemble model achieves a maximum prediction accuracy of 95.82%, outperforming individual machine learning models in terms of stability and error reduction. The key contributions of this research are three-fold: (i) the integration of multi-source district-level data such as weather and soil nutrients for crop yield prediction (ii) the design of a hybrid ensemble model that combines DT, RF, SVM, and ANN to address the limitations of individual models when data is limited, and (iii) the evaluation of the model using accuracy, $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$, MAE, and RMSE, which outperforms the individual models. The dataset is for a period of ten years, from 2015 to 2025, covering important nutrients in the soil.

title: Variance-Penalized MC-Dropout as a Learned Smoothing Prior for Brain Tumour Segmentation
authors: Satyaki Roy Chowdhury, Golrokh Mirzaei
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Brain tumor segmentation is essential for diagnosis and treatment planning, yet many deep learning approaches, particularly convolutional neural networks, such as UNet and its variations produce noisy boundaries in regions of tumor infiltration. We introduce UAMSA-UNet, an Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Scale Attention-based Bayesian UNet that leverages Monte Carlo Dropout to impose a data-driven smoothing prior while fusing multi-scale features and attention maps to capture both fine details and global context. The smoothingregularized loss augments binary cross-entropy with a variance penalty across stochastic forward passes, suppressing spurious fluctuations and yielding spatially coherent masks. On BraTS2023, UAMSA-UNet improves Dice by up to 3.3% and mean IoU by up to 2.7% over UNet; on BraTS2024, it delivers up to 4.5% Dice and 4.0% IoU gains over the best baseline. It also reduces FLOPs by 42.5% relative to UNet++ while maintaining higher accuracy. By coupling multi-scale attention with a learned smoothing prior, UAMSA-UNet achieves higher-quality, more efficient segmentation and provides a flexible foundation for future integration with transformer modules.

title: Using Google Authenticator and HashiCorp Vault to Build a Security System to Boost Digital Wallet Security and Recovery Secrets
authors: Samah Mahmood Ahmed, Raad A. Muhajjar, Mishall Al-Zubaidie
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: This paper presents a system designed to enhance and extend the security architecture of digital wallets by integrating HashiCorp Vault (HV) and Google Authenticator (GA) technologies with Amazon Web Services (AWS). GA is used as a mechanism to strengthen digital wallet security by protecting encryption keys and passwords from attackers. Access rights management is implemented in HV using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) capabilities, in addition to HV’s own encryption and secret management capabilities. By synchronizing these technologies, we create a comprehensive security system capable of mitigating the risk of unauthorized access to digital wallets and recovering encrypted and stored statements, while simultaneously restricting access to them. The results, based on the performance of the designed system, demonstrate the benefits of rapid response. When testing a database of 5,000 entries, we found that the recovery and storage processes were 100% successful. Running the Python code using Python3 entropy_test.py resulted in a quality mark of 97.02% with a Shannon entropy of 3.2914 bits, plus an achievable maximum mark of 3.3926 bits, indicating that the data generation process is random and reliable within the limits of a robust and reliable system.

title: Swarm Intelligence Based Approach Towards Construction of an Optimal Portfolio of Stocks
authors: Karthikeyan P, Manjula K A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Nature-inspired metaheuristic algorithms are popular for their capability to solve complex issues. Among the Nature-inspired computing techniques, Swarm intelligence techniques, especially Ant Colony Optimisation (ACO) algorithm are a widely used technique. ACO is inspired by how ants find paths. This study presents a novel approach to portfolio optimization based on the principles of Ant Colony Optimisation (ACO) algorithm. We applied this method to historical stock data of eight selected companies in India. The basic principles of search in ACO is used along with the principles of Modern Portfolio Theory to create the best possible portfolio. Our aim is to achieve the highest Sharpe Ratio, even when the market is highly unpredictable. In this study, the portfolio is created using data from eight major Indian stocks collected from the NSE, for the years from 2015 to 2024. A Sharpe Ratio of 1.45 is achieved, with an expected annual return of 18.2% and a risk level (volatility) of 10.5%. These results indicate that the new method based on ACO is an effective tool for developing an optimized portfolio.

title: Deep-GyralNet: Enabling Machine Learning in Gyral Folding Pattern Extraction on Cortical Surface
authors: Chao Cao, Jiale Cheng, Minheng Chen, Jing Zhang, Tianming Liu, Lu Zhang, Dajiang Zhu, Gang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The 3-hinge gyrus (3HG), where three gyri converge, acts as a structural and functional hub in the brain. Connecting these hubs forms the GyralNet, but traditionally extraction requires complex geometric steps, limiting scalability. To address this challenge, we propose Deep-GyralNet, a novel deep learning framework for efficient 3HG and GyralNet extraction. Deep-GyralNet leverages a Spherical U-Net to learn gyral crest representations from cortical morphological features, coupled with a Connectivity-Aware Path Enforcement loss that enforces topological continuity of predicted ridges. A minimum spanning tree-based refinement is then applied to ensure structural connectivity and yield a topologically valid GyralNet. Experiments on the Human Connectome Project dataset demonstrate that Deep-GyralNet achieves an average completeness of 98%, while reducing processing time by 94% compared to the conventional pipeline. These establish DeepGyralNet the first deep learning solution for fast and accurate 3HG identification, enabling large-scale studies of gyral folding networks in brain development, aging and disorders.

title: Enhanced Classification Using Dynamically Weighted Attribute Valued Averaged One-Dependence Estimators (DAVA-AODE)
authors: M. Vigilson Prem, D. Paul Raj, P.S. Priyanka, V. Chrissy Panfila
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
