abstract: In a variety of classification situations, the Averaged One-Dependence Estimator (AODE) classifiers have proven to be useful due to their interpretability and their ability to perform classification. Unfortunately, these classifiers usually struggle when trying to handle classification datasets with characteristics of different relevance but same weight, leading to inferior performance. To circumvent this limitation, a novel dynamically weighted attribute valued averaged one-dependence estimators (DAVA-AODE) proposed in this paper. The DAVA-AODE allows attribute values to be dynamically weighted according to their attribute importance. The dynamic weighting technique overcomes the limitations of conventional ODE classifiers by enabling the classifier to assign greater weight to attributes that contribute more substantially to the classification task under varying conditions. To compare DAVA-AODE's performance to other ODE variants, extensive experiments have been conducted on a wide range of datasets. The results show that DAVA-AODE outperforms traditional ODE classifiers in terms of classification accuracy, precision, and recall.

title: Design and Analysis of Leakage Inductance Integrated Transformer Structures Utilizing Interleaved Windings
authors: Kota Okada, Sihoon Choi, Jun Imaoka, Masayoshi Yamamoto, Takayuki Ikari, Akinori Okubo, Yuichi Iwasaki, Takahiro Tokita
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Power electronics converters have been required to achieve high efficiency and high power density. Magnetic components which occupy a significant volume in converters have been miniaturized to satisfy the requirements. One approach to realize the miniaturization is to reduce the number of magnetic components through the integration of multiple windings within a single magnetic core. This paper proposes two types of integrated transformer structures with interleaved windings that incorporate leakage inductance. This series inductance value can be designed based on the number of primary turns located on either the outer or center leg. A design method based on magnetic circuits for each inductance and the maximum flux density within the core is presented. The feasibility of the proposed magnetic components is validated using a 48V-24V 50W prototype LLC resonant converter.

title: A Noval Integrated-Core Lateral-Flux Coupled Inductor for Vertical Power Delivery in Voltage Regulator Modules
authors: Yu-Chen Chung, Chien-Chun Chan, Huang-Jen Chiu, Yu-Chen Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The design of voltage regulator modules is becoming increasingly challenging, and the size of magnetic components remains a critical factor in achieving high power density. The zero-bias trans-inductor voltage regulator (ZBTLVR) topology eliminates the DC flux among multiple magnetic cores, enabling opportunities for highly integrated implementations. This work analyzes the equivalent circuit model of the ZBTLVR and proposes a five-phase integrated magnetic core based on a lateral-flux coupled inductor for vertical power delivery applications. Finite-element simulations using ANSYS MAXWELL are performed to investigate the magnetic characteristics and phase coupling behavior of the proposed core, and a modified equivalent circuit model is introduced to account for the coupling effects. The simulation results are consistent with theoretical analysis. Finally, experimental validation of the magnetic core is carried out under 12 V input, 1 V output, 1 MHz switching frequency, and 200 A output current.

title: An IoT-Based Multi-Sensor Soil Health Monitoring System with LLM-Assisted Decision Support for Precision Agriculture
authors: Raj Singh, Achintya Singhal, Vandana Sethi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Precision agriculture requires continuous soil observation, but current sensor-based technology is mainly focused on environmental and moisture aspects, offering limited support for monitoring critical soil nutrients and chemical properties essential for crop growth. To overcome this challenge, we developed an ESP32-based multi-sensor system that combines soil moisture, pH, environmental factors, and macronutrient data into an integrated sensing framework capable of providing a comprehensive view of soil health conditions. The collected data are transmitted to a cloud-based web interface, which incorporates a Large Language Model for intelligent, context-aware decision-making and real-time soil health assessment. Unlike conventional machine learning approaches, our system employs context-aware language model reasoning to analyze sensor data, detect soil health anomalies, and deliver practical and timely agricultural advice directly to farmers. The system’s integration and usability are validated through experimental implementation, demonstrating that macronutrient sensing combined with large language model-based decision support are scalable and practical for modern agricultural needs. Notably, results indicate that this continuous monitoring approach provides actionable insights in real time, in contrast to traditional laboratory soil testing, which typically involves multi-day delays.

title: A Quasi-Single-Stage 48 V–to–0.8 V Voltage Regulator Module Based On Half-Bridge Current Doubler With Zero Bias TLVR
authors: Simone Mazzer, Roberto Rizzolatti, Mario Ursino, Kevin Zufferli, Gerald Deboy, Stefano Saggini
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This work introduces a quasi-single-stage 48 V, 350 A VRM voltage regulator module (VRM). A single module combines a four-phase half-bridge current doubler (HB-CD) and a single-phase non-isolated buck converter. The former performs the direct conversion from the 48 V, while the latter from an intermediate bus voltage (IBV) of 5 V. Phase coupling is realized thanks to TLVR, which the buck converter actively supplies to drive the output, achieving zero DC-bias flux in the HB-CD integrated magnetic (ZB-TLVR). Optionally, buck power stages can be further coupled by a conventional, second level TLVR (Cascaded TLVR). A system demonstrator for 48 V/0.8 V has been built. With multifrequency, cascaded ZB-TLVR operation, the converter is capable of fast transient response in an overall efficient (90% peak, up to 84.7% TDC) solution.

title: Pretext Task Adversarial Learning for Unpaired Low-Field to Ultra High-Field MRI Synthesis
authors: Zhenxuan Zhang, Peiyuan Jing, Zi Wang, Ula Briski, Coraline Beitone, Yue Yang, Yinzhe Wu, Fanwen Wang, Liutao Yang, Jiahao Huang, Zhifan Gao, Guang Yang, Peter J. Lally
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Given the scarcity and high cost of high-field MRI, synthesizing high-field images from low-field MRI offers great potential for augmenting limited data in downstream tasks (e.g., segmentation). Low-field MRI typically suffers from low SNR and spatial resolution, making cross-domain synthesis challenging due to feature misalignment and anatomical distortion. To address this, we propose a Pretext Task Adversarial (PTA) framework for high-field MRI synthesis. It consists of: (1) a Slice-wise Gap Perception (SGP) network for aligning inter-slice inconsistencies via contrastive learning; (2) a Local Structure Correction (LSC) network for refining local details from rotated and masked inputs; and (3) a pretext-guided adversarial training that enhances realism through additional supervision and discrimination. Experiments on low-to-ultra-high-field MRI demonstrate state-of-the-art performance (FID =16.892, IS =1.933, MS-SSIM =0.324), generating anatomically consistent high-field-like data to improve downstream training. The code is available at: https://github.com/Zhenxuan-Zhang/PTA4Unpaired_HF_MRI_SYN.

title: LNO piezoelectric resonator temperature characteristic
authors: Alex Marques, Martin Lott, Alexandre Rheinhardt, Marie Bousquet, Gaëtan Perez, Ghislain Despesse
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: One of today’s challenges in the field of power electronics is to increase power-density, which is mainly achieved by increasing the switching frequency. New semiconductor technologies, such as GaN HEMT devices, allow us to increase the switching frequency and achieved greater power-density. Nevertheless, limits are reached with passive materials such as inductors. In this context, piezoelectric resonators (PR) are used to offer an interesting alternative compare to inductor beyond MHz switching frequency. Nevertheless, there is a lack of information on the practical limits in terms of the maximum current that Lithium Niobate (LNO) PRs can withstand in steady state with and without air cooling and on their frequency shift under self-heating induced by current. This paper aims to provide insights on the temperature-dependent shift in the characteristics of LNO resonators and to quantify the current limit of PRs under various practical operating conditions.

title: Optimizing Usability and Security: A Study of Multi-Factor Authentication in Academic Environments
authors: Ahmad Shalaldeh, Seyed Hosseini, Shahbaz Pervez, Ayman Alnsour
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The educational platforms today are exposed to personal information of students and lecturers, rendering them victims of different cyber-attacks that include phishing, dictionary attacks, and brute-force attacks. The conventional authentication that had only included the use of passwords was no longer adequate to offer adequate security. Meanwhile, overly complex security systems may decrease usability levels and cause users to abandon the system. The objective of the study is to prepare an authentication prototype of e-learning platforms, which will incorporate maximum security, as well as the comfort of the user. The solution proposed will contain easy to use password policy with only slight length condition, password salting and hash using of Argon2id algorithm and adaptive multi-factor authentication (MFA) with choice between email, SMS or Google Authenticator. The system was tested in terms of security and usability by applying the John the Ripper tool to imitate a dictionary attack, the heuristic rules created by Nielsen, and the NIST SP 800-63B guidelines. The findings demonstrate that the most basic passwords (e.g., qwerty or password123) are still susceptible, but the extra protection can be ensured by MFA to secure the system. The prototype also meets 17 out of 20 recommendations provided by NIST and all 10 usability heuristics. These results prove that a balance between the security and usability of academic platforms is possible. The given prototype serves as a viable demonstration of how the current cryptography solutions and user-focused design can be implemented in the form of a practical educational authentication system.

title: Data-Driven Prediction and Explainability of Hybrid Nanofluid Density for Thermal Engineering Applications
authors: Sakthi K, Ramya V, Mahalakshmi A, Keerthiga G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Hybrid nanofluids have gained significant attention as advanced heat transfer fluids due to their enhanced thermophysical properties compared to conventional fluids and singleparticle nanofluids. Accurate prediction of hybrid nanofluid density is crucial for the design, optimization, and safe operation of thermal systems such as heat exchangers, solar thermal collectors, electronic cooling devices, and automotive cooling systems. However, experimental determination of density is often costly, time-consuming, and limited to specific operating conditions, highlighting the need for reliable data-driven predictive approaches. In this study, an explainable machine learning and deep learning framework is developed to predict hybrid nanofluid density using a dataset of 436 samples collected from peer-reviewed literature. Key physical and thermophysical parameters, including nanoparticle type, base fluid type, temperature, nanoparticle volume concentration, base fluid density, densities of primary and secondary nanoparticles, and volume mixture ratios, are considered. Four predictive models—Random Forest, XGBoost, CatBoost, and an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)—are evaluated using R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, RMSE, and MAE metrics. Ensemble-based tree models, particularly XGBoost and CatBoost, achieve superior accuracy with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values exceeding 0.99. Explainable artificial intelligence techniques, namely SHAP and permutation importance, are employed to identify influential features, revealing base fluid density, nanoparticle volume concentration, and temperature as the dominant factors. The proposed framework provides accurate, interpretable, and physically consistent predictions for hybrid nanofluid density estimation.

title: Shared-Weight Segmentation with Mask-Conditioned Risk Classification for Carotid Ultrasound
authors: Yujie Sun, Zexuan Fan, Haoyu Wu, Xiaowu Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Stroke is a leading cause of mortality and long-term disability worldwide, with carotid atherosclerosis serving as a major contributor to ischemic events. Carotid ultrasound is routinely used for plaque morphology assessment. However, automated plaque risk grading remains challenging due to small plaque size, low image contrast, and indistinct plaque-lumen boundaries. Moreover, longitudinal and transverse views provide complementary anatomical information that requires coherent cross-view reasoning, which is insufficiently leveraged in the existing automated pipelines. To address these challenges, we propose a two-stage, two-view framework that integrates semi-supervised plaque and vessel segmentation with mask-informed risk classification. The model is trained using 200 labeled cases and 800 unlabeled cases. On the challenge evaluation, our method achieves an F1-Score of 65.50 and a Seg-Score of 62.99, outperforming the official baseline (F1 33.96, Seg-Score 54.71) by 31.54 and 8.28, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating multi-view anatomical priors with semi-supervised learning for the precise grading of carotid atherosclerotic risk. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/06Yujie/CSV2026_Challenge.

title: Seamless Power Transfer of Bidirectional Converters in Microgrids Applications
authors: Emanuel Serban, Cristian Lascu
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Power converters utilizing bidirectional topologies are essential for maintaining continuous microgrid operation and ensuring reliable power delivery to both DC and AC loads. This paper introduces a hybrid AC–DC microgrid architecture that delivers reliable power to various loads while providing enhanced operational flexibility and robust resilience under dynamic and fault conditions. The design and inclusion of a bidirectional DC-DC converter provides critical functionalities such as prioritization of essential loads (e.g., life-support systems), extension of the DC voltage operating range, and galvanic isolation.During dynamic microgrid’s operating conditions, the high-frequency transformer within the DC–DC converter can exhibit increased sensitivity to pulsewidth modulation (PWM) mismatches, saturation and delayed response in power transfer detection. To address these challenges, a microgrid controller is proposed and integrated within the DC–DC converter to enable seamless power transfer during charge and discharge transitions. The proposed communication-free control strategy employs a voltage gradient detection method for rapid event identification and smooth bidirectional power transfer. Each module within the DC microgrid autonomously monitors local parameters and, based on positive or negative voltage gradient variations, determines its operating mode accordingly. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through experimental laboratory studies conducted under dynamic charging and discharging scenarios.

title: AI Transformation: Ready or Already?
authors: Felix Dobslaw, Lucas Gren, Markus Borg, Erik Sterner
abstract: Generative AI promises substantial productivity gains, but many initiatives fail. This column argues that outcomes depend on readiness, not technology alone. We examine AI transformation requirements across organizational, individual, and technological dimensions, highlighting why engineering practices, human factors, and strategic clarity determine success.

title: Evaluation of Commercially Available MHz Magnetic Materials Using a Virtual Datasheet Framework
authors: Burkhard Ulrich
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The adoption of GaN and SiC devices enables power converters to operate in the MHz range, requiring careful selection of magnetic materials to manage core loss and improve power density. To address the lack of a comprehensive overview of available materials, this article presents a comparative evaluation of 45 commercially available materials from 15 different manufacturers, all specified for operation above 1 MHz. An interactive virtual datasheet approach, based on prior work but extended to the MHz range, is introduced to process manufacturer-supplied data. The tool enables performance evaluation and direct comparison of core loss characteristics across materials using power loss density data and standard metrics such as the performance factor. The results provide practical guidance for selecting high-frequency core materials in power converter applications where core loss is a key constraint in magnetic component design.

title: Self-Powered Gate Driver with Nanosecond-Scale Fault Protection for 650 V GaN HEMTs
authors: Seongho Shon, Haoran Meng, Xiaoqing Song, Hui Li
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, self-powered gate driver with nanosecond-scale fault protection designed for 650 V discrete GaN HEMTs. Conventional isolated gate drivers rely on opto-isolators and bulky auxiliary power supplies for signal and power transmission, which increase system complexity. To address these limitations, the proposed design employs a 50 MHz Class-E converter with a novel modulation scheme, enabling simultaneous power and signal transfer through a single air-core transformer. Furthermore, compact di/dt-sensing-based fault protection schemes are integrated by utilizing the small parasitic inductance between the Kelvin-source and power-source terminals of a discrete device. This approach achieves nanosecond-scale fault response time and high-power density. To optimize the scheme for discrete GaN devices and prevent false triggering, a time-domain analysis is performed. The hardware prototype is built and experimentally validated. The results confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the presented gate driver design, demonstrating its suitability for high-density GaN-based power conversion systems.

title: FreshNet 360: A Hybrid Clustering-Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Stage Food Freshness Grading Using Image-Based Quality Signatures
authors: Sampurna Roy, Tanupriya Choudhury, S Christalin Nelson, Minakshi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Maintaining the freshness of perishable food products is a very imperative issue in the field of retail, supplychain management as well as consumer safety. The conventional methods of freshness determination also largely depend on manual examination which is subjective, inconsistent and laborious. To obtain multi-stage food freshness grading, this paper suggests FreshNet 360, a unified, hybrid based machine learning architecture that combines both unsupervised clustering and deep machine learning. An image pipeline was designed with a systematic order that consisted of noise removal, grayscale conversion, edge detection, and generalized augmentation which produced a solid dataset of various types of fruits and vegetables. To obtain natural groupings in the data, three clustering algorithms (K Means, Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering and DBSCAN) were used and the results were mapped onto five freshness levels Fresh, Slightly Aged, Stale, Spoiled and Rotten. Eight deep leaning models, VGG16, VGG19, MobileNetV2, ResNet50, DenseNet121, InceptionV3, Xception, and a custom AlexNet were trained and benchmarked with these labels. Experimental results demonstrate that MobileNetV2 achieves the highest performance with K-Means clustered data (accuracy: 0.77), while AlexNet excels with Agglomerative clustering (accuracy: 0.84). The study highlights the effectiveness of clustering-enhanced label generation in improving classifier stability, enabling scalable, reliable, and automated food freshness detection for realworld applications. The full link to the github repo of this is https://github.com/sampurna7373/FreshNet-360.

title: Submodule-Level Decentralized Control for Cascaded Quadruple Active Bridge-Based Medium-Voltage Converters
authors: Bowen Yang, Vikram Roy Chowdhury, Soham Dutta, Satish Tadala, Manish Tathode, Brian Johnson, Luca Corradini, Dragan Maksimovic, Gab-Su Seo
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: High-power, low-voltage DC (LVDC) loads, such as AI data centers, electric vehicle fast-charging hubs, and hydrogen electrolyzers, are accelerating the deployment of solid-state transformers (SSTs) that directly interface medium-voltage AC (MVAC) to LVDC, streamlining power delivery. This paper proposes a submodule-level decentralized control strategy for an SST comprising a cascaded H-bridge (CHB) active rectifier on the MVAC side and per-module quadruple-active-bridge (QAB) converters on the LVDC side with local controllers. The proposed strategy eliminates the need for high-fidelity, real-time cross-isolation communication by synchronizing all active bridges to a common heartbeat signal and enabling the primary- and secondary-side controllers to generate independent local control signals (phase shifts). The resultant phase shifts naturally govern average and double-line frequency power transfer between active bridges, enabling each side to meet its control objectives using only local measurements. Detailed controller design considerations are presented based on a stability analysis. Comprehensive electromagnetic-transient simulations demonstrate tight DC-link voltage regulation, high-quality grid currents, and robust operation under load transients and grid disturbances, without real-time communication among the submodule controllers. These results indicate that the proposed scheme can significantly enhance the reliability, scalability, and flexibility of cascaded bridge-based medium-voltage converters.

title: A Calibrated Memorization Index (MI) for Detecting Training Data Leakage in Generative MRI Models
authors: Yash Deo, Yan Jia, Toni Lassila, Victoria J Hodge, Alejandro F Frangi, Chenghao Qian, Siyuan Kang, Ibrahim Habli
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Image generative models are known to duplicate images from the training data as part of their outputs, which can lead to privacy concerns when used for medical image generation. We propose a calibrated per-sample metric for detecting memorization and duplication of training data. Our metric uses image features extracted using an MRI foundation model, aggregates multi-layer whitened nearest-neighbor similarities, and maps them to a bounded Overfit/Novelty Index (ONI) and Memorization Index (MI) scores. Across three MRI datasets with controlled duplication percentages and typical image augmentations, our metric robustly detects duplication and provides more consistent metric values across datasets. At the sample level, our metric achieves near-perfect detection of duplicates.

title: Follow Your Heart: Landmark-Guided Transducer Pose Scoring for Point-of-Care Echocardiography
authors: Zaiyang Guo, Jessie N. Dong, Filippos Bellos, Jilei Hao, Emily J. MacKay, Trevor Chan, Shir Goldfinger, Sethu Reddy, Steven Vance, Jason J. Corso, Alison M. Pouch
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Point-of-care transthoracic echocardiography (TTE) makes it possible to assess a patient's cardiac function in almost any setting. A critical step in the TTE exam is acquisition of the apical 4-chamber (A4CH) view, which is used to evaluate clinically impactful measurements such as left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF). However, optimizing transducer pose for high-quality image acquisition and subsequent measurement is a challenging task, particularly for novice users. In this work, we present a multi-task network that provides feedback cues for A4CH view acquisition and automatically estimates LVEF in high-quality A4CH images. The network cascades a transducer pose scoring module and an uncertainty-aware LV landmark detector with automated LVEF estimation. A strength is that network training and inference do not require cumbersome or costly setups for transducer position tracking. We evaluate performance on point-of-care TTE data acquired with a spatially dense “sweep” protocol around the optimal A4CH view. The results demonstrate the network's ability to determine when the transducer pose is on target, close to target, or far from target based on the images alone, while generating visual landmark cues that guide anatomical interpretation and orientation. In conclusion, we demonstrate a promising strategy to provide guidance for A 4 CH view acquisition, which may be useful when deploying point-of-care TTE in limited resource settings.

title: Design and Simulation of Regenerative Braking Strategy for BLDC Motor with Buck Converter
authors: Veeraiah. Nagulla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Regenerative braking act as a pivotal role in enhancing the efficiency of electric vehicles (EVs) by recovering the kinetic energy otherwise lost during conventional braking and redirect it back to the battery. This paper presents a detailed simulation study of regenerative braking in a Brushless DC (BLDC) motor-driven electric vehicle using MATLAB/Simulink. The model integrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500 ~\mathrm{W}, 48 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> BLDC motor, a six-cell battery pack rated at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 0 ~ A h}$</tex>, and a voltage source inverter who is governed by hall sensor-based commutation logic. A control approach combining PI speed regulation and braking logic ensure seamless transition between motoring and regenerative modes. During normal operation, the motor speed sustained at 3000 RPM under a reference command. At braking onset, the motor operates as a generator, with the back electromotive force (EMF) exceeding the terminal voltage, thereby channel current back into the battery. The simulation results shows that battery current become negative during braking, confirming the recharging process, while the state of charge (SOC) and terminal voltage increases correspondingly. Torque reversal in the BLDC motor further validate energy recovery. This study confirms the feasibility of integrating regenerative braking in BLDC motor-driven EVs, providing more energy efficiency, extended driving range, and reduced dependency on external charging. The proposed framework also highlights the significance of efficient control logic for practical EV application.

title: Enhancing Mammogram-Based Breast Cancer Prediction From Pretrained Vision-Language Models: the Role of Soft Prompts and Bidirectional Fusion
authors: Fareeha Sarwar, Nuno Miguel De Figueiredo Garrido, Margarida Silveira
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) such as CLIP and BLIP have demonstrated strong generalization in visual reasoning tasks. However, their potential for medical image analysis, especially breast cancer prediction from mammograms, remains underexplored. This study investigates how a pretrained VLM can be adapted for full mammographic classification. Unlike prior approaches that rely on costly region-of-interest (ROI) annotations, we process entire mammograms and adapt a general-purpose VLM (EVACLIP) using soft prompts, selective fine-tuning, and bidirectional fusion strategies. We compare different fusion methods, including Concatenation, Gated-Residual, Cross-Modal, Co-Weighted and Bi-Attention. Experiments on the CBISDDSM dataset show that bidirectional fusion methods consistently outperform other fusion approaches, while providing enhanced explainability through improved attention localization. Results also demonstrate that our adapted generalpurpose VLM significantly outperforms a mammographyspecific model (Mammo-CLIP), under domain-shift, in both zero-shot and linear-probe settings. This suggests that largescale general-purpose VLMs, when properly adapted, can outperform domain-specific models, reducing the need for extensive annotation and paired image-text training.

title: A Novel 3L-SNPC Power Module for EV Motor Drives
authors: Siqi Dai, Shuangxi Zhu, Jianwei Lv, Suhang Wei, Xuchen Sun, Cai Chen, Yong Kang, Wei Jiang, Pengyang Wang, Bin Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: SNPC(Simplified Neutral Point Clamped) topology as a promising three-level converter topology, implementing such a topology using existing commercial power module inevitably leads to significant parasitic inductance, which limits its further development. So it is necessary to integrate the entire topology within a single module. In this paper, a power module for the SNPC topology is designed. By independently optimizing the commutation loops of the former stage and latter stage, an overlapped busbar structure is introduced to reduce the difference in parasitic inductance between different phases. The proposed module design is validated through simulation and experiments.

title: IntelliGuard: Policy-Contextual Rumor Detection and Sentiment Analysis for Forum Posts
authors: Yanshu Sun, Chi Xu, Danying Zhou, Aobo Wang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In the digital age, online forums have become essential platforms for public discourse, yet they are increasingly vulnerable to the spread of rumors and misinformation. While existing rumor detection methods perform well in analyzing content and propagation patterns, they often lack the ability to incorporate authoritative policy context, making it difficult to flag posts that contradict or misinterpret official policies.To address this gap, we propose IntelliGuard—an intelligent system that enhances rumor detection by leveraging user-uploaded official policy documents for contextual grounding. Our core contribution lies in a novel two-stage training framework. First, we pre-train a BERT-based model on widely used rumor detection datasets such as PHEME, Twitter15/16, and FakeNews-Net to establish foundational detection capabilities. Next, we fine-tune the model using a domain-specific policy-context dataset, employing the parameter-efficient LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) technique for targeted adaptation.We further conduct an ablation study to quantify the impact of key components, including LoRA and policy-context matching. To promote transparency and interpretability, IntelliGuard incorporates explainable AI techniques using Integrated Gradients, allowing users to understand the rationale behind predictions. Extensive experiments on a large real-world Reddit dataset, combined with a qualitative user usability study, demonstrate IntelliGuard’s superior performance, generalizability, and practical value in policy-aware rumor detection.

title:
Robust T–S Fuzzy H
                    <sub>∞</sub>
                    Control for Maximum Power Point Tracking in Uncertain Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Rabii Azdoud, Abdelmounaim Khallouq, Ali Margal, Soukaina El Daoudi, Fatima Zahra Loudifa, Abderrazzak El Abdallaoui, Asma Karama
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: This work addresses the problem of maximum power point tracking (MPPT) in photovoltaic (PV) systems operating under parametric uncertainties and environmental variations. The MPPT is formulated using Takagi–Sugeno (T– S) fuzzy modeling approach, as a reference tracking problem. A robust H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> control law is designed to guarantee closed-loop stability while attenuating the effect of modeling uncertainties and external disturbances. Stability and performance conditions are established via Lyapunov-based analysis and expressed as Linear Matrix Inequalities (LMIs). The proposed strategy’s performance is evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink simulations under varying irradiance and temperature profiles, showing reliable convergence to the maximum power point.

title: Wearable Flexible Triangular Microstrip Antenna for Sub-6 GHz 5G Applications
authors: Asma Oirgari, Ibtissam Bahlaouane, Saida Ibnyaich, Samira Chabaa, Abdelouhab Zeroual
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: In this paper, a flexible triangular patch antenna is designed, simulated, and fabricated. A textile substrate with a presumed relative permittivity of 1.6 was considered. The antenna was fabricated using denim (jean) textile as the substrate with dimensions of 30 × 34 × 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and copper tape as the conductive material, while it was designed and simulated using ANSYS HFSS, and the results were validated using CST Microwave Studio software. The textenna (textile + antenna) attained a resonance frequency of 4.30 GHz and a bandwidth from 2.74 GHz to 5.00 GHz, exhibiting superior resonance characteristics in the Sub-6 GHz range. These findings verify that the proposed antenna is highly competitive as an inexpensive, compact, and easily integrable solution for Sub-6 GHz 5G, WiMAX, and wearable applications.

title: An Enhanced Multi Complex Coefficient Filter based Control for a Grid Connected Solar Energy Conversion System
authors: Nirmal Mukundan C M, Ahmed Al Durra, Mohamed El Moursi, Tarek El Fouly
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Industrial prosumers are increasingly adopting solar energy conversion systems and integrating them with the grid to enhance reliability. However, the quality of power exchange with the grid is often compromised due to poor grid voltage conditions and distortions introduced by local nonlinear loads. This paper presents a grid integration control strategy capable of operating reliably under weak grid conditions and in the presence of nonlinear, imbalanced, and reactive local loads. The ability of a multi-complex coefficient filter (MCCF) to extract the positive-sequence voltage component is typically affected by high-order harmonics, DC offsets, frequency deviations, and amplitude or phase imbalances. To address these challenges, an enhanced MCCF structure is proposed by incorporating a second-order generalized integrator (SOGI)-based prefilter. The bandwidth of the SOGI controller is carefully tuned for optimal filtering, and the corner frequencies derived from the MCCF are fed back to the SOGI to maintain accurate grid synchronization. The use of SOGI supports the fast dynamic response of the enhanced MCCF, owing to its computational simplicity. The improved SOGI used for active load current component extraction enables the SECS to effectively meet both the reactive power and harmonic compensation requirements of local loads. The proposed single-stage system eliminates the need for a separate DC-DC converter by integrating maximum power point tracking (MPPT) and grid synchronization control within a single voltage source converter. The system performances are validated through a MATLAB/Simulink model under varying source, load, and PV-side conditions.

title: EMI Reduction and Performance Assessment in a Multiphase Buck Converter using Digital Current Control Techniques under Periodic Frequency Modulation
authors: Teja Golla, Santanu Kapat, Philip T. Krein
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A bulky EMI filter is needed in a multiphase buck converter for higher current applications, even with phase interleaving, to attenuate conducted EMI due to discontinuous input current. In this paper, periodic frequency modulation techniques are explored in a multiphase buck converter, and their impacts on steady-state parameters and transient performance are investigated under digital current mode control. A frequency-modulated digital current control architecture is proposed, and a relevant sampling and controller update process is demonstrated. A framework is developed for analyzing power spectral density (PSD) under various periodic frequency modulation techniques. Design aspects of these techniques are discussed in context. A two-phase buck converter prototype and experimental case studies are presented for nominal 1 MHz switching frequency. Periodic bifrequency modulation and quantized triangular modulation techniques are considered. Both techniques are shown to have insignificant impacts on closed-loop stability, steady-state parameters, and transient performance. However, quantized triangular modulation results in substantial EMI reduction compared to bifrequency modulation and to unmodulated cases.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced Black-winged Kite Algorithm for Cloud Workflow Scheduling
authors: Mohammad Qasim, Md. Saquib Jawed, Raza Abbas Haidri
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The growing complexity of dynamic and heterogeneous cloud environments has significantly increased the need for efficient scheduling algorithms. Metaheuristic (MH) algorithms have attracted significant attention for solving scheduling problems over the past decade. This study presents a recently developed Black-winged Kite Algorithm (BKA) along with three reinforcement learning (RL)-enhanced BKA variants: Q-learning-based BKA (BKA-Q), SARSA-based BKA (BKA-SARSA), and deep Q-network (DQN)-based BKA (BKA-DQN) to tackle the workflow scheduling problem. The performance of RL-enhanced BKA variants compares to standard BKA across the LIGO, Montage, and Epigenomics workflows in cloud environments. Experimental findings demonstrate that the BKA-DQN variant improves the makespan results compared to the standard BKA and the other two RL-enhanced BKA variants. The BKA-DQN variant achieves a superior makespan and faster convergence across all workflows. The DQN-enhanced BKA improves the makespan performance by $\mathbf{1. 5 \%}$ to $\mathbf{5. 4 \%}$ across different scientific workflows compared to the other algorithms. Statistical analysis further validates BKA-DQN’s strong potential for solving workflow scheduling problems.

title: Optimized TPS with New Symmetric Mode Boundaries for Bidirectional DAB Converters in Ultra-Wide Range SiC EV Charging Applications
authors: Mahmoodreza Eskandarpour Azizkandi, Wensong Yu, Md Tanvir Ahammed, Liming Liu, Hongrae Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel optimized triple-phase-shift (TPS) with symmetric mode boundaries for bidirectional dual-active-bridge (DAB) converters, enabling practical and efficient ultra-wide voltage range (150 V - 900 V) and high frequency (150 kHz) operation for SiC-based EV chargers. Unlike state-of-the-art TPS methods, which suffer from asymmetric mode plots, complex multi-case equations, lack of reverse power flow coverage, and impractical real-time implementation, the proposed approach defines a normalized voltage ratio and absolute normalized power to unify and cover both forward and reverse power flow under a single, super-simplified, and computationally efficient control law and reveal a symmetrical relationship between buck and boost regions, eliminating the need for separate equations. Experimental validation on a 12 kW, 150 kHz prototype demonstrates the proposed TPS strategy enables full ZVS even at very low power, and fast real-time execution—confirming its practicality for SiC EV charging applications.

title: Morphology-Embedded Graph Optimization for EM Neuron Tracing
authors: Qihua Chen, Xuejin Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reconstructing neurons from large-scale electron microscopy (EM) 3D images for connectomics is a significant challenge, particularly for neurons with complex morphologies. Current automatic reconstruction pipelines first generate oversegmented fragments, followed by tracing algorithms that merge them into more complete neuronal structures. However, these merging approaches rely on local features, failing to model long-range dependencies and global structural constraints, which often leads to biologically implausible morphology. To address this, we propose the MorphologyEmbedded Graph Optimization Network for global neuron tracing. The model employs a path-tracing-based message passing mechanism, analogous to human experts, to model long-range connectivity, overcoming the local limitations of traditional graph-cut methods. Additionally, it fuses multiscale morphological representations into the graph to model fine-grained and global biological plausibility. Experiments demonstrate this method significantly reduces reconstruction errors compared to the Multicut algorithm and also outperforms state-of-the-art graph neural networks, enhancing the biological plausibility of the results.

title: Harmonic-Fused Dual-Domain Network for Low-Concentration Magnetic Particle Imaging Reconstruction
authors: Haicheng Du, Guanghui Li, Ziwei Chen, Penghua Zhai, Yu An, Jie Tian
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reconstruction of low-concentration magnetic particle imaging (MPI) is a critical challenge for clinical translation, due to low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), severe artifacts, and blurred details in the reconstructed harmonic images. The ISBI-2026 LCR-MPI Challenge is focused on achieving high-fidelity fifth-harmonic reconstruction from low-concentration data. The network takes normalized third- and fifth-harmonic images as dual-channel input and integrates a spatially adaptive denormalization-based fusion module to leverage the structural prior of the third harmonic for guiding the restoration of noisy fifth-harmonic images. To further improve the signal-to-noise ratio, a spatial-frequency dual-domain fusion scheme based on a symmetric encoder-decoder architecture with fast Fourier convolution is adopted. Additionally, a learnable gated skip connection is incorporated to adaptively regulate network information flow and enhance feature representation capability. Experiments on the challenge dataset demonstrate that DuLCR-Net outperforms the baselines, providing an effective solution for low-concentration MPI reconstruction and advancing its clinical applicability.

title: An Interpretable Ensemble Framework Using Dimensionality Reduction and XAI for Financial Fraud Detection in Manufacturing
authors: Hui Xia, Qin Wang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to investigate the application of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) in financial fraud detection (FFD) in manufacturing industry. Financial fraud has become increasingly sophisticated and concealed, particularly prevalent in the manufacturing sector. Such corporate misconduct severely infringes the interests of stakeholders and poses a significant threat to the stability of the entire market system. In recent years, machine learning models have been increasingly applied to FFD. However, traditional models primarily emphasize predictive accuracy while overlooking interpretability, and they are becoming increasingly inadequate in identifying more complex fraudulent activities. To address these challenges, this study proposes an interpretable ensemble learning framework for FFD. The framework employs a stacked integration of multiple well-established models, including XGBoost, LightGBM, decision trees, and random forests. At the top level of the stacking architecture, a logistic regression model is utilized as the meta-learner to integrate the predictions of the base learners. Regarding dimensionality reduction, this study employs LightGBM-based feature selection to reduce information redundancy in high-dimensional data, enhancing FFD model efficiency. Furthermore, this study incorporates SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) into the model framework, endowing the predictive outcomes with transparent and interpretable characteristics.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> We compare several baseline models with the proposed method, and the results show that our model achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title: Automated Sarcopenia Risk Stratification from POCUS Using Vision Transformers and Radiomics
authors: Ricardo Teresa Ribeiro, Théo Coutaudier, Patrizia D'Amelio
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Sarcopenia is an age-related condition characterized by progressive loss of skeletal muscle mass, quality and function, leading to increased morbidity and mortality. Early detection remains challenging due to lack of scalable assessment tools. POCUS offers a portable, non-ionizing modality for bedside muscle evaluation, but currently analysis is operator dependent and time-consuming. This study proposes, an automated framework for sarcopenia risk stratification from standardized POCUS acquisitions. A Vision transformer-based segmentation model (UltraSAM) was fine-tuned to delineate three lower-limb muscles in Bmode ultrasound images acquired from 70 elderly inpatients. The model achieved 88.0% Dice and 79.6%. IoU. Segmentation masks were used to automatically estimate muscle thickness and extract reproducible radiomic features, which were combined with clinical variables. This feature set was integrated within a nested crossvalidation to predict <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S A R C-F \geq 2, S A R C-F \geq 5$</tex> and low handgrip strength. These classifiers were combined using a rule-based ensemble strategy to derive a unified Sarcopenia Risk Ultrasound (SaRUS). The ensemble achieved 75.6% balanced accuracy and 84.6% sensitivity, demonstrating strong screening capability. The proposed approach highlights the potential of integrating AI-driven segmentation, radiomics, and clinical indicators for scalable sarcopenia assessment using POCUS.

title: A Hybrid HOGI-ANN Synchronization Technique for Fast Estimation of Grid Voltage Parameters Under Distorted Grid Conditions
authors: Sapram Giridhar, Chandrasekaran S, Ashwani Chandel
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: For the control and stability of modern inverter dominated modern power systems, accurate estimation of grid voltage attributes namely frequency, phase, and amplitude is critical. This paper proposes a hybrid design that combines the strengths of a higher-order generalized integrator (HOGI) and an artificial neural network (ANN) to enhance the reliability and precision of grid voltage parameters estimation in distorted grid scenario. The HOGI structure combined with synchronous reference frame phase-locked loop serves as a robust front-end estimator that tracks the parameters of fundamental component of the grid voltage. However, residual errors in amplitude and phase estimation may still persist due to dynamic grid frequency drifts. To address this issue, a supervised ANN is integrated to learn and correct those estimation errors. The ANN is trained using simulation data representing a wide range of grid disturbances. MATLAB/Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed HOGI-ANN hybrid method significantly improves estimation accuracy. In future, the proposed technique will be experimentally validated and the results would be compared with conventional generalized integrators.

title: A Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning based Offloading and Resource Allocation for Vehicle Edge Computing
authors: Huilin Yang, Xinjie Yang, Jiazhi Yang, Ruizhe Li, Nan Ma, Pei Xiao
abstract: In the Internet of Vehicles (IoV), vehicles have the capability to offload their computational tasks to the Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) servers in order to reduce service delay. However, the majority of existent task offloading and computational resource allocation schemes are static and lack consideration of the heterogeneous nature of tasks. Furthermore, in scenarios involving both collaborative and competitive resource utilization, there remains considerable room for performance enhancement for delay-sensitive tasks. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel Global Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (GHMARL) that is an enhancement to the general MARL. In GHMARL, each vehicle and MEC server is represented by an agent, and intelligent collaboration and dynamic resource allocation are employed to balance resource usage and delay performance. In particular, GHMARL introduces a global Critic network and a local Critic network, working in a collaborative manner. The former is responsible for guiding the overall system performance to ensure the service delay performance, and the latter is responsible for MEC servers’ performance to ensure the resource usage efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that, in comparison with alternative schemes, GHMARL significantly enhances the overall system performance, particularly with regard to resource usage efficiency. Furthermore, GHMARL offers distinct advantages in balancing task delay and resource consumption under various system dynamics, making it a robust solution to address issues of resource wastage and delay violations for IoV systems.

title: Transceiver Optimization for Mainlobe Deceptive Jammer Suppression with coherent FDA Radar using Element-Pulse Coding
authors: Zhaoyi Wang, Lan Lan, Shengqi Zhu, Xiang Zhang, Guisheng Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper investigates the optimization of transceiver parameters, including the transmit waveform, frequency increment, and receive filter in a coherent frequency diverse array (FDA) radar using element-pulse coding (EPC) to suppress main lobe deceptive jammers (i.e., full pulse forwarding jammer (FPFJ) and intermittent sampling repeater jammer (ISRJ)). Specifically, the optimization problem is formulated as a generalized signal to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) maximization, incorporating the integrated sidelobe level (ISL) of target and ISRJ, and considering some practical constraints on the transmit waveform, frequency increment, and receive filter. To solve the resultant non-convex and NP-hard optimization problem, the maximum block improvement (MBI) strategy is employed by dividing the optimization problem into three subproblems with respect to (w.r.t.) transmit waveform, frequency increment, and receive filter. In particular, the subproblems are respectively solved via Riemannian manifold, majorization minimization (MM), as well as a close-formed expression, while only the variable leading to the maximum improvement of the generalized SINR is updated in each iteration. Moreover, the convergence condition is rigorously proved. Numerical results in terms of generalized SINR and filter response demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in suppressing with both FPFJ and ISRJ.

title: A Flight-to-Safety Principle for Cost-Sensitive Distributionally Robust Log-Optimal Portfolio
authors: Chung-Han Hsieh, Xiao-Rou Yu
abstract: This paper investigates how distributional ambiguity interacts with transaction costs in a log-optimal portfolio control formulation. Return ambiguity is modeled by a 1-Wasserstein ball centered at the empirical distribution, and trading frictions are incorporated via a convex, positively homogeneous transaction cost function applied at each rebalance. We establish a flight-to-safety principle: as trading frictions rise, the critical ambiguity radius—the smallest radius beyond which the optimizer retreats entirely to the risk-free asset—weakly decreases. Rolling-window experiments on S&P 500 constituents illustrate this mechanism, showing that higher transaction costs systematically shift the robust allocation toward the risk-free asset.

title: Novel Deep Learning Architectures for Soybean Kernel Defect Classification
authors: Akshay Kumar, Niteen Sapkal, Ankur Miglani, Dheeraj Kumar, Pavan Kumar Kankar
abstract: Reliable identification of soybean kernel defects is essential for automated quality inspection in grain processing and export systems. However, current approaches face challenges when dealing with subtle defect patterns and heavily imbalanced class distributions. This study introduces a soybean kernel defect classification framework built on a high-resolution dataset of 9866 red, green, and blue images spanning eight defect categories, including underrepresented classes, such as purple-mottled and stained, stinkbug damage, and tear damage. To address performance degradation in rare categories, we propose a novel unified training pipeline that combines data augmentation and class-weighted focal loss. The proposed pipeline is evaluated using three deep learning architectures: EfficientNet-B0, ResNet-50, and the hybrid convolution–attention model CoAtNet0. Experimental results demonstrate that CoAtNet0 achieves the highest performance, with 83% accuracy and a 6%–9% improvement in F1-score over convolutional neural network baselines. gradient-weighted class activation mapping analysis further confirms improved localization of defect-relevant regions in CoAtNet-0, supporting stronger recognition of minority classes. Overall, this work highlights the benefit of combining hybrid modeling, imbalance-aware training, and visual interpretability to enable practical, scalable deployment for real-world soybean kernel defect inspection.

title: Revised Carrier Transport Model Based on Depletion Region Boundary Dynamic Equilibrium for MWIR HgCdTe
authors: Fuxing Dai, Fang Wang, Xiao Fu, Ruiqi Jiang, Xiao Zhuo, Liqi Zhu, Boris Mizaikoff, Xun Li, Chun Lin, Weida Hu
abstract: Mercury cadmium telluride (HgCdTe) mid-wavelength infrared (MWIR) photodetectors exhibit complex nonlinear responses under high-power laser irradiation, challenging the classical boundary condition of low minority carrier concentration at the depletion region edge. We establish a revised carrier transport model by introducing a finite-concentration dynamic equilibrium at the boundary, governed by the balance between diffusion, field-driven drift, and recombination. Through combined electric field simulations and high-precision photocurrent measurements, we propose a semiquantitative formula based on a dynamic equilibrium process model, establishing the relationship between macroscopic current and microscopic electric field. The spatially resolved photocurrent mapping further visualizes the impact of voltage changes on the distribution of light response during dynamic equilibrium processes. This new dynamic equilibrium model contributes to fully revealing the carrier transport mode of narrow-bandgap semiconductors.

title: Distributed Fixed-Time Secondary Control for Microgrids With Communication Delays and Disturbances
authors: Xin Li, Xiaodong Wang, Mingfei Ban, Yiqi Liu
abstract: The paper addresses the distributed secondary control (DSC) problem of the islanded microgrid composed of six distributed generators (DGs) with communication delay and external disturbances. Firstly, by utilizing the Artstein model reduction method, a distributed delay-compensated strategy is proposed to restore the voltage and frequency of each DG to reference values in a fixed time. Secondly, a novel sliding mode reaching law is designed to resist disturbances and accelerate convergence, thereby ensuring the sliding surface is reached within a fixed-time. Thirdly, the proposed approach integrates an event-triggered (ET) mechanism with an integral sliding mode surface, effectively conserving communication resources while avoiding Zeno behavior. In summary, the proposed delay-compensation strategy integrated with sliding mode control (SMC) guarantees the fixed-time synchronization of voltage and frequency for DGs with coordinated power sharing control. Finally, the simulation results validate the control strategy’s effectiveness, robustness to load changes, plug-and-play capability, and indicate that the proposed ET mechanism can greatly decrease communication frequency.

title: An Adaptive Load-Shedding and Thermal Management (ALSTM) Framework for 5G AND 6G Infrastructure Resiliency in Tropical Climatic Zones
authors: J.O. Aji
abstract: The increased intensity of fifth-generation (5G) wireless infrastructure deployment, has brought about significant challenges of systems thermal management, specifically like in high ambient temperature environments which is the characteristic of tropical regions. The traditional thermal control mechanisms, which are usually reactive do not properly mitigate sustained thermal stress overtime, which leads to performance degradation, reduced reliability, and increased operational expenditure (OPEX).This report proposes an Adaptive Load-Shedding and Thermal Management (ALSTM) framework, which is designed to improve thermal resilience in climate constrained telecommunications infrastructure. The proposed approach introduces a proactive, thermal aware workload regulation mechanism; that dynamically adjusts process demand based on real time thermal conditions, effectively decoupling the workload intensity from temperature increase. Simulation results demonstrate that, the ALSTM framework maintains CPU operating temperatures within a safe threshold of 80°C - which is significantly below the critical limit of 85°C. The proposed method, achieves an estimated shift in Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of 18% to 35%, thereby reduces the peak temperature by approximately 12% and cooling energy consumption by about 20% to 30%. These improvements, translate into an an extimated overall site level energy savings of about 10% to 18%. These outcomes thereby transslates into an estimated reduction in maintenance driven capital expenditure (CAPEX) of 12% to 25% over a period of 5 years. The analysis indicates that there is a reduction in temperature difference of 30% which confirms an enhanced thermal stability and operational consistency. These results also showed a practical trade off between instant network throughput and thermal reliability, which demonstrates that a controlled workload adaptation can prevent sudden system failures while ensuring near continuous service availability. The proposed ALSTM framework provides a scalable and data driven solution for improving the reliability, energy efficiency, and sustainability of next generation telecommunications systems, particularly in emerging markets and environmentally constrained deployment scenarios.

title: Deep-Learning-Based Scattering-Enhanced Dynamic Microwave-Induced Thermoacoustic Tomography Applying Single Measurement_supp1-3678045.mp4
authors: Xiong Wang
abstract: The rapid advancement of microwave-induced thermoacoustic tomography (MITAT) technology has shown significant potential in many biomedical application disciplines. However, conventional MITAT modalities struggle to achieve an optimal balance among quantity of measurement data, sampling speed, and system cost. This renders it difficult to apply MITAT to dynamic imaging, which is a highly active frontier in biomedical imaging. This work proposes a novel MITAT modality leveraging artificially introduced wave scattering and deep learning (DL) to perform dynamic imaging based on only one measurement. We name this technique as DL-based scattering-enhanced MITAT, that is, DL-SE-MITAT. The scattering induced by many scatterers can efficiently increase the amount of received information of the sample being imaged and improve image quality. The DL technique can facilitate the imaging and enhance image quality applying scattered signals using only one measurement. We perform proof-of-concept validation of the DL-SE-MITAT method for dynamic imaging by investigating saline solution flowing in tube-shaped blood vessel phantoms. Experimental results, including both images and videos, clearly demonstrate the efficacy of the DL-SE-MITAT method in faithfully reconstructing the time-varying sample at a frame rate of 1 frames/s. It is also found that deploying more scatterers can notably enhance the quality of images. This work presents a new avenue for dynamic imaging with single measurement and contributes to the potential applications of MITAT in various dynamic imaging fields as well as other microwave-related imaging methods.

title: Core Temperature Estimation and Prediction for Batteries Using the Multi-task Coordinated Framework with Limited Data
authors: Nan Wang, Zhi Li, Zhongrui Cui, Yun Zhang, Guisen Ye
abstract: The core temperature of lithium-ion batteries substantially impacts their safety and reliability in electric vehicles and energy storage systems. However, its direct measurement remains challenging, particularly under complex operating conditions, wide temperature variations, and limited data due to sparse temperature sensing. To address these challenges, we propose a method that integrates a temperature-sequence diffusion model (TSDM) for data augmentation with a Bayesian optimization–gated recurrent unit–attention mechanism multitask coordinated framework. TSDM learns the thermal properties of batteries to realistically augment data, supplementing extreme-condition samples, while multitask coordinated framework enables efficient multitask coordination through the joint optimization of network parameters and task weights. This integrated approach accurately estimates the core temperature over a wide temperature range (from -10 °C to 55 °C) and achieves precise prediction up to 120 s ahead, while maintaining strong performance for aging batteries. Experimental results show maximum RMSE of 0.1720 °C in core temperature estimation and 0.8504 °C in 120-s ahead prediction, substantially outperforming existing methods. The proposed scheme provides crucial technical support for BMS in real-time thermal safety monitoring and long-term fault early warning.

title: Identifying Barely Visible Impact Damage in Carbon Fiber Reinforced Plastic Using a Raster-Rotational Transmit-Receive Eddy Current Probe
authors: My Nguyen, Ian Gravagne, Scott Koziol
abstract: This paper presents a system for identifying barely visible impact damage (BVID) in carbon fiber reinforced plastic (CFRP) using a raster-rotational transmit-receive (TR) eddy current probe. The system can differentiate among: 1) Barely visible impact damage (BVID), which has left a small barely visible indentation with some internal damage and 2) Barely visible no damage (BVND) which is just a small barely visible indentation with no internal damage and 3) Areas without any visible impact damage or internal damage (non-BVID). The probe runs in a raster-rotational pattern to image BVID defects over an area at different rotational angles to account for the anisotropic nature of carbon fiber. A coupon with barely visible indentations without internal damage was manufactured to test for lift-off effects. Pre-processing methods are applied to compensate for noise and uneven scan surfaces during the scanning process. Visual and thresholding techniques are then employed to classify a BVID area. After the procedure, a measurable difference among BVID areas, BVND areas, and non-BVID areas were identified. The lift-off effect from the BVND areas could be measured, and they are still distinguishable from BVID parts.

title: Integrated IMU-EMG Wearable System for Real-Time Gait Analysis and an Audio Feedback System
authors: M. Ponnibala, N. Divija, C.K. Gokulanand, R. Dharani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Gait analysis is an instrumental technique for the assessment of human walking patterns, carrying important information relative to neuromuscular and musculoskeletal functions. Conventional techniques for gait analysis are based on optical motion capture systems, force plates, and videobased methods. Although these techniques provide accurate results, they are normally highly expensive, not easily portable, and confined to the laboratory. This work proposes an integrated real-time gait monitoring system using Inertial Measurement Units and Electromyography sensors. IMU sensors can measure all kinetic parameters of gait, including acceleration, angular velocity, and orientation, while EMG sensors detect muscle activation patterns throughout a gait cycle. This will continuously detect and analyse gait parameters in real time by comparing them with standard reference values for identifying abnormalities or deviations. Once processed, the results would be instantly conveyed through audio or an alert feedback system so as to enable immediate awareness and corrective measures. Thus, the system can provide a cost-effective, portable, efficient solution for continuous gait monitoring, rehabilitation, and performance assessment for clinical and personal health applications.

title: Synergistic Foundation Models for Semi-Supervised Fetal Cardiac Ultrasound Analysis: SAM-Med2D Boundary Refinement and DINOv3 Semantic Enhancement
authors: Tonghao Zhuang, Shanglong Hu, Yongsheng Luo, Zhiqi Zhang, Yu Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We present a semi-supervised framework for joint segmentation and classification of fetal cardiac ultrasound images. Built upon the EchoCare multi-task backbone, our method integrates SAM-Med2D for boundary refinement and leverages DINOv3 to enhance pseudo-label quality. We introduce view-specific hard masking along with a two-stage optimization strategy: an EMA phase to consolidate segmentation capabilities, followed by a Classification Fine-Tuning phase that freezes segmentation parameters and resets the classification head to recover classification performance without compromising segmentation gains. Evaluated on the FETUS 2026 leaderboard, our method achieves a Dice Similarity Coefficient at 79.99%, Normalized Surface Distance at 61.62%, and F1-score at 41.20%, validating the effectiveness of our approach for prenatal congenital heart disease screening. Source code is publicly available at the following link: https://github.com/2826056177/zcst_fetus2026.

title: An Underwater Sonar Target Tracking Method Based on Improved YOLOv10
authors: Haoyu Zhao, Gang Wang, Ying Zhang, Caicui Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)
abstract: To address the identity switching problem in conventional object tracking caused by high appearance similarity among targets with distinct trajectories, this study optimizes the computation of appearance feature similarity. Within an underwater sonar target tracking framework, a lightweight YOLOv10 network integrated with a PSA attention mechanism module serves as the detector, while an enhanced DeepSonarSort algorithm functions as the tracker. Evaluated on an underwater sonar dataset and benchmarked against the original DeepSort method, the proposed approach validates the efficacy of DeepSonarSort in handling nonlinear motion dynamics. The DeepSonarSort pipeline mainly consists of three key parts: IoU matching, cascade matching, and state update and prediction based on the Unscented Kalman Filter. In terms of detection, due to the performance limitations of YOLOv8 in underwater scenarios, mainly caused by the NMS processing of redundant bounding boxes, an improved detector, YOLOv10s-BB, is introduced. This model adds the BiFormer Convolutional Attention Module into the backbone and the BiFormer attention module into the neck to enhance its ability to extract and fuse features. For tracking, DeepSonarSort adopts a nonlinear matching strategy to make it more robust against complex underwater motion patterns. The experimental results show that YOLOv10s-BB can achieve a mean average.

title: Non-linear Discrete Time Modeling of a 1 MHz Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Naveed Ishraq, Writtik Dutta, Ayan Mallik, Saikat Dey, Changkyu Bai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: For dual active bridge (DAB) converters operating at multi-MHz switching frequencies, particularly under light-load conditions, conventional reduced-order and averaging approaches often lose accuracy since they neglect dead-time effects, which occupy a significant portion of the switching cycle. The state-of-the-art discrete-time models incorporate the ZVS interval only for the primary-side tank voltage when characterizing the resonant dynamics. However, these modeling approaches are limited to basic single phase-shift (SPS) DAB operation and consider only the primary leakage inductance in the power-flow formulation, neglecting the influence of FET body capacitances and their role during dead-time transitions. To address this limitation, a discrete-time small-signal model of the T-network DAB is proposed, capturing the influence of zero-voltage switching (ZVS) intervals on converter dynamics as well as the impact of multi-phase-shift modulation strategies. This nonlinear, time-invariant state-space formulation serves as a robust foundation for controller design, facilitating accurate realization of the desired bandwidth and transient response without the need for repeated design iterations. Finally, the proposed model is validated on a 160 V to 120 V, 1 kW 1 MHz DAB hardware proof-of-concept which resulted in only 1.22dB error in the loop gain magnitude of the output voltage frequency response.

title: Vessel Motion State Recognition and Trajectory Calculation Based on Radar and AIS Fusion
authors: Yuhang Zhang, Qianqian Chen, Huafeng Kong
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: With the rapid development of China's economy, the volume of inland waterway freight is increasing and so is the number of ships. Radar and AIS, as the two most commonly used devices in the field of inland waterway maritime supervision, can provide important information for the relevant personnel to maintain the order of water traffic and ensure the safety of ship movement. In this context, a method is proposed to identify and calculate the ship motion status and trajectory based on radar and AIS. Firstly, the radar data is processed by time sliding window to establish the parameter indexes describing the position characteristics, speed characteristics and heading characteristics of the ship, which can be used to quickly distinguish the ships with different movement patterns; then different interpolation calculation models are selected to align the two types of tracks in time by the ship movement status reflected by the track points, and the position characteristics, speed characteristics and heading characteristics reflected by the two types of tracks are used to make coarse correlation judgments. Based on the generalised absolute grey correlation method, an improved correlation index system is proposed to measure the similarity of the ship motion characteristics reflected by the two types of trajectories, thus realising the correlation of trajectories; finally, the ship motion state is determined based on the two types of trajectory data within the same time period, and the corresponding Kalman filter equation is established to process the two types of trajectories, and the fusion weights are weighted according to the quality of the trajectories. The ship motion trajectory is calculated. The experimental results show that the proposed method can achieve correct correlation judgement in a dense environment, and the fused trajectories can correctly reflect the ship's motion characteristics, and the fused results are closer to the real trajectories.

title: Gender Bias in Spoken Language Translation : A Review
authors: Jamaluddin, Chandresh Kumar Maurya
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Deep neural network architectures for speech have been found to learn representations that contain information about the speaker, such as the gender. Current speech-to-speech translation technologies often employ a series of steps in cascade, comprising automatic speech recognition, machine translation, and text-to-speech synthesis. Recent studies have discovered that many speech translation systems have a gender bias. This review focuses on research conducted between 2020 and 2025 on gender bias, as speech technology has evolved significantly over the past five years. Our review considers how this bias arises, spreads and continues in systems of speech translation. The sources of bias can be found at various levels of the system, encompassing the stages of data collection, representation, and how the model learns from data, as well as evaluating the performance of the ST system. Gender bias in the datasets is reviewed. Techniques to reduce bias are outlined. While much of the existing research into gender bias has been conducted in European languages, a lot of attention is being paid to languages in which there are fewer resources; languages spoken in India are a focal point of research into gender bias studies. Our review concludes by outlining open research directions and advocating for gender-aware, culturally grounded, and ethically informed approaches to the design and evaluation of future speech translation systems.

title: CALM-VLM: Calibration and Selective Prediction in Vision-Language Models for Reliable Brain MRI Classification
authors: Nikhil J. Dhinagar, Chirag Jagad, Pavithra Senthilkumar, Sophia I. Thomopoulos, Mahir H. Khan, Sook-Lei Liew, Paul M. Thompson
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have demonstrated strong multimodal reasoning capabilities for medical image understanding. However, their confidence in predictions is often unclear, limiting adoption in clinical settings. We introduce CALM-VLM, which integrates confidence calibration and selective prediction into a generative 3D VLM. To create CALM-VLM, we fine-tuned the Med3DVLM architecture for Alzheimer's disease (AD) and stroke classification, as initial test cases. To improve reliability, we incorporated temperature scaling on the VLM's generative outputs. The calibrated model then selectively abstains from predictions when uncertain; this also improved its diagnostic accuracy. Experiments across multi-site MRI datasets show that CALM-VLM improved confidence relative to uncalibrated VLMs. Coverageadjusted test ROC-AUC increased by 5 % to 13 % for both tasks across independent test sets. The calibrated VLM achieved a test ROC-AUC of 0.951 for AD classification and 0.905 for Stroke classification. These findings highlight the importance of calibrated, uncertainty-aware VLMs for trustworthy neuroimaging AI.

title: Antecedents of Continuance Usage Intention of AIPowered Smartwatches among Generation Z Users in the Philippines: A PLS-SEM Approach
authors: Victor James C. Escolano, Yann-Mey Yee, Alexander A. Hernandez, Elizabeth T. Sta Ana, Joferson L. Bombasi, Maria Crystal E. Orozco
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly integrated into smartwatches to enhance functionality, user experience, personalization, and health monitoring capabilities. Despite the rapid expansion of the global smartwatch market, empirical research on their long-term use remains limited, especially in developing countries. This study extends the Technology Continuance Theory (TCT) by incorporating AIspecific constructs to examine the antecedents of continuance usage intention of AI-powered smartwatches among Generation $\mathbf{Z}$ users in the Philippines. Data were collected from 410 users and analyzed using partial least squares structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM). The results revealed that trust in AI is the strongest predictor of continuance usage intention, followed by satisfaction and cognitive load reduction. In contrast, attitude and perceived usefulness were found to have no significant direct effects on continuance usage intention, suggesting that among Filipino Generation $\mathbf{Z}$ users, continued use is driven more by trust-related and cognitive factors than by traditional utilitarian evaluations. These findings offer valuable theoretical and managerial implications for the sustainable growth of the AI-powered smartwatch industry.

title: Fault-Tolerant Transient Sensorless Control of Induction Motors Using Extended Kalman Filter
authors: Yacine Boudria, Raymond B. Sepe, Steven P. Bastien
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: Transient sensorless control of induction motors is an enabling technology for high-performance electromechanical systems requiring reliability, reduced hardware complexity, and robustness. By eliminating speed and position sensors, sensorless control decreases system cost and weight while improving robustness and suitability for harsh environments. This paper presents a control-oriented modeling and sensorless control framework for a rotary induction motor emulating the dynamics of a linear electromagnetic launch system. Traditional sensorless induction motor control methods are not suitable for the rapid transients and high leakage inductance seen with linear induction motors in linear launching applications.
