Our results indicate that deep learning can reliably predict participant-specific brain MRI at the voxel level, offering new opportunities for individualized prognosis.

title: A Machine Learning (ML)-Aided Design Automation Framework for Rapid Parameter Optimization in Modular Multilevel Converters
authors: Youngkeun Kim, Sooan Pack, Yeonho Jeong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel machine learning (ML)- aided design automation framework for optimizing arm inductor (L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">arm</inf>) and sub-module (SM) capacitor (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sm</inf>) parameters in modular multilevel converter (MMC). The proposed framework formulates the parameter selection problem as a constrained sequential decision-making task and employs a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based deep reinforcement learning (DRL) agent to directly search the admissible design space. A hierarchical space-partitioning strategy is first applied to construct a feasible set by enforcing resonance and voltage regulation constraints. A modified nodal analysis (MNA)-based time-domain circuit simulation environment is integrated with the DRL agent to evaluate key performance metrics, including output voltage regulation, circulating current magnitude, and arm current ripple characteristics. A multi-objective cost function is designed to balance these coupled performance indices, enabling autonomous convergence toward an optimized parameter region. The derived parameters from the developed design framework are validated through MATLAB simulations by comparison with conventional expert-designed values.

title: Renewable Energy Forecasting for Green Hydrogen Production and Photovoltaic Power Using Hybrid Models and Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Falong Cheng, Zhimin Mao, Jie Xu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The global energy landscape is swiftly transitioning to cleaner, lower-carbon power generation, driving the need for alternative energy sources. Green hydrogen (produced via water electrolysis powered by solar energy, wind energy, biomass energy, and other renewable sources) has emerged as one of the key factors in replacing the traditional energy structure, which is characterized by instability and high costs. This article presents a regional integrated energy system that employs a hybrid electric-hydrogen storage setup, combining battery and hydrogen storage. This system addresses the instability of solar power consumption and enables a combined electricity and hydrogen energy supply on the user side.The abundant solar energy resources in spring and summer are stored through this hybrid energy storage system and released when the autumn and winter light is insufficient, achieving seasonal regulation of clean energy consumption. The model focuses on economic and environmental protection research.The goal is to obtain the optimal solution through intelligent algorithms, and to demonstrate the application value and prospects of hybrid energy storage through forms such as energy cascade utilization, seasonal analysis, and key influencing factor analysis.

title: Hierarchical Clustering for False Negative Aware Prototypical Contrastive Learning in Histopathology
authors: Laetitia Rebiere, Aïna Venkatasamy, Thomas Lampert
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Digital histopathology generates large images in which annotation is difficult. Contrastive learning is an approach to learn from large amounts of unlabelled data that allows the performance of fully supervised methods to be achieved with fewer labels. However, the success of contrastive learning is largely dependent on the reliable definition of negative sample pairs. Extracting patches from histopathology datasets results in considerable intersample similarity, which increases the presence of false negative samples that can perturb selfsupervised training. In this work, we present HARP, a model for false negative aware contrastive learning. Hierarchical clustering is performed in the latent space to capture general contextual information over the data. This information is then used to properly select negative samples and limit the presence of false negative samples that can degrade the final representation. Using the publicly available Camelyon 16 dataset, we show that HARP's performance is within 1% F1score of the fully supervised baseline while requiring only 0.5% of the labels.

title: Medical Image Analysis Integrating ViT Network and Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Hangyu Cao
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The development of deep learning provides technical support for the analysis of medical image data. In order to solve the problem of data scarcity of medical images in deep learning training, this study proposes a medical image analysis method that integrates VisionTransformer and self-supervised learning. A self-supervised learning strategy that integrates hybrid pre-training is adopted to solve the problem of data scarcity, and the combination of self-supervised learning and downstream tasks is realized through the VisionTransformer training framework to strengthen the deep data mining of medical images. In the analysis of main and auxiliary tasks, the optimal Dice coefficient is determined by mixing data sets of different proportions, and the maximum Dice value of the mixed data set is 0.815. In the classification analysis of lung medical images, the model performs best under the mixed data set, with an area under the curve of 99.13%, which is better than the unfused data. It can be seen that the research technology has good application effect. This study provides technical support for medical image training and analysis.

title: Deep Learning Analysis of Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocyte, High Endothelial Venule, and Tertiary Lymphoid Structures in Breast Cancer Histopathology Whole Slide Imaging
authors: Hongxuan Tan, On Ki Tang, Chi Wai Lou, Conrad H.C. Lee, Wai Ki Wong, Ngou Men Wong, Scotty Kwok, Julia Y.S. Tsang, Ronald C.K. Chan, Gary M. Tse
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Understanding the tumor immune microenvironment (TIME) is crucial for predicting prognosis and guiding immunotherapy in breast cancer. In this work, we present a deep learning pipeline to jointly analyze tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), high endothelial venules (HEVs), and tertiary lymphoid structures (TLSs) from whole slide images (WSIs). Our method integrates multi-modal histopathology data with advanced segmentation and classification models. Specifically, we use HoverNet to segment tumor and immune cells, followed by ResNet-50-based classification to identify immune cell types and generate spatial heatmaps via Gaussian smoothing. For HEV detection, we apply nnU-Netv2 with color normalization using ICC profiles and morphological post-processing to improve accuracy. The resulting masks enable quantitative assessment of immune infiltration patterns and vascular architecture. This automated framework provides a scalable tool for comprehensive TIME analysis in digital pathology.

title: Using OPC UA PubSub for Real-time Control in a Conveyor Tracking Application
authors: David Pereira, Luis Perdigoto, Hugo Costelha, Carlos Neves
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: This paper evaluates the use of the OPC UA publisher/subscriber (PubSub) protocol in a real-time motion control case study, specifically, a Conveyor Tracking application using a GoFa10-CRB15000 collaborative robot and a linear conveyor belt. The robot’s goal is to maintain the end-effector in contact with a touch screen placed in the conveyor, maintaining a predetermined relative pose, independently of the conveyor motion. To achieve this, the conveyor’s encoder signals are sent to the robot, in real-time, using OPC UA PubSub. The communication is established through low cost Raspberry Pi 5 single board computers that serve as gateways between the devices. Tests are performed using standard Ethernet and WiFi 7, using dedicated and shared networks (i.e. with and without additional traffic in the network). The touch screen is used to sense the point of contact and measure the positioning error.Results show that using OPC UA PubSub, over standard Ethernet or WiFi 7, is a feasible solution for real-time motion tracking and control. The measured tracking errors obtained with the OPC UA PubSub communication were comparable to the reference case, where the robot uses encoder signals through direct electrical connections.

title: Carrier Sense Multiple Access Protocols in Smart Multi-domain Integrated Learning Environments: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Salsabeel Suhail Theeb, Roaa Mohammad Alabdelal, Batool Mohammad Alrashdan, Obaida M. Al-Hazaimeh, Ashraf A. Abu-Ein
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: The protocols of Carrier Sense Multiple Access (CSMA) underpin contemporary network communication. This study will examine CSMA/CD (Collision Detection) and CSMA/CA (Collision Avoidance) regarding their operational mechanisms, performance characteristics, and application domains. Alongside protocol mechanics, we present a comprehensive study of cybersecurity vulnerabilities inside both protocols and propose innovative defense solutions, including adaptive backoff checking, channel access fairness verification, and multi-layered security systems. Our efforts achieve a balance between enhancing security and optimizing efficiency, providing practical solutions for safeguarding CSMA-based networks against contemporary threats.

title: Radio Signal Detection and Labeling Method Based on Differential Statistical Features of Orthogonal Components of RF I/Q Data
authors: Yuxuan Shao, Yaosen Huang, Zhenjia Chen, Siyuan An
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: This paper proposes a radio signal detection method based on the statistical characteristics of the quadrature component difference of radio frequency (RF) I/Q data. It relies on the consistency of the quadrature component difference in two consecutive frames of RF I/Q data and sets a detection threshold based on the characteristic value of background noise to determine the presence of a signal. The RF I/Q data frames containing signals are converted into energy data, and a radio signal annotation method based on a dynamic spectrum energy threshold edge localization method is proposed. Verified by real-world data, the signal detection algorithm proposed in this paper exhibits higher accuracy than traditional energy detection algorithms in low signal-to-noise ratio environments. The signal detection rate reaches 93.6% in low SNR conditions, the signal detection time is shorter than that of the cyclic smoothing detection algorithm, and the signal annotation algorithm can better determine the frequency and quantity of signals.

title: Closed-loop Slew Rate Control of Active Current Source Gate Driver with Analog IC Design for SiC MOSFET
authors: Wen-Xin Wu, Wei-Ru Lin, Wen-Lin Ko, Guan-You Wu, Ching-Jan Chen, Giao Huu Thuc
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an Active Current Source Gate Driver (ACSD) designed for SiC MOSFETs. The driver is applied to HybridPACK Drive modules and controls the drain current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>) slew rate during turn-on and the drain-source voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>) slew rate during turn-off. The proposed ACSD employs a start-up current path in the output stage, providing an initial bias for closed-loop regulation. In the control section, the maximum slew rate is sensed using an analog approach. The peak value is captured by a peak detector, stored by double sample-and-hold and fed back into the system, enabling precise slew rate control through cycle-by-cycle iterative method. Circuit simulations are performed using Cadence virtuoso, and the IC is implemented in the TSMC T18 HVG2 process. A Boost Converter is utilized as a multi-pulse test circuit to validate the controllability and performance of the proposed gate driver.

title: Multi-Port Operation of Modular Bridge Rectifier Solid-State Transformer with Integrated Medium-Voltage Energy Storage
authors: Zhenchao Li, Giacomo Andrioli, Drazen Dujic
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Solid-state transformers (SSTs) demonstrate significant potential for hybrid power conversion systems requiring medium-voltage (MV) and low-voltage (LV) DC interfaces to a MVAC grid. The modular bridge rectifier (mBR) topology represents an emerging single-stage SST implementation, featuring just one diode and isolated DC/DC converter per submodule to achieve superior power density and modularity. This paper presents the first comprehensive exploration of mBR’s multi-port functionality for MVAC / MVDC / LVDC power conversion. The quantitative analysis of each port’s achievable power range, accounting for topological constraints and current stress limitations, is presented. A novel branch current distribution method is proposed to reduce operational voltage range of branch DC/DC converters while compensating for MVDC current disturbances. Furthermore, the islanded operation without MVAC grid is discussed, wherein the three-port mBR morphs into three parallel input-series-output-parallel (ISOP) DC/DC converters. A hierarchical control architecture capable of seamless transition between grid-connected and islanded modes is also introduced. Finally, all proposed operational modes of multi-port mBR SST are validated through a thorough simulation of a 250 kW demonstration converter connecting a 3.3 kV MVAC and a 4.67 kV MVDC grids to an 800 V LVDC load.

title: Omnidirectional Wireless Power Transfer at MHz for Capsule Endoscopy
authors: Heng Zhang, Paul D. Mitcheson, Chi-Kwan Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The advancement of capsule endoscopy toward higher-resolution imaging and multifunctional capabilities has significantly increased power consumption, rendering traditional low-frequency (kHz) wireless charging methods and limited battery capacities insufficient. We present a miniaturized 6.78 MHz wireless power transfer (WPT) system that eliminates magnetic cores and leverages compact MHz coils to drastically reduce both transmitter and receiver size. A three-dimensional orthogonal receiving coil design achieves near-zero mutual coupling, enabling independent operation without interference, with outputs combined via full-bridge rectification and filtering. A prototype, validated through modeling, simulation, and experiments under varying positions and orientations, delivers up to 1.681 W with 23.0 % peak efficiency. These results demonstrate efficient high-frequency wireless charging in a compact form factor, offering a viable solution for next-generation capsule endoscopes.

title: Quantitative Research on Safety Distance between Ships and Offshore Floating Aquaculture Platforms
authors: Zhixiu Du, Xiaozhi Liu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: To mitigate navigation safety risks near aquaculture farming platforms and standardize their offshore siting and construction, it is imperative to quantify the safe distance between ships and aquaculture structures. First, this study establishes a boundary determination model for floating aquaculture platforms based on their deployment characteristics. By analyzing the nuclear density of AIS traffic flow around such platforms, a methodology for deriving safe distances is proposed. Taking the "Mintou No.1" aquaculture platform in Lianjiang as a case study, the safe distances are calculated separately using a ship out-of-control model, emergency stopping model and AIS traffic flow model. Comparative analysis of theoretical safe distances derived from different models demonstrates that the AIS traffic flow model is most safety model. It can effectively control the navigation safety risk of ships in the vicinity of the waters of offshore aquaculture platforms.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network based Modeling of Magnetic Couplers for EV Wireless Charging System
authors: Chang Li, Junjun Deng, Zheng Yi, Mengchen Duang, Shuo Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The design of magnetic couplers is crucial to the efficiency of inductive wireless power transfer (WPT) systems in EV charging application, where coil geometries and material layouts must be engineered under tight electromagnetic and packaging constraints. Conventional finite-element modeling (FEM) provides high fidelity but is computationally expensive for iterative design, while purely data-driven surrogate models are fast but often neglect electromagnetic constraints and material interactions. This paper presents a physics-informed neural network (PINN) framework that embeds the frequency-domain quasi-static A-form of Maxwell’s equations with boundary and interface conditions, incorporates material-aware eddy-current effects, and leverages lightweight FEM-based calibration for absolute scaling. The method parameterizes DD-shaped and rectangular coils together with ferrite and aluminum shielding, trains a physics-constrained network on these inputs, and performs forward inference to directly predict inductive characteristics from geometry parameters. Validation on representative prototypes at 85 kHz shows agreement within a few percent relative to FEM and bench measurements, while reducing evaluation time from tens of minutes to a few minutes per design, thereby enabling rapid and accurate modeling of practical WPT couplers.

title: KOCOBrain: Kuramoto-Guided Graph Network for Uncovering Structure-Function Coupling in Adolescent Prenatal Drug Exposure
authors: Badhan Mazumder, Lei Wu, Sir-Lord Wiafe, Vince D. Calhoun, Dong Hye Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Exposure to psychoactive substances during pregnancy, such as cannabis, can disrupt neurodevelopment and alter largescale brain networks, yet identifying their neural signatures remains challenging. We introduced KOCOBrain: KuramotO COupled Brain Graph Network; a unified graph neural network framework that integrates structural and functional connectomes via Kuramoto-based phase dynamics and cognition-aware attention. The Kuramoto layer models neural synchronization over anatomical connections, generating phase-informed embeddings that capture structure-function coupling, while cognitive scores modulate information routing in a subject-specific manner followed by a joint objective enhancing robustness under class imbalance scenario. Applied to the ABCD cohort, KOCOBrain improved prenatal drug exposure prediction over relevant baselines and revealed interpretable structure-function patterns that reflect disrupted brain network coordination associated with early exposure.

title: A Triple-Path 12V/24V-to-1V GaN-Based Hybrid DC-DC Converter
authors: Saket Jha, Rafaella Gomes, Hanh-Phuc Le
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a new 12V/24V-to-1V non-isolated hybrid converter topology. This converter employs multiple current paths at the output, resulting in reduced current stress on each passive component. The lower current reduces I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>R conduction losses and thus enables the use of passive components with smaller footprints, improving the power density of the implementation. Experimental results from a 500kHz, 25W hardware prototype verify the converter’s operation and performance, demonstrating efficiencies of 90.25% and 90.96% at 12V-to-1V and 12V-to-1.2V conversions, respectively, and achieving a peak power density of 495 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>.

title: A Secure Retrieval-Augmented LLM Framework for Cloud-Based Incident Response
authors: Saravanan Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The response to a cloud incident is characterized by the need to make fast decisions and maintain evidentiary integrity, tenant isolation, and adherence to policies. In this paper, the authors introduce SRAG-IR, a secure retrievalaugmented LLM framework governing an incident response on the cloud that interconnects a controlled evidence lake and an attested retrieval and action layer. SRAG-IR builds a timeindexed part of incident memory over logs, alerts, tickets, and certified threat intelligence and determines only those fragments that are policy authorized through attribute-based access control and tenant-bound vector indices. A context firewall removes untrusted instructions in text accessed and enforces schema constrained prompts to minimize prompt injection and data exfiltration. Chunks of evidence are hash-chained and signed and produce verifiable cites and auditable respondent provenance. To have containment and recovery, sandboxed tool broker runs approved cloud actions as per playbook templates, producing change plans and rollback hints. The design enhances trust, privacy, and operational safety and does not need model retraining with multi-tenant production clouds. Dual retrieval integrates semantic search and asset-dependency graphs allowing root-cause hypothesis and prioritized triage suggestions should there be strict latency and cost budget limits.

title: Overhang-Constrained Bezier Strut Optimization for Body-Centered Cubic Lattice Structures in Additive Manufacturing
authors: Ali El Mechhouri, Othmane Noureddine, Younes Moubachir, Zouhair Guennoun
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Support structures in laser powder bed fusion (LPBF) increase build time, material waste, and post-processing burden, and are particularly problematic for internal lattice geometries where access for removal is limited. We present a manufacturability-driven optimization of body-centered cubic (BCC) lattice unit cells that reduces support-critical downward-facing surfaces by minimizing a differentiable, normalized overhang-violation metric subject to relative density control. Strut radii are parameterized with cubic Bézier curves using four control radii, enabling asymmetric, variable-thickness profiles while preserving periodicity. Effective elastic properties are computed via computational homogenization with periodic boundary conditions. Across target relative densities (0.20 to 0.45), cell sizes (2 and 3 mm), and overhang thresholds (40, 45 and 50 degrees), the method achieves up to a 9.5% reduction in the normalized overhang-violation metric for cell sizes of 2 mm and above while maintaining or improving homogenized stiffness.

title: DiGMa: A Dual-Domain Collaborative Framework for Efficient Temporal MPI Reconstruction
authors: Zining Liu, Gen Shi, Ziwei Chen, Jie Tian, Hui Zhang, Jingying Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Magnetic particle imaging (MPI) is a novel imaging technique offering radiation-free and fast imaging with promise for temporal imaging studies (e.g., vascular and functional imaging). However, MPI temporal reconstruction is challenged by per-frame noise and the cost of joint spacetemporal modeling. In this study, we first propose a dualdomain collaborative framework for efficient temporal MPI reconstruction, named DiGMa, to address the two main challenges. DiGMa performs signal-domain denoising via GCN-based inter-frame modeling, and image-domain enhancement via Mamba that efficiently captures long-range dependencies. The proposed method was evaluated on both simulation and measured data, demonstrating superior performance over traditional approaches. Besides, qualitative results indicate fewer artifacts and superior image quality, while the computational load of the model is also reduced. DiGMa thus achieves a favorable balance between reconstruction quality and computational efficiency for temporal MPI.

title: IMACT-CXR: An Interactive Multi-Agent Conversational Tutoring System for Chest X-Ray Interpretation
authors: Tuan-Anh Le, Anh Mai Vu, David Yang, Akash Awasthi, Hien Van Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: IMACT-CXR is an interactive multi-agent conversational tutor that helps trainees interpret chest X-rays by unifying spatial annotation, gaze analysis, knowledge retrieval, and image-grounded reasoning in a single AutoGen-based workflow [5]. The tutor simultaneously ingests learner bounding boxes, gaze samples, and free-text observations. Specialized agents evaluate localization quality, generate Socratic coaching, retrieve PubMed evidence, suggest similar cases from REFLACX, and trigger NV-Reason-CXR-3B for vision-language reasoning when mastery remains low or the learner explicitly asks. Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) maintains skill-specific mastery estimates that drive both knowledge reinforcement and case similarity retrieval. A lung-lobe segmentation module derived from a TensorFlow U-Net enables anatomically aware gaze feedback, and safety prompts prevent premature disclosure of ground-truth labels. We describe the system architecture, implementation highlights, and integration with the REFLACX dataset for real DICOM cases. IMACT-CXR demonstrates responsive tutoring flows with bounded latency, precise control over answer leakage, and extensibility toward live residency deployment. Preliminary evaluation shows improved localization and diagnostic reasoning compared to baselines.

title: Deep Diffusion Models for Subject-Specific Sar Estimation in 10.5 T Magnetic Resonance Imaging
authors: Javier Hernandez-Rubia, Candela Chinchilla-Sicilia, Blanca Rodriguez-Gonzalez, Alireza Sadeghi-Tarakameh, Yigitcan Eryaman, Sydney N. Williams, Angel Torrado-Carvajal
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultra-high-field magnetic resonance imaging (UHF-MRI) at 10.5 T enables high-resolution imaging but raises safety concerns due to increased radiofrequency (RF) energy deposition and tissue heating. We propose a diffusion-based deep learning model for fast, subject-specific specific absorption rate (SAR) estimation using individual transmit RF field maps. Trained on simulated data from virtual anatomical models, our framework leverages a 3D SwinUNet architecture within the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) paradigm. Comparative analysis of 3D and 2.5D patching strategies shows that overlapping patches significantly improve prediction accuracy and hotspot localization. The model achieves high structural fidelity and reduced error metrics, offering a viable alternative to traditional SAR simulations for real-time safety monitoring in UHF-MRI.

title: A Multistage Soft Start-up Technique for SHB-FB Type DAB Converter Considering Precharge of DC Blocking Capacitors
authors: Yanda Lyu, Ziwei Liang, Fred Wang, Hua Bai, Zhou Dong, Debanjan Chatterjee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Soft start-up in Dual-Active-Bridge (DAB) converters is a significant challenge due to the need to mitigate initial inrush currents. This challenge is even more pronounced in the Stacked Half-Bridge Full-Bridge (SHB-FB) converter incorporating an additional blocking capacitor and is a derivative of the conventional DAB converter. This paper initially assesses the start-up issues introduced by the dc blocking capacitor in the SHB-FB converter, which has received limited attention in existing literature. Then, a multistage soft start-up technique for the SHB-FB converter is proposed, considering the precharging process of the dc blocking capacitor. The technique is first validated through simulations and further experimentally verified with a Gallium Nitride (GaN)-based SHB-FB converter. The start-up challenges arising from the blocking capacitor are addressed, and experimental results show a 74% decrease in the initial inrush current compared with the hard start-up condition.

title: Effectiveness of SAM for Conjunctiva Segmentation
authors: Adapa Sunil Kumar, Kotiya Mohith Goud, Kamalaker Dadi, Raju S. Bapi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We propose an end-to-end framework for anemia detection from eye conjunctiva images, integrating a LoRA fine-tuned Segment Anything Model (SAM) for segmentation with machine learning models for hemoglobin estimation and classification. The LoRA-based SAM achieved mean IoU scores of 83.4 % on the Eye-Defy dataset and 76.1 % on the Eye Conjunctiva Segmentation Dataset, outperforming zero-shot SAM variants. Handcrafted RGB and CIE-Lab features extracted from the segmented conjunctiva enabled Random Forest regression to achieve an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.52 (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.23 ~\mathrm{g} / \text{dL})$</tex>, closely matching results using ground-truth masks. For binary anemia classification, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM achieved accuracies up to 90 %, exceeding prior literature. These results highlight the effectiveness of SAM in producing high-quality conjunctiva segmentation without manual annotation, enabling scalable and automated anemia screening. Code is available online.

title: Deep Learning-Based Accelerated Adaptive Beamforming for Enhanced Musculoskeletal Ultrasound Imaging
authors: Midhila Madhusoodanan, Mahesh Raveendranatha Panicker, Abhilash R Hareendranathan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Medical ultrasound (US) imaging typically uses delay and sum beamforming (DASB) for image reconstruction due to its computational simplicity. In DASB, the backscattered signals from the tissue are geometrically compensated for the time-of-flight (TOF) and apodized to reconstruct the final image. However, DASB images are susceptible to interference and are not optimized for any particular medical imaging use case. Various adaptive beamforming algorithms that leverage data covariance (like Minimum Variance Distortionless Response (MVDR)) have been developed to address the limitations of DASB. Despite their effectiveness, these algorithms are computationally intense and not suitable for high frame rate applications or real-time clinical use, especially for point-of-care US systems (POCUS). In this work, we propose a patchGAN-based beamforming accelerator for generating multiple plane wave (MPW) MVDR beamformed images directly from single plane wave (SPW) radio frequency (RF) data, learning both TOF correction and apodization. The model was trained and evaluated on in-vivo musculoskeletal (MSK) US datasets. The proposed model achieved a mean CR of 4.25 dB, CNR of 3.62 dB, and SNR of 6.00 dB, closely approaching MPW-MVDR (CR: 4.80 dB, CNR: 3.47 dB, SNR: 5.54 dB) while substantially improving over SPW-DASB (CR:3.25dB, CNR: 2.54 dB, SNR: 2.63 dB). Our patchGAN beamforming accelerator outperformed simpler encoder-decoder models like the U-Net by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-10 \%$</tex>. This approach generated high-quality MVDR-like US images in 0.004 s vs. 148 s for conventional MVDR, which makes it well suited for real-time applications.

title: From Client-Side to Server-Side: Rethinking Web Security in the Age of Multimodal AI
authors: Abdelrahman Elsayd, Saeed Hamouda, Ayman Mohamed, Mohamed M. Reda Ali, Salameh A. Mjlae
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The rise of multimodal artificial intelligence requires a shift from client-side to server-side data protection. In this paper, we empirically show that recent multimodal AI advances have compromised client-side obfuscation mechanisms to protect sensitive information (e.g., email addresses). We validate our approach based on 200 hybrid text-image obfuscated samples and testing it with state-of-the-art AI systems (GPT-4V and Google Gemini), for which we show that we obtained nearest 100 % extraction success rate by AI, whereas legacy scrapers that use OCR extracted only 1 % of exact emails, and 12 % of emails with spaces. This paper argues that in era of advanced AI, client-side obfuscation is no longer relevant. Hence, a paradigm shift should be implemented toward server-side protection models based on zero-exposure storage, verified proof of intent, and action-oriented disclosure. Additionally, it will serve as empirical validation for the failure of client-side defenses while providing a framework for next-generation web security architectures.

title: Cross-Domain Vision-Language Transfer Learning for Surgical Drain Detection in Real-World Postoperative Images
authors: Ashok Choudhary, Frank G. Lee, Ellen L. Larson, Cornelius A. Thiels, Hojjat Salehinejad
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate detection of surgical drains in postoperative patientsubmitted images is vital for monitoring recovery and preventing complications. We present a transfer learning approach using vision models pretrained with natural language supervision to identify drains in real-world surgical images. Multiple architectures are fine-tuned on a diverse dataset of patient-submitted photos. Results highlight model performance trade-offs and provide insights toward developing automated systems for postoperative surgical site monitoring.

title: Pathology-Centric Region Exchange: Joint Carotid Plaque Segmentation and Vulnerability Assessment in Ultrasound
authors: Xuanqi Huang, Bodong Du, Xinrui Zhou, Xiaomeng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Automated plaque segmentation and vulnerability assessment of carotid ultrasound images plays a critical role in stroke prevention and clinical risk stratification. However, developing robust models for this joint task is hindered by limited annotated dataset and sparse supervisory signals of classification task. In this paper, we propose our Pathology-Centric Region Exchange framework to address the task in CSV 2026 Challenge: Carotid Plaque Segmentation and Vulnerability Assessment in Ultrasound in ISBI 2026. Specifically, we utilize unlabeled data through a pathology-centric region exchange strategy, swapping plaque-centric patches between labeled and unlabeled samples across longitudinal and transverse views to preserve discriminative morphological cues. Subsequently, we employ a region-aligned loss to mitigate pseudo-label uncertainty in critical pathological regions and optimize joint supervision. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves segmentation accuracy and classification performance compared with existing semi-supervised baselines. As a technical report for the challenge, this paper elaborates on our methodology and experimental findings in detail. The code will be made publicly available upon acceptance.

title: Imaging-Formation-Guided Implicit Neural Representation for Multi-View Fusion in Light-Sheet Microscopy
authors: Jun Zhu, Mingzhe Wei, Yue Li, Min Guo, Huafeng Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multi-view light-sheet microscopy captures complementary information from different angles to obtain a comprehensive representation of the specimen. However, fusing these views into a single, high-fidelity reconstruction is challenging, particularly under significant noise. Traditional methods like content-weighted fusion and deconvolution struggle with noise, while supervised deep learning requires difficult-to-acquire, high-quality ground-truth data. To circumvent these limitations, we propose Guided Implicit Fusion (GIF), a self-supervised framework that represents the specimen as a continuous function using an Implicit Neural Representation. By leveraging the image formation model as a constraint, GIF directly optimizes the object representation from the low-SNR multiview data itself, obviating the need for external labels. It enables fusion, denoising, and deconvolution within a unified framework. Evaluations on simulated and experimental datasets show GIF provides high-quality, efficient multi-view fusion, outperforming both traditional and supervised methods, especially under noisy conditions.

title: Impact of Colour Temperature Variation on Thermographic Inspection of Mild Steel
authors: Suresh Kumar Bhambhu, Vanita Arora, Ravibabu Mulaveesala
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Pulse thermography (PT) is a widely used active infrared thermographic technique for non-destructive testing of metallic components due to its simplicity and rapid inspection capability. From a signal-processing perspective, PT generates transient thermal heat flux whose quality strongly depends on excitation conditions and material properties. In this work, the influence of excitation correlated colour temperature (CCT) on phase-based defect detectability in pulse thermography is experimentally investigated. Active infrared thermographic inspection is performed on a mild steel specimen containing flat bottom holes of varying depths and diameters using LED based thermal excitation with adjustable CCT. The acquired thermogram sequences are treated as time evolved thermal signals and processed using frequency domain phase (FDP) analysis to enhance robustness against non-uniform heating and emissivity variations. The effect of excitation CCT on the resulting phase contrast is quantitatively evaluated using signal to noise ratio (SNR) as the performance metric. Experimental results demonstrate that excitation CCT significantly influences the quality of the thermal signals and the effectiveness of FDP based detection, highlighting the importance of excitation signal characteristics in conjunction with signal processing techniques for reliable pulse thermographic inspection of mild steel components.

title: Self-contrastive Learning to Boost Weakly Supervised Anomaly Detection
authors: Jingyou Chen, Zhangfa Wu, Yi Hua, Hongqi Li, Yilei Shi, Hongping Gan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Weakly supervised anomaly detection methods (WADMs) can effectively utilize incomplete label data to address the issue of imbalanced samples, thereby reducing the reliance on the quantity of labeled data and demonstrating superior anomaly detection capabilities in network and service management. However, when confronted with significant label noise and missing labels, existing WADMs still struggle to adequately extract the deep feature information of samples, leading to a decline in model performance. To tackle this challenge, we propose a self-contrastive enhanced weakly supervised anomaly detection framework, called SEAD-Net, which enhances the feature representation capability of data in the model’s feature space, thereby improving the accuracy and robustness of anomaly detection. Specifically, we first design a personalized data enhancement module that augments data representation by applying various transformations to the raw data. Subsequently, a self-contrastive enhanced learning module is introduced to impose hybrid constraints on the augmented samples, constructing the overall distribution structure while learning deep sample feature spaces under complex scenario disturbances. Finally, we extract contrastive enhancement features within the deep sample feature space and perform probabilistic generation to enable effective decision-making via an anomaly probability generation module. Experimental results on a series of public benchmark datasets demonstrate that our SEAD-Net outperforms the second-best WADM by 5.95% in average AUC-ROC and 16.28% in average AUC-PR.

title: Design and Implementation of an Adaptive Control for Seamless Operation of Multiple Rooftop Solar PV Systems for Remote Zones With Local Grid
authors: Subhadip Chakraborty, Bhim Singh, B.K. Panigrahi
abstract: In remote regions, access to a stable utility grid is often unreliable, necessitating development of autonomous power solutions. Integrating multiple rooftop solar photovoltaic systems (SPVSs) with centrally coordinated battery energy storage (BES) enables cooperative power sharing and energy self-sufficiency through BES-based power levelling and backup support, forming a local sub-grid. Such integration introduces significant challenges in maintaining seamless operational continuity and ensuring high power quality during transitions between grid-connected and standalone modes. Guaranteeing voltage stability and maintaining high-quality injected currents from each SPVS are crucial to safeguard sensitive loads and minimize line losses. Moreover, compliance with stringent voltage and current quality requirements of the IEEE Std. 1547 2018 is essential for interoperability and reliable performance. To address these challenges, this research presents a multisource rooftop SPVS architecture interfaced with a BES-supported local grid that facilitates seamless mode transitions and reliable synchronization with utility grid based on its availability. Proposed control strategy ensures stable and disturbance resilient operation by providing uninterrupted power during periods of low solar generation or a complete grid outage. Effectiveness of proposed control is thoroughly validated through detailed simulation studies under extreme operating scenarios, and through real-time results. Results justify conformity with power quality standards

title: Counter-Based Scatter Matrix Estimation for One-Bit Centered Bivariate Cauchy Signals_supp1-3694498.pdf
authors: Chun-Lin Liu
abstract: Scatter matrix or covariance matrix estimation under data with outliers is crucial to signal processing applications. These data can be modeled as Cauchy or elliptically symmetric (ES) distributions, under which many estimators have been developed. However, these estimators primarily assume continuous-valued data. For implementation purposes, these data are quantized into discrete levels, which can be represented by finite bits. The complexity of the estimators is also restricted. These hardware constraints degrade the performance of existing scatter matrix estimators. This paper proposes the Cauchy onebit scatter matrix estimator (COSME). The COSME is founded on the proposed integral expressions of the marginal and joint probability for one-bit centered bivariate ES (CB-ES) data. These probabilities are simplified in closed forms for one-bit centered bivariate Cauchy (CB-Cauchy) data. For equal normalized quantization thresholds, these forms admit the COSME with lowcomplexity steps. In particular, these steps involve counting the occurrences of the events in the one-bit CB-Cauchy data and evaluating trigonometric functions. The minimum number of snapshots and the multivariate extension of the COSME are also investigated. Numerical examples demonstrate that the COSME exhibits shorter CPU time under the same number of snapshots and improved estimation performance under the same number of bits, compared to several existing estimators. Empirical results show the robustness of the COSME to Student’s t distributions and parameter variations.

title: Single Inverter Driven Segmented DWPT Transmitter Track Based on Separated-Rectangular Quadrature Pad
authors: Hai Xu, Junwei Liu, Jingyu Wang, Fei Xu, Xian Zhang, Zhicong Huang
abstract: Stable output feature and wide array distance are the contradictions of segmented dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) systems. Hybrid coupler designs, such as double-D quadrature pad (DDQP) and rectangular solenoidal pad (RSP), are effective technical routes to mitigate this contradiction based on the magnetic flux complementary. However, the magnetic flux zero-crossing polarity reversal results in the need for a specific angle driven via dual-inverters or additional detection and active control efforts, which increases circuit components, size, cost, and complexity of DPWT systems. To overcome this challenge, this paper proposed a novel single inverter driven segmented DWPT transmitter track, which integrates auxiliary separated-rectangular pads and quadrature pads (SRQPs), while the receiver side configures a simple rectangular pad. This segmented coupler exhibits axisymmetric and peak-to-valley complementary features of mutual inductances during dynamic displacement to achieve stable output. Such that this design serves to reduce circuit complexity and simplify the control method, thereby eliminating the need for active detection and control mechanisms. Additionally, a novel DWPT system with a self-decoupled transmitter design method to eliminate cross-coupling effects and a detailed parameter design methodology for further stable power output based on the proposed coupler is developed. Finally, a 300 W DWPT experimental prototype is built to experimentally verify the proposed design. The experiment results show that the output current is around 3 A with less than 4.5% fluctuation, and the efficiency can be maintained at about 89% during the receiver pad displacement process.

title:
Enhanced Polarization Switching in HfZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    MFS FeFET Using WO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Interlayer
authors: Eunjin Kim, Hyoungjin Park, Jiae Jeong, Jiyong Woo
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: We demonstrate that the memory window (MW) of HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO)-based ferroelectric field-effect transistors (FeFETs) with an InZnO channel/HZO/W gate stack can be noticeably widened to over 2 V by introducing an amorphous WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> interlayer (IL) into the gate interface. Although an orthorhombic (o-) phase is clearly observed in HZO using microscopic images and diffraction patterns, a narrow MW of 0.5 V is obtained from the FeFET. Unlike those obtained from the HZO FeFET, a large 2 remnant polarization of 19 μC/cm<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and intense diffraction peaks corresponding to the o-phase are obtained from the HZO/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> FeFET. Surface topology and elemental analyses reveal that most of the phases of HZO can be uniformly transformed into the o-phase by the amorphous WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL with a low thermal expansion coefficient of ~1 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–6</sup> K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–1</sup>. In addition, the WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL prevents unwanted defect generation at the HZO/W gate interface. Therefore, the number of dipoles in the HZO/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL FeFET participating in switching increases without charge trapping, thereby improving cycling endurance and low-temperature (–150 °C) operation.

title: A Neurosymbolic Multi-Agent System for Explainable and Traceable Decision-Making in Organizational Contexts
authors: Asimina Mertzani, Panagiotis G. Giannopoulos, Vangelis Malamas, Panos T. Chountalas, Thomas K. Dasaklis
abstract: The increasing use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Human Resource Management (HRM) raises critical challenges for transparency, explainability, and accountability. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer powerful natural-language interfaces, their tendency to hallucinate and their non-transparent reasoning limit their suitability for decision-making in HR contexts. We propose a transparent-by-design neurosymbolic decision-support system that integrates a provenance-aware knowledge graph with a modular multi-agent architecture and a constrained LLM interface. A scenario-based evaluation on a public HR dataset using a set of operational, strategic, and evidence-retrieval HR analytics queries shows that the system achieves mean fact-level performance of F1 = 0.677, mean traceability completeness of 0.680, hallucination resistance of 0.733, and mean end-to-end latency of 0.067 seconds, alongside a query-level accuracy of 0.733. The paper contributes to Information Retrieval and Generative AI research by illustrating how neurosymbolic grounding, provenance-aware retrieval, and agent-based architectures can enable trustworthy and governable information systems.

title: Robust Wearable Motion Recognition Using a Two-Stage FMG Pipeline with Rest Filtering and Placement Adaptation
authors: Ye-Jin Lee, Kyoung Tae Kim, Keonyoung Oh, Kang-Ho Lee
abstract: Wearable force myography (FMG) sensors enable continuous motion monitoring through mechanical interaction with soft tissue, but their practical deployment is challenged by rest-related signal ambiguity and placement-induced variability. This study proposes a two-stage Action Discriminator– Action Classifier (ADAC) pipeline that explicitly separates rest-dominant segments from motion-related signals and applies domain-adaptive inference to mitigate placement-induced distribution shifts. Using a lightweight two-channel air-bladder cuff sensor, the ADAC pipeline achieves stable motion recognition under realistic wearing conditions. Under unified cross-participant evaluation with pooled multi-subject data, the proposed framework demonstrates an overall accuracy of 94.9% and an F1-score of 87.9% using cuff-only sensing, without requiring subject-specific calibration. Compared with conventional FMG systems that rely on dense sensing configurations or per-user recalibration, the ADAC pipeline achieves robust performance using only two pressure channels through integrated rest-aware filtering and placement-adaptive classification. These results demonstrate the feasibility of lightweight cuff-type FMG sensors for continuous motion recognition in real-world, multi-user scenarios and underscore the importance of system-level design in pressure-based wearable sensing.

title: MDV: Resolving the Auxiliary Data Dilemma in Model Extraction Defenses
authors: Chuang Liang, Jie Huang, Shuaishuai Zhang, Zeping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Current studies have discovered that model extraction attacks (MEA) can steal the functionality of deep learning (DL) models, thus causing economic loss and other security threats. Extraction attackers can build a clone model locally that has a different structure but similar functionality to the victim model. To counter MEA, defenders utilize realistic auxiliary data to enhance the victim model and produce misleading predictions for attack data. However, these defense methods have three critical problems caused by utilizing realistic auxiliary data. First, in some scenarios, realistic auxiliary data is absent and difficult to obtain. Secondly, the defense effectiveness brought by realistic auxiliary data is unstable. Finally, the realistic auxiliary data did not protect all categories of training data, resulting in higher clone accuracy for some categories. To address these issues, we propose Model Defense Variational Autoencoder (MDV) to generate virtual auxiliary data as a replacement for realistic auxiliary data. MDV combines the Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and classifier, compelling the latent features to obey different multivariate Gaussian distributions according to the categories. Then, MDV samples deep features from low-likelihood regions of different distributions as realistic auxiliary data. During the experimental phase, we apply our auxiliary data to different defense methods that use auxiliary data and compare the defense effects in different scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively addressed the three aforementioned issues.

title: Discrete Diffusion-Based Sampling for Massive MIMO Detection
authors: Lanxin He, Zheng Wang, Zhen Gao, Shaoshi Yang, Yongming Huang, Dusit Niyato
abstract: In this paper, we study a sampling-based detection strategy for massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems, driven by a modified discrete diffusion model formulated as an analytical, non-learning sampling process. Built upon this framework, the proposed discrete diffusion-based sampling (DDS) algorithm improves decoding performance by leveraging residual-dependent sampling, compared to the independent randomized successive interference cancellation (SIC). Specifically, the modified diffusion model incorporates a shortcut perturbation toward the SIC solution, a forward diffusion step to enhance diversity, and step-wise alignment with the perturbed received signal. Within this framework, the DDS algorithm further adopts one-dimensional discrete Gaussian distribution, involving a reformulated discrete Gaussian noise and an explicitly characterized sampling range, but retains computational complexity amenable to practical deployment. Moreover, we theoretically demonstrate an improved expected decoding radius over randomized SIC. Finally, simulation results based on massive MIMO detection are presented to confirm performance gain of the proposed DDS algorithm.

title: A Real-Time Speech-to-Sign Language Translation Framework Using Transformer-Based Gesture Synthesis
authors: Hemantha Kumar B N, Vijay Kumar S, Suchithra H N, Sachitha M S, Shivaprasad B M, Prashanth R Kaigaddi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Effective communication between hearing individuals and the deaf community continues to be a great challenge because of the linguistic complexity and the visual-spatial element of sign languages. This paper introduces a real-time speech-to-sign language translation framework based on Transformer-based gesture synthesis for natural, expressive and interactive communication. The proposed system includes the Conformer Automatic Speech Recognition module for converting speech to text accurately, and then the Transformer linguistic reordering model to generate sign gloss sequences in accord to the sign language grammar. An editable, intermediate, and human readable representation in a format of editable interview with elements that allow a form of human-in-the-loop correction to allow users to “refine” hand shapes, facial expressions, timing and emphasis. For the task of generating gestures, a Transformer - Mixture Density Network is used to generate smooth and probabilistic joint-level movement trajectories in which both manual and non-manual sign components are taken into consideration. The system utilizes motion statistics based upon motion data from the How2Sign dataset to increase the expressiveness and realism of the system. Experimental evaluation shows the overall accuracy is 92.8% with low end-to-end latency of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 3 m s}$</tex> guaranteeing real-time performance. User studies have further shown that there is an improvement in comprehension along with an increase in trust and decreased cognitive load. The framework as proposed is a comprehensible and scalable way of providing a practical solution for inclusive speech-to-sign language communication.

title: Comparative Analysis of EMI in Full-Bridge Inverters with Si and SiC Power Devices
authors: Sarvvesh R, Dinakar R, Saniya Ahamad, Sai Charan K, Soniya Agrawal, Prema V
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The versatility and simplicity of the full-bridge inverter make it essential for modern systems like electric vehicles and solar inverters. Silicon (Si) devices remain prevalent due to their cost-effectiveness, reliability, and mature manufacturing. Conversely, silicon carbide (SiC) devices offer higher thermal performance, greater breakdown voltage, and enable higher switching frequencies, reducing losses and system size. However, the fast switching of SiC devices introduces increased electromagnetic interference (EMI) and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) concerns due to higher dv/dt rates. This paper presents a comparative EMI analysis of a full-bridge inverter using Si and SiC power devices. The study further focuses on the separation and evaluation of common-mode (CM) and differential-mode (DM) EMI components to better understand their individual contributions to total conducted noise. LTSpice, an open-source circuit simulation software, is utilized to model and analyze EMI behavior at a switching frequency of 50 kHz.

title: PulmoNet: A Deep Learning Framework for Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Ray Images with Feature Analysis
authors: Amjad Albashayreh, Maria Yousef, Loai Al-Alnemer, Alhareth Abdelmuhdi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a major global health challenge, underscoring the need for accurate and automated diagnostic solutions. Previous studies have widely applied transfer learning–based deep learning models for pneumonia detection; however, many rely on a limited range of architectures and provide insufficient analysis of model interpretability and decision-making behavior. To address these limitations, this paper proposes PulmoNet, a comprehensive deep learning framework for pneumonia detection that systematically evaluates multiple high-capacity architectures while integrating feature-level explainability analysis. The proposed framework encompasses data collection, preprocessing, feature engineering, model training, performance evaluation, and explainable feature attribution. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that among the evaluated architectures, ResNet152 achieved the best performance, attaining an accuracy of 94.39% and an F1-score of $94.31 \%$, with a detection time of 13.31 seconds. Beyond classification accuracy, the framework incorporates explainability techniques to enhance transparency and clinical interpretability. These findings suggest that PulmoNet provides a structured and interpretable evaluation pipeline that can support reliable and efficient pneumonia diagnosis in clinical settings.

title: Realizing High-Q Quasi-BIC Through Monolithic Dimer Metasurface in SOI Platform
authors: Abdullah Bin Shams, Shadman Shahriar Sharar, J Stewart Aitchison
abstract: We introduce a novel monolithic dimer meta-atom design that exhibits a coupled resonant behavior characteristic of a conventional dimer. Such metasurface, relative to dimers, demonstrate higher Q-factor, lower fabrication error and less optimization parameters. These attributes are ideal to achieve an experimental high-Q Quasi-Bound-States-in-Continuum.

title: Positional Segmentor-Guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning for Spatially Localized Image Synthesis
authors: Tian Xia, Matthew Sinclair, Andreas Schuh, Fabio De Sousa Ribeiro, Raghav Mehta, Rajat Rasal, Esther Puyol-Antón, Samuel Gerber, Kersten Petersen, Michiel Schaap, Ben Glocker
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Counterfactual image generation enables controlled data augmentation, bias mitigation, and disease modeling. However, existing methods guided by external classifiers or regressors are limited to subject-level factors (e.g., age) and fail to produce localized structural changes, often resulting in global artifacts. Pixel-level guidance using segmentation masks has been explored but requires user-defined counterfactual masks, which is tedious and impractical. Segmentor-guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning (Seg-CFT) addressed this by using segmentation-derived measurements to supervise structurespecific variables, yet it remains restricted to global interventions. We propose Positional Seg-CFT, which subdivides each structure into regional segments and derives independent measurements per region, enabling spatially localized and anatomically coherent counterfactuals. Experiments on coronary CT angiography (CCTA) show that Pos-Seg-CFT generates realistic, region-specific modifications, providing finer spatial control for modeling disease progression.

title: Ranking Xai Methods for Head and Neck Cancer Outcome Prediction
authors: Baoqiang Ma, Djennifer K. Madzia-Madzou, Rosa C.J. Kraaijveld, Jin Ouyang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: For head and neck cancer (HNC) patients, prognostic outcome prediction can support personalized treatment strategy selection. Improving prediction performance of HNC outcomes has been extensively explored by using advanced artificial intelligence (AI) techniques on PET/CT data. However, the interpretability of AI remains a critical obstacle for its clinical adoption. Unlike previous HNC studies that empirically selected explainable AI (XAI) techniques, we are the first to comprehensively evaluate and rank <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> XAI methods across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex> metrics, covering faithfulness, robustness, complexity and plausibility. Experimental results on the multi-center HECKTOR challenge dataset show large variations across evaluation aspects among different XAI methods, with Integrated Gradients (IG) and DeepLIFT (DL) consistently obtained high rankings for faithfulness, complexity and plausibility. This work highlights the importance of comprehensive XAI method evaluation and can be extended to other medical imaging tasks.

title: Geometric, Photometric, and Semantic Multi-Layer 3D Mapping Towards a Forest Digital Twin
authors: Dinis S. Simões, Arush Mendon, M. Eduarda Andrada, Afonso E. Carvalho, Mário P. Cristóvão, Tobias Marsh-Hulland, Duarte Cruz, André M. Silva, Luís M. Gaspar, Paulo Peixoto, David Portugal, Carlos Viegas, Domingos X. Viegas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: Accurate, rich 3D forest representations are essential for perception, analysis, scene understanding and decision-making in woodland and off-road rugged terrain scenarios. This paper presents an approach for multi-layer 3D mapping for forest environments, integrating geometric, photometric, and semantic representations towards unified spatial models.The geometric layer is constructed using a state-of-the-art factor graph–based SLAM formulation with global georeferencing, enabling consistent large-scale mapping. The photometric layer is obtained through precise multi-sensor registration between LiDAR and RGB imagery, allowing the projection of color information onto the 3D structure. For the semantic layer, a transformer-based deep learning model is employed to perform dense semantic segmentation, enriching the map with comprehensive classification of entities, terrain, and structures.Experimental results in a real-world wildland-urban interface environment highlight the capabilities and limitations of each layer, as well as their combined suitability for off-road rural scene representation. The proposed mapping methods constitute a key step towards interpretable digital twins for forest management.

title: A Multimodal Slice Discovery Framework for Systematic Failure Detection and Explanation in Medical Image Classification
authors: Yixuan Liu, Kanwal K. Bhatia, Ahmed E. Fetit
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Despite advances in machine learning-based medical image classifiers, the safety and reliability of these systems remain major concerns in practical settings. Existing auditing approaches mainly rely on unimodal features or metadata-based subgroup analyses, which are limited in interpretability and often fail to capture hidden systematic failures. To address these limitations, we introduce the first automated auditing framework that extends slice discovery methods to multimodal representations specifically for medical applications. Comprehensive experiments were conducted under common failure scenarios using the MIMIC-CXR-JPG dataset, demonstrating the framework's strong capability in both failure discovery and explanation generation. Our results also show that multimodal information generally allows more comprehensive and effective auditing of classifiers, while unimodal variants beyond image-only inputs exhibit strong potential in scenarios where resources are constrained.

title: Immersive 3D Virtual Laboratory with an AI-Powered Pedagogical Avatar for Remote Practical Learning
authors: Abdelali El Gourari, Mustapha Ezzini, Mohammed Ennejjar, Mustapha Raoufi, Mohammed Skouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Remote practical education faces significant challenges in replicating hands-on laboratory experiences. This paper presents a novel immersive 3D virtual laboratory integrated with an AI-powered pedagogical avatar for electronics education. The system combines a web-based architecture (Next.js) with an interactive Three.js environment featuring realistic component manipulation on a virtual breadboard. A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) avatar provides contextual assistance using a specialized electrical engineering knowledge base. Preliminary results demonstrate significant improvements in student engagement and comprehension compared to traditional remote learning approaches.

title: SFENet: Shallow Feature Excitation for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Lei Zhang, Zihuang Wu, Xinyu Xiong, Ming Li, Guanbin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is crucial for ophthalmic diagnosis, yet deploying deep learning models on mobile devices remains challenging due to high computational complexity. We propose a lightweight framework, SFENet, that achieves accurate segmentation with minimal resource requirements. Unlike traditional U-Net-based models that rely on deep, computationally intensive encoders, SFENet employs a streamlined single-branch architecture and emphasizes shallow features. Its core innovations include three modules: a hierarchical receptive field module (HRFM) for efficient multi-scale feature extraction, a feature refinement module (FRM) for enhancing vessel detail through dual-branch fusion, and a spatial context mining module (SCMM) for modeling long-range dependencies to ensure vessel continuity. This design significantly reduces model parameters and inference time while maintaining high accuracy, making SFENet suitable for real-time applications on edge devices. Experiments on multiple public datasets confirm that SFENet outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Learnable Total Variation with Lambda Mapping for Low-Dose CT Denoising
authors: Yusuf Talha Başak, Mehmet Ozan Unal, Metin Ertas, Isa Yildirim
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: While Total Variation (TV) excels in noise reduction and edge preservation, its reliance on a scalar regularization parameter limits adaptivity. In this study, we present a Learnable Total Variation (LTV) framework coupling an unrolled TV solver with a LambdaNet that predicts a per-pixel regularization map. The proposed framework is trained end-to-end to optimize reconstruction and regularization jointly, yielding spatially adaptive smoothing. Experiments on the DeepLesion dataset, using realistic LoDoPaB-CT simulation, show consistent gains over classical TV and FBP+U-Net, achieving up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 3. 7} \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> PSNR and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 \%}$</tex> relative SSIM improvement. LTV provides an interpretable alternative to black-box CNNs for low-dose CT denoising.

title: An Ultra Compact RGB LED Driver with Only One Off-Chip Component, 97.25% Peak Efficiency, and Nearly Full-Range Color Gamut Coverage
authors: Wangkang Lan, Caiyu Tong, Yuan Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The proliferation of intelligent electric vehicles imposes stringent demands on energy efficiency, integration, and reliability for on-board LED drivers, especially in RGB multi-color applications (e.g., automotive ambient lighting) requiring precise color mixing. Conventional LDO or DC-DC buck-boost solutions cannot simultaneously achieve high efficiency and high integration. This paper presents a highly integrated 3-switch RGB LED driver that requires only one off-chip inductor. Its topology merges DC-DC converter switches with RGB channel control switches, reducing switching losses and enhancing integration. The proposed design ensures robust operation against input voltage variation. An integration-comparison control scheme enables stable regulation of RGB color ratios. The driver maintains high efficiency across a wide color gamut and offers an efficiency–color gamut trade-off, enabling expanded gamut coverage at an acceptable efficiency cost. Fabricated in a 180nm CMOS process, the prototype IC achieves a peak efficiency of 97.25% under 5V input with mixed-color light output, demonstrating its potential for energy-efficient, space-constrained automotive LED applications.

title: An IoT-Enabled Deterministic Computational Model for Real-Time Water Quality Assessment of the River Ganges
authors: Yugansh Sachdeva, Achintya Singhal, Manoj Kumar Singh, Vimal Dungdung
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The River Ganges is under enormous pressure due to rapid urban expansion, industrial effluent discharge, agricultural runoff, and increasing hydrological variability. Conventional laboratory-based water-quality monitoring, while analytically sound, suffers from limited temporal resolution and is often unable to record short-term and rapidly evolving changes in water quality. This paper presents an Internet of Things based deterministic (rule-based and reproducible) computational framework for water-quality assessment of the River Ganges. The system integrates in-situ sensing with a computational pipeline aligned with the Central Pollution Control Board guidelines. The pipeline performs parameter transformation, sub-index generation (parameter-wise quality scoring), and weighted aggregation to compute the water quality index. The primary contribution of this work is the development of a transparent, interpretable, and operationally viable framework intended to serve as a foundation for future large-scale deployments. The framework was validated under controlled laboratory conditions across three water-quality scenarios, confirming correct Central Pollution Control Boardaligned Water Quality Index computation across full classification range. Field deployment along the River Ganges is currently in progress. The developed framework provides a structured basis for continuous water-quality surveillance and offers a scalable approach to the development of digitally enabled river management practices.

title: Fault Classification in Power Transmission Lines Using Entropy-Aided Temporal Windowing and Hybrid Distance Measures
authors: Nirmalya Mallick, Chandan Kumar Shiva, Vedik Basetti, Sachidananda Sen, V. Mukherjee, Shriram S. Rangarajan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The present power system requires a fault detection system which can identify faults with both speed and accuracy to maintain system stability while avoiding equipment damage. The research studies a fault classification method which uses entropy to classify faults through its application of optimized wavelet transforms and hybrid distance measurement techniques. The method uses temporal windowing to extract important fault data through its implementation. The adaptive wavelet selection tool delivers its most suitable frequency output. The development of hybrid distance classification framework enables automatic parameter optimization to function as its matching component. The test-bed achieved exceptional results with 95% total accuracy and perfect area-under-curve results and F1-scores which ranged from 0.80 to 1.00.

title: Intelligent Correlation of Indicators of Compromise Using AI for Proactive Threat Detection
authors: Orieb Abualghanam, Hadeel Alazzam, Malik Al-Essa, Wesam Almobaideen, Mohammad Qatawneh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The increasing complexity of cyber threats, characterized by sophisticated multi-stage attacks, exposes a fundamental weakness that conventional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems are suffering from in term of their reliance on reactive, and isolated alerts. This limitation increases false positives alerts, leads to operational fatigue for analysts, and delays in identifying genuine threats. Proactive defense in SIEM can benefit from the integration of external Threat Intelligence (TI) in providing essential context. Indicator of Compromise (IoC) repositories can be used to support this via enabling the correlation of internal security events with data related to global threats. In this paper, a systematic methodology for integrating multi-source Threat Intelligence into SIEM platforms is proposed. The methodology implementation is done via a custom IoC repository within the Splunk SIEM environment, designed to facilitate the ingestion, normalization, and contextual correlation of external threat data. Additionally, correlation rules are developed using an autoencoder-based technique to synthesize this external intelligence with internal telemetry from diverse log sources and to identify complex attack patterns. An empirical evaluation of the proposed system is conducted using three distinct log sources: Windows Event Logs, Firewall Logs, and Web Server Logs. Results indicate a significant improvement in detection accuracy across all evaluated datasets when compared to traditional, intelligence-agnostic correlation. These results demonstrate that the strategic integration of TI substantially reduces alert noise and enhances the detection of novel, multi- vector attacks, thereby advancing SIEM functionality from basic log aggregation to contextual, proactive threat intelligence.

title: Automated CRYPT Architecture Categorization Using Advanced Endoscopy
authors: Ujwala Bhangale Chaudhari, Bisi Bode Kolawole, Cecilia Pugliano, Irene Zammarchi, Giovanni Santacroce, Rocio Del Amor, Pablo Meseguer, Valery Naranjo, Subrata Ghosh, Marietta Iacucci, Enrico Grisan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Alteration of mucosal structure of the colon is one of the major hallmarks of disease activity in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) such as Ulcerative Colitis (UC) and Crohn Diseases (CD). Ultra-magnification Endocytoscopy (ECS) enables real-time, in vivo assessment of mucosal microstructure, but its clinical application remains limited due to the complexity of image interpretation. This work focuses on the assessment of the crypt architecture of the colon mucosa using Endocytoscopy data leveraging recent foundational models in endoscopy, we classify ECS frames as showing normal crypt architecture or necrotic mucosa, domain aware pretrained ResNet-50 model with GastroNet weights is used to extract the features; using 5-fold cross-validation, we achieve a frame level classification accuracy of 79 % and when aggregating the frame scores to obtain a video-level classification, the overall accuracy obtained is 89 % with 83 % sensitivity, 90 % specificity and 91% AUC.

title: Image Quality Assessment of Imputation-Induced Biomarker Distortions in Multiplex Immunofluorescence
authors: Priyanka Rana, Xingnan Li, Tuba N. Gide, Nurudeen A. Adegoke, Yizhe Mao, James S. Wilmott, Sidong Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Stain imputation in multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) aims to reconstruct missing biomarkers but can inadvertently distort inter-marker dependencies essential for biological interpretation, leading to misleading downstream analyses. Conventional metrics such as Structural Similarity Index Measure or Mean Absolute Error quantify pixel-level fidelity but do not ensure the preservation of biologically meaningful relationships. We present a biologically informed image quality assessment (IQA) framework that assesses imputation-induced distortions in inter-marker relationships. Images with imputed CD8 generated by OS2CR-Diff and Stain Imputation in Multiplex Immunofluorescence Imaging (SIMIF) are labelled according to changes in CD8-CD16 colocalisation (inflated, deflated, or preserved states), and a deep learning model is trained to identify these states directly from the imputed images, enabling quality control of stain-imputation outputs. To address class imbalance in the curated dataset, we introduce a Correlation-Aware Augmentation (CAA) approach that perturbs CD8 spatial distributions relative to CD16, simulating imputation-relevant inflation and deflation patterns. CAA consistently improved classification performance (AUC and macro-F1) across both datasets, outperforming conventional augmentation approaches.

title: RTFGM-Net: A Deep Learning Model for Auto-Detection of Hypsarrhythmia EEG in West Syndrome
authors: Chenghao Xue, Yifei Bai, Yuanning Li, Dake He, Lin Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
