title: Beyond Bias Detection: Community Auditors and Normative Reasoning in AI Oversight CSCW006
authors: Corey Jackson, Tallal Ahmad, Shelcia David Raj, Natalie Wu
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction
abstract: None

title: Role-based Adaptive Coordination for Resilient Signal Coverage in Stratospheric Balloons_supp1-3694500.pdf
authors: Zhenglin Liang
abstract: Superpressure balloons in the stratosphere are emerging as a promising platform for extending network connectivity to remote and disaster-affected regions. Yet, their dependence on altitude control and absence of horizontal maneuverability render them highly susceptible to wind drift, leading to unstable coverage. Coordinating multiple balloons further challenges current multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) methods, as wind-induced moderation effects can simultaneously disrupt all agents. Here we present a novel Adaptive Role-Switching Cooperative Learning (ARSCL), a dual-policy MARL framework that achieves resilient, cooperative coverage through adaptive role specialization. ARSCL assigns complementary roles —a main policy maintaining primary coverage and a compensatory policy introducing diversity to counteract correlated drift—linked by a dynamic role-switching mechanism that adapts to spatial deviation. A drift-prevention module further constrains learning to feasible trajectories. Simulations demonstrate that ARSCL achieves an average coverage duration exceeding 94% within a 25 km radius over a 24-hour period, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art MARL baselines in both efficiency and resilience.

title: Research on High-Speed Interrogation Method for Optical Fiber Fabry–Perot Sensors Based on Photonic Time-Stretch
authors: Yuru Chen, Miao Hu, Liang Jin, Jie Luo, Meihua Bi, Mengmeng Xu, Kairen Xu, Lijun Cheng
abstract: Optical fiber Fabry-Perot (FP) sensors are widely used for measuring parameters such as strain and temperature. However, they still face limitations in measurement rates when dealing with high-frequency parameters like vibration and explosive shock. The time-stretch-based demodulation method for FP sensors, for the first time, integrates FP sensing technology with photonic time-stretch technology, mapping the FP interference signals in the frequency domain to the time domain. This approach combines the advantages of high-speed time-domain acquisition and high-precision frequency-domain interference demodulation, representing a promising development trend

title: Traffic Flow Forecasting Based on Complex Gray Relational Degree Network and Multi-Period Exattention-GRU Model
authors: Zhuoxuan Li, Jinde Cao, Yifan Chen, Mahmoud Abdel-Aty, Leszek Rutkowski
abstract: Traffic flow forecasting is vital but challenging in traffic management. This study proposes a complex gray relational degree-based network to cluster traffic flow observation nodes using the Louvain algorithm. The method effectively distinguishes the nodes, providing accurate clustering for equivalent training sets and traffic flow prediction. To capture the characteristics of time series data in traffic flow data, the gated recurring unit (GRU) is the backbone network. For improved accuracy, a multi-period Exattention-GRU model is developed. GRU enhances network performance as the main component with an external attention mechanism and a residual structure. Stacking and fusing the improved GRU network captures various traffic information, which contributes to improving accuracy. The model, trained on an equivalent set using the RAdam algorithm, demonstrates effectiveness with favorable prediction results across various datasets and traffic conditions. Taking into account 36 data sets, the average metrics for RMSE, MAPE and MAE are recorded as 60.6363, 7.40%, and 41.4897.

title: A Machine Learning-Assisted High-Precision Fiber Optic FPI Pressure Sensor
authors: Jiaxin Liu, Yizhou Jiao, Yuxiang Zhao, Jing Yang, Peng Ye, Xinjie Li, Jinhui Shi, Chunying Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: A high-precision optic fiber Fabry-Perot interferometer (FPI) pressure sensor is proposed and experimentally demonstrated. The Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) model is employed to demodulate the pressure signal and suppress the temperature crosstalk. The sensing FP cavity is designed as a bowl shape and incorporates the Vernier effect to amplify the differences in the reflection spectra of the pressure sensor under different gas pressures, which can improve the prediction accuracy performance of the GPR model. Comparing with the traditional wavelength demodulation method, the root mean square error (RMSE) of the pressure measurement can be reduced from 21.64 kPa to 0.77 kPa (a 96.4% reduction) in the temperature range of 25 – 75 °C by using GPR model. Due to the high precision and resistant to temperature interference, this sensor has great potential for application in high-precision air pressure measurement.

title: Bridging Domain Gap and Preserving Privacy for Battery Diagnosis in Data Centers
authors: Yitong Shang, Thomas Tongxin Li, Peijin Li, Junqing Tang, Man-Fai Leung, Duo Li
abstract: Data centers, the cornerstone of the digital economy, depend on Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) systems, where battery health is critical for operational continuity. However, the practical application of data-driven anomaly detection is severely hampered by two interconnected challenges: distribution shifts between different battery batches degrade model generalization, while data silos arising from commercial and privacy constraints prevent cross-institutional data aggregation. To overcome these barriers, we systematically formulate this industrial challenge as a Federated Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (FUDA) task, which enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data. The core contribution of this paper is the proposal of a novel scenario-driven adaptation strategy, which posits that no single algorithm is universally optimal. We demonstrate that for homogeneous scenarios with small domain gaps, a lightweight discrepancy-based method (CORAL) paired with a powerful feature extractor (Transformer) is most effective. Conversely, for heterogeneous scenarios with large domain gaps, a stable, decoupled adversarial approach (ADDA) combined with a capacity-constrained feature extractor (DeeperMLP) is superior as it ensures convergence and promotes generalization. Experiments on a real-world data center dataset validate our FUDA approach (F1-score of 0.872) not only significantly outperforms local non-adapted models but also achieves performance highly comparable to, and in some cases exceeding, that of privacy-violating centralized models (0.908).

title: Efficient Nonlocal Tensor Function Representation for 3D Seismic Random Noise Attenuation
authors: Shengrui Wang, Yisi Luo, Bangyu Wu, Jiangjun Peng, Yueming Ye, Junxiong Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-dimensional 3D seismic data is inevitably contaminated by random noise due to environmental interference and acquisition system limitations. While many deep learning methods have been proposed for random noise attenuation, they often suffer from various limitations. For instance, some convolutional neural network (CNN)-based methods tend to be computationally inefficient when processing 3D seismic volumes, while implicit neural representation (INR)-based methods frequently fail to recover complex structural details owing to the spectral bias. To address these challenges, this study introduces an efficient nonlocal tensor function representation (NLTFR) for unsupervised 3D seismic denoising. Specifically, we cluster similar cubes within 3D seismic data into nonlocal similar groups. To efficiently and effectively exploit the nonlocal self-similarity inherent in seismic data, we employ tensor function factorization parameterized by INRs to model these clustered nonlocal similar groups. Furthermore, we propose a partially shared transfer learning strategy for NLTFR to accelerate model convergence when applied to large-scale seismic datasets. Multiple-frequency sinusoidal functions are incorporated into the INRs to mitigate spectral bias, thereby enhancing the model’s representation ability. Additionally, a weighted total variation and a second-order 3D total variation are introduced to improve denoising performance and robustness of the NLTFR model. Comprehensive experiments conducted on synthetic and field 3D seismic datasets demonstrate that the proposed NLTFR achieves a superior balance between efficiency and effectiveness compared with conventional deep learning methods. Overall, our method attains an average performance gain of 1.5dB while significantly reducing execution time relative to traditional deep learning methods for 3D seismic volumes, underscoring its practical efficiency.

title: Learning-Based Airflow Templates for Real-Time Hand–Wind Interaction in Web-Based Cloth Simulation
authors: Jong-Hyun Kim
abstract: This paper presents a learning-based hand–wind interaction framework that enables real-time airflow generation and cloth simulation in a web-based environment using only a standard webcam. Conventional gesture-driven wind interfaces typically map hand motion to a single force vector, which limits their ability to represent the spatial structure and temporal continuity of airflow induced by natural hand gestures. As a result, abrupt wind transitions and visually implausible cloth responses often occur, especially during gesture changes. To address these limitations, we propose a data-driven airflow representation based on learned airflow templates. Instead of directly solving physically accurate fluid dynamics, our approach focuses on visual plausibility by learning representative airflow patterns from paired hand-motion and material-response data. We construct a dedicated dataset by simultaneously recording hand gestures and airflow-induced paper motion using a dual-camera setup. The resulting airflow vector fields are clustered via unsupervised learning to extract a small set of representative airflow templates. At runtime, a temporal gesture classification model based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks analyzes recent hand-motion sequences and predicts a probability distribution over the airflow templates. The inferred airflow is obtained through probability-weighted template blending, which ensures smooth transitions between different wind patterns. This airflow field is then injected as an external force into a lightweight fluid solver to generate divergence-free velocity fields, which are subsequently coupled with a Position-Based Dynamics (PBD) cloth simulation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework produces stable, continuous, and visually coherent hand–wind interactions in real time within a web browser. The system successfully translates natural hand gestures into spatial airflow patterns that interact intuitively with deformable cloth, without requiring specialized sensors or plugins. Our work highlights the potential of learning-based airflow modeling as a practical alternative to heuristic wind control for immersive, web-based interactive simulations.

title: Performance Enhancement of Cooperative NOMA in Satellite Communication through Combining Techniques
authors: K. Ashwini, Ashish Singh, V. K. Jagadeesh, Rachana Shenoy, Dushantha Nalin K. Jayakody
abstract: This article studies the analysis of the key performance parameters in the proposed cooperative Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) system considering the satellite network. NOMA can be coupled with standard relaying procedures to increase the overall capabilities of the wireless communication system. The system under consideration consists of two users, one near and a far user, and a satellite. The paper explores by using a Decode and Forward (DF) relaying mechanism at the user closer to the satellite, which will forward the signal received from the satellite user that is distant from it. In separate case studies, the far user combines the direct and indirect transmission signals using Maximal Ratio Combining (MRC) and Selection Combining (SC) techniques. The expressions for outage, average bit error rate (BER), and capacity are derived for MRC and SC methods. It is also derived and approximated, and closed-form expressions are obtained. The simulation results of these analytical expressions reveal that the MRC method of combining outperforms the SC technique in terms of outage, BER, and ergodic capacity.

title: ICECI 2026 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)

title: Optimizing Leaky Private Information Retrieval Codes to Achieve O (log K ) Leakage Ratio Exponent
authors: Wenyuan Zhao, Yu-Shin Huang, Chao Tian, Alex Sprintson
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: We study the problem of leaky private information retrieval (L-PIR), where the amount of privacy leakage is measured by the pure differential privacy parameter, referred to as the leakage ratio exponent. Unlike the previous L-PIR proposed by Samy et al., which is merely a re-allocation of the clean (low-cost) retrieval pattern within the generalized TSC family, we show that the active pure-DP constraints couple adjacent Hamming-weight layers of the random key, which reduces the optimization to a layered problem whose optimum is geometric across these layers. As a result, only cyclic permutations are needed without loss of optimality, and lower-Hamming weight keys should be assigned higher probabilities. This new scheme provides a significant improvement, leading to an O(logK) leakage ratio exponent with fixed download cost D, in contrast to the previous art that only achieves a Θ(K) exponent, where K is the number of messages.

title: Image-Guided Navigation Control for Automated Nanopositioning Based on Dense Photometric_supp1-3688826.mp4
authors: Hai Li
abstract: Image-guided automated positioning of microobjects in dynamic environments is highly needed in robot micro–nano manipulation. In this article, rendering the problem of collision-free microobject nanopositioning problem as a visual servoing problem, an image-guided navigation control scheme based on dense photometric is proposed. Specifically, the finite convergence domain existed in classical dense photometric-based visual servoing is treated as a constraint and uniformly modeled together with obstacle constraints in the navigation process. Subsequently, an online constraint handling unit based on artificial potential fields is developed to generate collision-free poses while consistently constraining the image evolution to remain within the local convergence region. Additionally, an inverse compositional algorithm is adopted to accelerate control computation, thereby enabling real-time performance. Finally, a series of experiments are conducted to verify the performance of the proposed method. Experimental results demonstrate that the method achieves reliable automatic nano-positioning and effective collision avoidance in both static and dynamic obstacle environments, confirming its feasibility and robustness in practical applications.

title: Residual-Augmented Dual-Layer Koopman Modeling for Distributed Parameter Systems
authors: Yaru Zhao, Han-Xiong Li, Yun Feng
abstract: Given that many industrial processes are governed by distributed parameter systems (DPSs), it is crucial to develop online models capable of robust spatiotemporal estimation under nonlinear, uncertain, or even entirely unknown conditions. To address this need, this article proposes a residual-augmented dual-layer Koopman modeling framework for DPSs, enabling real-time state estimation. First, an offline dual-layer Koopman model is constructed using Koopman randomized orthogonal decomposition and the lifting function to avoid nonlinear approximation. An adaptive term is then added to enable online coupling with a residual extended state observer. This allows the observer to estimate the mismatch between the process’s temporal dynamics in the lifting function space and the adaptive dual-layer Koopman model. The full spatiotemporal dynamics are then reconstructed through an inversion and time-space synthesis module. Additionally, a rigorous convergence guarantee is provided for the framework. The feasibility and effectiveness of the method are validated through two benchmark case studies with different boundary conditions, and its practical utility is demonstrated in real-time temperature monitoring of battery thermal processes.

title: IEEE Computer Society Grants for Emerging Technology
authors: N/A

title: Centroid-Guided Continuous Adaptation for Class-Incremental Source-Free Unsupervised Domain Adaptation of Ground Source Heat Pump Units
authors: Shouqi Wang, Xuejin Gao, Huayun Han, Hongyan Yang, Yue Liu, Huihui Gao, Yongsheng Qi
abstract: During long-term service, the fault types of ground source heat pump (GSHP) units gradually evolve with system aging. At the same time, constrained by engineering conditions, source-device data are usually unavailable at the model deployment stage, which makes cross-device diagnostic models prone to negative transfer and catastrophic forgetting when adapting to incremental faults. To address the above issues, this article proposes a class-incremental source-free unsupervised domain adaptation method based on centroid-guided continual adaptation. First, a gramian angular summation field is adopted to transform 1-D data into 2-D images, and the initial model training is completed using source-device data, after which the source data are inaccessible. Subsequently, a centroid-guided shared class mining method is designed to identify the shared classes at each incremental stage, and a collaborative discriminative pseudolabel assignment method is used to generate reliable pseudolabels for target domain data to drive model training while constructing a memory bank. Furthermore, a prototype topology distillation mechanism is introduced to maintain the topological consistency between new and old classes at the prototype level, effectively mitigating the impact of new class knowledge and thereby alleviating catastrophic forgetting. Validation on real-world GSHP industrial data indicates that, under the condition that source data are inaccessible, the proposed method demonstrates good cross-device incremental fault diagnosis capability, achieving an average accuracy of 80.20%.

title: A Boundary-Aware Surrogate Model for Predicting Feature-Scale TiN Etching Profiles
authors: Quanzhi Long, Fuqiang Zheng, Ting Yao, Xiaoming Shi, Xuqing Zhang, Dawei Gao
abstract: In semiconductor manufacturing research, AI efforts mainly concentrate on virtual metrology and defect inspection, outputting scalars or labels rather than full cross-sectional morphology. Feature-scale plasma etch simulators remain the primary way to obtain the morphology by numerically evolving the etched front, but they require meticulous physical modeling and take tens of minutes per recipe. This cost and sensitivity make systematic process-window exploration and design–technology cooptimization (DTCO)-style iteration impractical. We propose a boundary-aware, time-conditioned surrogate model that maps six process parameters and absolute etch time to a 256×256 TiN cross-section. The model is a lightweight decoder trained with an edge-weighted composite loss and evaluated through a single unified boundary pipeline. We report recipe-level boundary-F1 and HD95 with right-continuous P90 tails, and treat pixel MSE/SSIM as secondary checks. On 80 TiN etch recipes (2.4 k frames) generated by Latin hypercube sampling, compared with a capacity-matched U-Net decoder, the surrogate model has reduced the mean HD95 value by about 10%, tightened the tail distribution (HD95 P90 from 1.047 px to 0.986 px), and lowered MSE by about 12%. Generating an entire about 30-frame trajectory for one recipe takes about 53 ms on a laptop-class GPU, which is about 3×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> times faster than a commercial topography solver that takes 28 min per recipe. These improvements can be attributed to absolute time conditioning, CoordConv-based spatial localization, and deep-supervised edge-weighted loss, offering the promise of near-interactive exploration of etching evolution hypotheses.

title: Resilient Tube-Based Model Predictive Control With Polytopic Constraints Subject to DoS Attacks
authors: Shuang Shen, Junhong Liu, Runqi Chai, Yuanqing Xia, Senchun Chai
abstract: Tube-based model predictive control (TMPC), a well-performed control algorithm in disturbed control scenarios, is widely applied in networked control systems (NCSs). Due to the inherent nature of communication networks, NCSs always are at risk of being attacked. Consequently, the pristine TMPC-based systems may undergo severe destruction. However, the field about resilience of this control method against cyber-attacks in communication links is still in its infancy. In this article, we consider a resilience scheme based on an actuator buffer to eliminate the harmful effects caused by denial-of-service attacks. In addition, we assess the inherent attack tolerance of TMPC by calculating the maximal permissible open-loop steps, the maximal steps with no feedback control while provably maintaining recursive feasibility and input-to-state stability, for arbitrary states. The effectiveness and merits of the proposed resilience scheme and algorithm are demonstrated through numerical comparisons, furthermore, implemented on a three-wheeled omnidirectional robot platform.

title: Fast Frequency Regulation with Supercapacitor Energy Storage Systems
authors: Michael Hetzel, Roberto Bellapianta, Jonghun Yun, George Weiss, Federico Silvestro, Giovanni De Carne
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In this paper, the influence of grid following Supercap Energy Storage System (SCESS) on the frequency response of power systems is investigated. For this purpose, a single machine model with a gas turbine for primary control is augmented by a SCESS. To present the realistic behavior of SCESSs, the analysis is conducted using a 1 MW Power Hardware in the Loop (PHIL) setup comprising a Real Time Simulator (RTS) and a SCESS with a nominal power of 400 kW. The SCESS is operated using a Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) control algorithm to provide virtual inertia by injecting active power, thereby contributing to grid frequency stabilization. With the introduced model, a study is performed with parameters that mimic the whole behavior of the European Network of Transmission System Operators for Electricity (ENTSO-E) grid to show which impacts SCESSs can have on frequency regulation. The results demonstrate that SCESSs can enhance Rate of Change of Frequency (RoCoF) and the nadir of the grid significantly.

title: Multi-Query: Information Extraction-Driven Multi-Perspective Query Generation for Financial Report Generation
authors: Jiali Li, Yinshuang Liao, Cong Pan, Yihao Wang, Jingyao Meng, Jianeng Zhuo
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: For the task of generating financial reports from financial news, generating systems typically face numerous challenges, including insufficient original news information, semantic redundancy, and a lack of necessary domain-specific financial knowledge. These issues make it challenging for the generated content to strike a balance between accuracy and professionalism, thereby limiting the effectiveness and scalability of existing methods in practical applications.To address this issue, this paper proposes an optimized Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) method, which incorporates a structured information extraction module and combines a novel multi-view query generation strategy. Unlike traditional RAG methods that rely on a single original query or template-based queries, which often lead to semantic dilution and inaccurate retrieval, this paper introduces a novel approach. First, we build a structured Information Extraction module based on Machine Learning Comprehension (MRC) and a Pointer Network mechanism to automatically identify and extract key event elements from financial news texts. Then, using the extracted structured event information, we design prompt templates and input them into a Large Language Model (LLM), guiding it to generate multiple semantically focused natural language queries (Multi-Query) from several predefined semantic perspectives. Finally, we conduct systematic experiments on the public News-Report dataset to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The results show that, compared to full-text generation methods using raw news as query input and template-based query construction strategies, our Multi-Query generation approach achieves significant improvements on mainstream text generation evaluation metrics such as BLEU and ROUGE.

title: Evaluation and Application of Kilovolt GaN-on-Sapphire Diode IC
authors: Zineng Yang, Hengbo Zhang, Hehe Gong, Xin Yang, Hongchang Cui, Yuan Qin, Yuhao Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This work presents a comprehensive evaluation of a kilovolt-class monolithically integrated GaN-on-sapphire diode bridge IC designed for high-frequency, low-current, high-voltage power conversion. Building on our latest application of this IC in a 10 MHz, 1000 V Cockcroft-Walton voltage multiplier (CWVM), this study further studies the diode’s switching behaviors, the CWVM’s loss analysis, and the diode IC’s voltage scalability. Experimental double-pulse testing confirms the absence of reverse recovery charge, validating the unipolar nature of the device for megahertz operation. A detailed decomposition of the CWVM multiplication gain (Av) reveals that performance losses are primarily dominated by static forward voltage drops and load-dependent drops, while dynamic switching losses remain negligible due to the low-parasitic monolithic design. Furthermore, an investigation into the dynamic voltage blocking mechanism of the monolithically-integrated, series-connected diodes identifies a non-uniform voltage distribution governed by the parasitic capacitance network, where the top-most diode sustains the highest voltage stress. These results provide critical insights for the design and scalability of future monolithic high-voltage GaN ICs.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: AI Integrated Cybersecurity Risk Assessment Framework
authors: Ragesh K, Hariharan J, SanjayKumar S, Sarathi Ganesh S, P. Mohan Kumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The blistering-growing digital infrastructures, including Internet of Things (IoT), cloud infrastructures and cyber-physical systems, have greatly augmented the elements of complexity in the contemporary cyber environments. This development has also brought more cybersecurity threats particularly in those systems, which are based on artificial intelligence (AI). Conventional models of cybersecurity risk assessment are commonly rigid and they do not take into account AI-specific threats like adversarial attacks, model evasion, and data poisoning. This research presents an intelligent framework of AI-based cybersecurity risk assessment tool that will help assess dynamic threat in smart settings. The framework combines risk scoring based on the machine learning algorithm, adversarial threat modeling, explainable artificial intelligence methods, and the real-time threat intelligence. The architecture proposed is that which constantly monitors the risks in the AI lifecycle such as the data collection phase, the model training phase, the deployment phase, and the operational phases. The experimental assessment shows the enhanced threat detection and increased the risk transparency and adaptive support to the security management of complex digital ecosystems.

title: Electrical model and characterization of near-field antennas for communications from induction cooktop
authors: Javier Hernandez, Oscar Lahuerta, Sergio Llorente, Francisco Villuendas, Jesús Acero
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Induction cooktops can act as wireless power transmitters thanks to their internal structure, being able to transfer up to several kW of power. This range is well suited for real-time wireless operation of other household appliances such as cooking robots, which would be wirelessly powered by the cooktop itself and therefore would not require connection to the power grid. For successful operation, communication is generally required between the transmitter and receiver, which can be achieved by means of a near-field antenna placed between the heater and the receiver. These antennas are typically designed to operate at a frequency of 13.56 MHz with impedance matching to the amplifier. However, the elements that are part of the induction heating system can affect the impedance of the antenna and may compromise its correct operation. In this work, the influence of these elements is analyzed.

title: Design and Control of a GaN-Based High-Frequency Converter for Low-Voltage Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle with Extreme Voltage Gain
authors: Sang Min Lee, Hyeon Soo Kim, Yun Seong Hwang, Seung Hyun Kang, Ju-A Lee, Joo Seung Lee, Ju Han Kim, Jeong Hee Park, Byoung Kuk Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a DC-DC converter with extreme voltage gain suitable for a 55kW rated fuel cell electric vehicle (FCEV) with fuel cell (FC) stack in the range of 50 to 150V to enhance cost effectiveness. The proposed converter can achieve a voltage gain more than 10 times, which satisfies the voltage range of 350 to 650V for driving motor even with a low FC voltage around 100V. Additionally, the efficiency of proposed converter is measured over 96% under maximum power conditions. The operational modes according to the duty ratio are theoretically analyzed, and its feasibility is verified through experimental results of a 2.6kW prototype.

title: AMLSBF: Adaptive Multi-Layer Semantic–Behavior Fusion Framework for Intelligent Cloud Security
authors: Mande Sandeep, Shaik Kareemulla
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The growing complexity of cloud infrastructures has brought about major problems in the management and security of environments that are highly dynamic and very large-scale. The conventional defense models based on rules and regulations are not effective in solving the emerging environments of cyber threats that breed on contextual reliance and behavioural-patterns. In this paper, I am going to introduce a framework of the adaptive multi-layer semantic behavior fusion which provides stronger intelligent cloud security by integrating semantic reasoning and behavior analytics seamlessly. The given framework builds on the contextual semantics -based on the ontological structures, data interrelationships, and the intent of the user and utilizes real-time behavioral models of user and system interactions. These layers together can be used to identify anomalies in advance, predict possible threats, and automatically optimize its defense mechanisms with the help of constant learning. The paper offers an extensive literature review of the published research on semantic-based threat intelligence, behavioral detection models, and adaptable fusion practices. Moreover, it constructs a taxonomy which classifies existing models based on their design structures, computing performance, and scalability to various cloud eco systems. The current challenges covered in the survey are also lack of interoperability between heterogeneous systems, explainability in AI-based decision-making, and trade-off between adaptability and computational performance. The work forms a solid foundation on how to merge the aspect of semantic depth, and behavioral flexibility in the development of the future-generation, intelligent and self-evolving cloud security structures that can support complex and dynamic threat environments.

title: Performance Analysis of LSTM-Based Radar Scanning Pattern Recognition Under Quantum-Noise-Limited Sensing Conditions
authors: Sourav Kumar Mitra, Md. Waquib Bin Awal, Md Hossam-E-Haider
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Radar antenna scanning pattern recognition is an important task in electronic intelligence and electronic warfare, as it helps identify the scanning behavior of unknown radar emitters for threat evaluation. Recent studies have shown that long short-term memory (LSTM) networks can effectively recognize radar scanning patterns using pulse amplitude sequences under normal measurement conditions [6]. However, the performance of such methods under extremely noisy sensing environments has not been clearly studied. This work analyzes the limitations of amplitude-based LSTM recognition under quantum-noiselimited conditions. A realistic simulation framework is developed that models photon shot noise using Poisson statistics and includes practical effects such as amplitude-frequency response distortion, pulse amplitude measurement errors, missing pulses, and outliers. Mechanical scanning, one-dimensional phased array scanning, and two-dimensional phased array scanning patterns are represented as time-series amplitude sequences. Robust least absolute deviation (LAD) interpolation within coherent processing intervals is applied before classification using a lightweight LSTM network. Simulation results under different noise levels and measurement error conditions show that, in quantum-noiselimited scenarios, the recognition accuracy of amplitude-only LSTM models quickly saturates near random-guess performance. This demonstrates that the accuracy loss is caused by fundamental sensing noise rather than model design or training issues. The results highlight the need for additional signal information beyond amplitude to achieve reliable radar scanning pattern recognition in extreme noise conditions.

title: Integration of Cardiac Histopathological and Imaging Data to Improve Understanding of Stereotactic Arrhythmia Radioablation
authors: Juan A. Cisneros Jacome, Louis Rigal, Raphaël Martins, Solène-Florence Kammerer-Jacquet, Annegret E. Werner, Melvyn Dezecot, Loig Duverge, Renaud De Crevoisier, Julien Bellec, Antoine Simon
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ventricular tachycardia (VT) refractory to conventional therapies can be treated with stereotactic arrhythmia radioablation (STAR), yet its biological mechanisms remain unclear. To locally analyze the myocardial response to irradiation, we propose an full workflow integrating in-vivo treatment planning, ex-vivo imaging, and histopathology from an explanted human heart post-STAR. The method establishes a landmarkbased registration framework aligning ex-vivo and in-vivo data within a common coordinate system using anatomical landmarks (base, apex, and a rotation point). Twenty-one anatomical landmarks enabled consistent registration of CT, radiation dose maps and histopathology descriptors. By quantifying the fibrosis fraction in each histological sample, the framework expanded the dataset from 21 to 210 localized measurements. Correlation analysis revealed heterogeneous dose-effect relationships, with higher fibrosis density in regions exposed to 27-31 Gy and minimal changes in apical areas (< 2 Gy). This framework demonstrates the feasibility of direct dose-histopathology correlation in human cardiac tissue and provides a reproducible approach bridging radiotherapy, cardiology, and pathology for STAR evaluation.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization of Multi-Level DC-AC Converter for Single-Phase PV Microinverters
authors: Abhishek Awasthi, Majid Pahlevani, Praveen Jain
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A flying capacitor (FC)-based multilevel structure is proposed as a viable solution for voltage-source inverter (VSI) implementation in a two-stage, single-phase, grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) microinverter application. The compact size and high-density design requirements for micro-inverter applications necessitate the optimal design of passive components, specifically the flying capacitors (FCs). Existing studies lack a systematic methodology for designing FCs of individual voltage levels and are based on a single objective of satisfying energy storage requirements, leading to suboptimal designs. The lack of generalized analytical expressions for FC-multilevel inverter in the grid-connected mode further inhibits study of critical performance characteristics, impeding optimal design. This study presents a practical multi-objective design optimization framework for FC-based VSI structure. The results show that the proposed capacitor-aware optimization procedure reduces the resulting DC-link capacitor design volume by 7% compared to a pure film-capacitor DC-link for a 2-level VSI with 10% lower cost. Additionally, a 17% volume reduction compared to the heuristic ratiometric FC design strategy with no efficiency degradation is achieved with the proposed optimization method.

title: IoT Based Water Monitoring and Automatic Tank Filling System
authors: Sweta Bokade, Sumit Jaisinghani, Anish Mankani, Hitesh Motwani, Devansh Motwani, Juhi Sawlani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: A vital natural resource, water's availability and quality are critical to industry, agriculture, and human health. However, in many areas, there are common issues due to water contamination and wash and wastage and manual tank management. To solve these problems, the project presents a system of monitoring an IOT-based water monitoring and automatic tanks that add smart sensing, automation and wireless communication to improve the efficiency of water use and ensure water safety. The system appoints various sensors for monitoring major water quality indicators such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, TDS, turbidity, and temperature, provides a comprehensive view of water purity in real time. In addition to quality monitoring, a water level sensor is used to track the water level of the tank. When the level falls below a set threshold and water is considered safe depending on the sensor reading, the system automatically activates a solenoid valve to recreate the tank. Once reaching the desired level, the valve is closed to prevent overflow and water dissipation. A microcontroller, such as ESP32, collects and processes the sensor data, which is then wirelessly transmitted to a firebase or blynk like a cloud platform. Users can access real -time data, historical logs and alerts through mobile or desktop applications, which can enable remote monitoring and control. This system enhances the facility, promotes water conservation, and improves health protection only by storing and using clean water to ensure that. Overall, the project provides a smart, low cost and scalable solution for water management in houses, schools, hospitals and industrial facilities. Automation and IOT integration minimizes human labor, conserves water, and promotes sustainable water management techniques.

title: DERMAE: Improving Skin Lesion Classification Through Conditioned Latent Diffusion and MAE Distillation
authors: Francisco Filho, Kelvin Cunha, Fábio Papais, Emanoel dos Santos, Rodrigo Mota, Thales Bezerra, Erico Medeiros, Paulo Borba, Tsang Ing Ren
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Skin lesion classification datasets often suffer from severe class imbalance, with malignant cases significantly underrepresented, leading to biased decision boundaries during deep learning training. We address this challenge using classconditioned diffusion models to generate synthetic dermatological images, followed by self-supervised MAE pretraining to enable huge ViT models to learn robust, domain-relevant features. To support deployment in practical clinical settings, where lightweight models are required, we apply knowledge distillation to transfer these representations to a smaller ViT student suitable for mobile devices. Our results show that MAE pretraining on synthetic data, combined with distillation, improves classification performance while enabling efficient on-device inference for practical clinical use.

title: TAPE: A Two-Stage Parameter-Efficient Adaptation Framework for Foundation Models in OCT-OCTa Analysis
authors: Xiaofei Su, Zengshuo Wang, Minghe Sun, Xin Zhao, Mingzhu Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Automated analysis of optical coherence tomography (OCT) and OCT angiography (OCTA) images is critical for robust ophthalmic diagnosis. Existing mainstream methods trained from scratch rely heavily on massive data and model scale, thereby hindering their practical deployment in resourceconstrained clinical settings. Although transfer learning based on foundation models (FMs) is promising, it still faces significant challenges: domain shift and task misalignment. To address these, we propose TAPE: A Two-stage Adaptation Framework via Parameter-E fficient Fine-tuning, which strategically decouples adaptation into domain alignment and task fitting for downstream segmentation. The domain adaptation stage notably applies parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) in the context of masked image modeling for medical image domain adaptation, a novel approach to the best of our knowledge. Applying TAPE to retinal layer segmentation on both universal (masked auto-encoder, MAE) and specialized (RETFound) FMs, it demonstrates superior parameter efficiency and achieves state-of-the-art generalization performance across diverse pathologies.

title: Electrically Injected 1.55 μm InAs/InP QD Lasers on On-Axis (001) Si Using a Ge-Based Buffer
authors: Zhao Yan, Shangfeng Liu, Ka Ming Wong, Haotian Zeng, Yangqian Wang, Jae-Seong Park, Hui Jia, Zhongming Cao, Curtis Hentschel, Mickael Martin, Mingchu Tang, Samuel Shutts, Thierry Baron, Huiyun Liu, Peter M. Smowton, Qiang Li
abstract: We report <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>-band InAs/InP quantumdot lasers grown on CMOS-compatible on-axis (001) Si using a Ge buffer and InAsP/InP dislocation filters. Deepetched ridge waveguide lasers show threshold current of 301 mA, an optical output power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>10 \text{mW}$</tex> per-facet, and operation up to 80° C.

title: Design of a 6.5 kV SiC MOSFET Full-bridge Module
authors: Max Rogers, Arthur Boutry, Andrew N. Lemmon, Nicholas Turner, Nick Baker
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This article details the design of a 6.5 kV Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFET module in the full-bridge topology. The increased industrial use of SiC modules in industrial and transportation applications is driving intensified interest in module topologies beyond the conventional half-bridge. Advanced module topologies unlock greater levels of physical integration and power density for power electronic systems such as DC-to-DC converters and electric motor drives. The prototype module described in this article adopts an industry-standard footprint and represents a significant advancement of a previous prototype design performed by the authors. This article discusses the design of the prototype module, along with fabrication details, simulation results, and preliminary experimental results.

title: Generative AI for Overcoming Rf Data Inaccessibility in Quantitative Ultrasound Imaging
authors: Nasrin Sheibani-Asl, Gregory Czarnota, Ali Sadeghi-Naini
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Quantitative ultrasound (QUS) imaging enables noninvasive tissue characterization using frequency-dependent parameters derived from raw radiofrequency (RF) data. However, the limited accessibility to RF data, particularly in commercial scanners, restricts its clinical use. This study adapts a conditional generative adversarial network (cGAN) to synthesize RF data from conventional B-mode images, addressing this limitation. A large paired dataset of B-mode and RF signals acquired from 152 patients with benign and malignant breast lesions was used for training and evaluation. Quantitative pixel-level metrics were employed to assess reconstruction fidelity, while histograms of QUS parametric map mean-values as well as the attenuation coefficient estimates derived from the original and synthetic RF data were analyzed for distributional agreement. The synthesized RF data showed close similarity to the originals (SSIM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.81 \pm 0.06$</tex> for RF and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.87 \pm 0.04$</tex> for B-mode). These findings demonstrate the feasibility of translating B-mode images into RF-equivalent representations for QUS analysis, which support wider clinical adoption of QUS imaging.

title: Intelligent Optimal Design of Community Public Interaction Space for Aging Groups
authors: Zhicheng Zhang, Kyoung Kook Chang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In view of the trend of population aging, it is necessary to construct an efficient and interactive community service network to meet the needs of the aging group. In order to improve the quality of daily life of the aging population and ensure efficient allocation and utilization of resources, the study focuses on the design of community public interaction space for the aging population, aiming to enhance the efficiency and quality of the community service network through intelligent optimal design. The study is based on genetic simulated annealing algorithm for intelligent optimal design of community public interaction space for aging groups, combining the global search efficiency of genetic algorithm and the local search advantage of simulated annealing algorithm in order to seek the optimal solution. The results show that the genetic simulated annealing algorithm outperforms other algorithms in terms of the convergence speed and accuracy of the loss function, especially after 100 iterations, the value of the loss function is close to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-5</sup>, and the accuracy rate is close to 1.0. The separation degree of the genetic simulated annealing algorithm after optimization is improved from 0.57 to 0.62 in the range of 10km, showing the effectiveness of the optimization measures. The study can significantly improve the quality of life of the elderly by improving the separation degree and service quality of community public interaction space, and provide a sustainable community development model for the aging society.

title: Cross Regulation Characteristics by Current Dip in Fly-buck Converter
authors: Younghoon Cho, Paul Jang, Jong-Won Shin
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A Fly-buck converter is one of the multiple-output converters which has non-isolated and isolated outputs. The isolated output voltage suffers from poor voltage regulation characteristic since it is controlled indirectly. The parasitic capacitances and leakage inductance induce current dip at the non-isolated output during dead time between the high- and low-side switches, and the isolated output voltage is affected by this phenomenon. In this paper, the cross regulation of isolated voltage is analyzed. The 20-W prototype to power gate driver was built and tested to verify the analysis.

title: Overvoltage Mitigation via Variable Switching Frequency Discontinuous PWM in SiC Inverter Systems
authors: Min-Gi Kim, Kyo-Beum Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes an overvoltage mitigation via variable switching frequency discontinuous PWM in SiC inverter systems. Variable switching frequency secures zero-vector voltage in each DPWM interval to suppress the double pulsing effect. The variable switching frequency is classified into modulation for double pulsing suppression and compensation for switching. The variable switching frequency is formulated with consideration of the voltage reference in DPWM. A flowchart illustrating the proposed algorithm is presented. The validity of the proposed method is verified through simulations.

title: A Novel Dual-Stream Framework for dMRI Tractography Streamline Classification with Joint dMRI and fMRI Data
authors: Haotian Yan, Bocheng Guo, Jianzhong He, Nir A. Sochen, Ofer Pasternak, Lauren J O'Donnell, Fan Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Streamline classification is essential to identify anatomically meaningful white matter tracts from diffusion MRI (dMRI) tractography. However, current streamline classification methods rely primarily on the geometric features of the streamline trajectory, failing to distinguish between functionally distinct fiber tracts with similar pathways. To address this, we introduce a novel dual-stream streamline classification framework that jointly analyzes dMRI and functional MRI (fMRI) data to enhance the functional coherence of tract parcellation. We design a novel network that performs streamline classification using a pretrained backbone model for full streamline trajectories, while augmenting with an auxiliary network that processes fMRI signals from fiber endpoint regions. We demonstrate our method by parcellating the corticospinal tract (CST) into its four somatotopic subdivisions. Experimental results from ablation studies and comparisons with state-of-the-art methods demonstrate our approach's superior performance. Code: https://github.com/Hotel-Y/DF-NET_ISBI.git

title: Joint User Grouping and Power Allocation in RIS-Aided MISO-NOMA System under Rician Fading Channel
authors: Karima Ait Bouslam, Jamal Amadid, Fatim-Zahra Bennioui, Radouane Iqdour, Abdelouhab Zeroual
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: For 6G and beyond, the combination of reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) and non-orthogonal multiple access has shown to be a successful strategy. offering a number of benefits, such as excellent spectral efficiency (SE) and extensive connectivity. Nevertheless, the RIS-aided NOMA system’s effectiveness was still hampered by interference. In order to address this, we use a joint dynamic user grouping and power allocation-based particle swarm optimization technique (PSO) for the RIS-assisted MISO-NOMA system in this research. The SE optimization problem is then posed under stringent constraints. The chordal distance serves as the foundation for the dynamic user grouping technique. The results of the simulation evaluate how well the suggested grouping performs in comparison to current benchmark algorithms.

title: From Aeronautical Information Management to Aeronautical Knowledge Management : Case study of the Moroccan Air Navigation Service Provider
authors: Youness Belkhoumani, Abdelati Hakmaoui
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Aeronautical Information Management (AIM) plays a crucial role in the global air navigation system. AIM is considered as a pre-requisite for a collaborative decision-making environment based on high-quality, timely-available and reliable aeronautical data and information intended to ensure safe and efficient air navigation operations. The aim of this paper is to promote an Aeronautical Knowledge Management (AKM) approach based on a suitable knowledge management system designated for aviation professionals by investigating the significance of Knowledge Management (KM) processes’ implementation on Air Navigation Services Provider (ANSP) Employee Performance (EP) through the mediating role of Information Technology Capability (ITC).This research was founded on Resource-Based View (RBV) and on Knowledge-Based View (KBV) theories. It involves a quantitative approach with a case study method. Data obtained from 210 participants collected through a self-administrated questionnaire were assessed by SPSS V.27 and Smart PLS V.4 software, with results displayed in numerical form evaluating structural model, hypotheses, direct and indirect effects.The results acknowledge the direct significance of KM on ITC. However, the obliviousness of the concept of KM by nearly the two third of the survey participants could interpret the analysis’ findings inconsistent with the literature which indicates the positive direct and indirect effect of KM on EP. Results also illustrate the positive influence of ITC on EP. This research investigates the mediating role of ITC on the relationship between KM and EP especially in aviation public sector and it admits KM "awareness gap" as a crucial precondition for KM efficiency with an interesting 64% rate of respondents unaware about KM processes explaining why KM could not affect EP.

title: A Model-Based Reconstruction Approach to Field-Free Line Magnetic Particle Imaging
authors: Jonas Schumacher, Artyom Tsanda, Patricia Römer, Niklas Hackelberg, Tobias Knopp
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Magnetic particle imaging (MPI) visualizes the spatial distribution of superparamagnetic iron oxide nanoparticles by exploiting their nonlinear magnetization response to time-varying magnetic fields. In open-sided MPI scanners using a field-free line (FFL), tomographic imaging is achieved by rotating the FFL and reconstructing from angledependent projections. At low tracer concentrations, the induced voltage signal has reduced signal-to-noise ratio resulting in pronounced reconstruction artifacts. The ISBI2026 low-concentration MPI reconstruction challenge aims to address this problem by providing a dataset of high- and low-concentration samples. This paper describes a solution to this challenge. We propose a two-stage pipeline: a simulated system matrix based on an equilibrium Langevin forward model is used to recover sinograms followed by iterative image reconstruction. Despite limited system information, the approach yields competitive reconstructions, though remaining artifacts and moderate similarity metrics indicate a high potential for improvement.

title: Four-Quadrant Control of CSI fed PMSM Drive for EV Applications with Improved Efficiency
authors: Ashish Kumar, Apurv Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The advent of wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductor devices, such as silicon carbide (SiC), has renewed interest in Current Source Inverter (CSI) topologies, particularly due to the high dv/dt stress limitations associated with conventional Voltage Source Inverters (VSIs). This paper presents an H7-type CSI architecture employing SiC-based four-quadrant (FQ) switches, developed for electric vehicle (EV) traction applications. To enhance system efficiency, a new pulse-width modulation (PWM) strategy is proposed that effectively minimizes switching transitions, thereby reducing overall switching losses. The inverter is supplied by a controlled DC current through a prestage converter, forming a complete current-source-based drive system. An Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine (IPMSM) is used as the traction motor, and the control strategy incorporates Maximum Torque Per Ampere (MTPA)-based Field-Oriented Control (FOC) implemented with a single current sensor. Simulation results validate the proposed approach by demonstrating the stable operation across both motoring and regenerative braking modes under various dynamic conditions with improved inverter efficiency.

title: A Unified Control Architecture for Multiport Resonant Converters with Independently Regulated Output Ports
authors: Arkadeb Sengupta, Waseah Anjum, Marco Liserre
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Frequency-modulated multiport resonant converters (MPRCs) incorporate additional controllable elements, such as controlled inductors, capacitors, or rectifiers, to regulate individual output ports. However, existing studies do not clearly describe how the closed-loop control of individual output ports, achieved using these controllable elements, is to be combined with conventional frequency-modulated control approaches. In this paper, a generalized architecture is proposed to unify variable-frequency control of MPRCs with strategies involving other controllable components. The stability of the proposed control approach, as well as the optimization of control effort achieved through it, is evaluated for a controlled rectifier. Simulation and experimental results on a 100V-to-12V, 600W, 4-output MPRC are presented to validate the proposed strategy.

title: Semantic-Aware 3D Polyp Segmentation in Dynamic Endoscopy Scenes
authors: Jinhua Liu, Wen Tang, Yongsheng Shi, Dongjin Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: This paper proposes the first 3D polyp segmentation method based on Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs) for dynamic endoscopy scenes. A two-staged training approach, SAPolyp-NeRF (Segment Anything Polyp in NeRFs), is introduced. The first stage is to reconstruct the dynamic implicit field of soft tissue. Then, a multi-view self-prompt mechanism is employed to automatically predict 2D polyp masks for the rendered views generated by the dynamic implicit field. The second stage is a 2D-to-3D segmentation module, in which a view-alternating weak supervision strategy is introduced to train SAPolypNeRF for the second time using 2D polyp masks supervision aggregated from multiple training views to produce a dynamic 3D mask implicit field for 3D polyp segmentation. The experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves higher-quality comprehensive reconstruction and accurate segmentation of the lesion region than the state-of-the-art 3D segmentation methods, resulting in the best overall performance.

title: Circuit-Based Analytical Model for the Airgap Effect on Current Sharing in Parallel Windings of a Planar PCB Transformer
authors: Yan Liang, Yi-Hsun Hsieh, Qiang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Planar transformers with PCB winding are increasingly popular in modern power conversion systems due to their controllable design and high-power density. The high-frequency operation enables small core volume of planar transformers, which improves power density but also introduces undesirable effects such as current redistribution within the windings, increasing conduction losses. To mitigate eddy-current effects and handle high AC currents, multiple winding layers are connected in parallel. However, current sharing among these parallel layers can be influenced by a magnetic core air gap, which degrades the effectiveness of parallel windings. Previous research on air gap effects has mainly focused on fringing flux mitigations. In contrast, the impact of the air gap on current sharing among parallel windings has not been thoroughly investigated. To address this gap, this paper presents a simple analytical model for predicting current distribution in parallel windings while accounting for the air gap effect. The model is derived from the electrical circuit of the transformer and provides both efficient evaluation of winding configurations and air gap positioning, as well as physical insight into the mechanisms governing current sharing in planar transformers.

title: DIFF-MAMBA: Efficient State-Space Modeling for End-To-End Ultrasound Video Diagnosis
authors: Jinghan Yang, Tian Jie, Kun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Breast cancer remains the most common malignancy among women worldwide. B-mode grayscale ultrasound is widely used for screening and diagnosis in dense breast tissue due to its safety, accessibility, and cost-effectiveness. While conventional computeraided diagnostic (CAD) systems rely on static frames, clinical interpretation depends on temporal cues such as probe motion, boundary stability, and posterior acoustic changes. To address this gap, we propose Diff-Mamba, an end-to-end framework that directly processes grayscale ultrasound videos and leverages inter-frame differences to capture motion-consistent structural and textural patterns. Built upon the Mamba selective state-space model, Diff-Mamba efficiently encodes long-range temporal dependencies while preserving temporal continuity. The dual-branch attention pooling module further integrates spatial and temporal information for malignancy prediction. Experiments on two public ultrasound video datasets demonstrate that Diff-Mamba achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing deep learning baselines. The code and dataset resources are publicly available at https://github.com/hanliumu/DIFFMAMBA.

title: Gaze2Report: Radiology Report Generation via Visual-Gaze Prompt Tuning of LLMs
authors: Aishik Konwer, Moinak Bhattacharya, Prateek Prasanna
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Existing deep learning methods for radiology report generation enhance diagnostic efficiency but often overlook physician-informed medical priors. This leads to a suboptimal alignment between the structured explanations and disease manifestations. Eye gaze data provides critical insights into a radiologist's visual attention, enhancing the relevance and interpretability of extracted features while aligning with human decision-making processes. However, despite its promising potential, the integration of eye gaze information into AI-driven medical imaging workflows is impeded by challenges such as the complexity of multimodal data fusion and the high cost of gaze acquisition, particularly its absence during inference, limiting its practical applicability in real-world clinical settings. To address these issues, we introduce Gaze2Report, a framework which leverages a scanpath prediction module and Graph Neural Network (GNN) to generate joint visual-gaze tokens. Combined with instruction and report tokens, these form a multimodal prompt used to fine-tune LoRA layers of large language models (LLMs) for autoregressive report generation. Gaze2Report enhances report quality through eye-gaze-guided visual learning and incorporates on-the-fly scanpath prediction, enabling the model to operate without gaze input during inference.

title: Entropy-Guided Agreement-Diversity: A Semi-Supervised Active Learning Framework for Fetal Head Segmentation in Ultrasound
authors: Fangyijie Wang, Siteng Ma, Guénolé Silvestre, Kathleen M. Curran
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Fetal ultrasound (US) data is often limited due to privacy and regulatory restrictions, posing challenges for training deep learning (DL) models. While semi-supervised learning (SSL) is commonly used for fetal US image analysis, existing SSL methods typically rely on random limited selection, which can lead to suboptimal model performance by overfitting to homogeneous labeled data. To address this, we propose a two-stage Active Learning (AL) sampler, Entropy-Guided Agreement-Diversity (EGAD), for fetal head segmentation. Our method first selects the most uncertain samples using predictive entropy, and then refines the final selection using the agreement-diversity score combining cosine similarity and mutual information. Additionally, our SSL framework employs a consistency learning strategy with feature downsampling to further enhance segmentation performance. In experiments, SSL-EGAD achieves an average Dice score of 94.57% and 96.32% on two public datasets for fetal head segmentation, using 5% and 10% labeled data for training, respectively. Our method outperforms current SSL models and showcases consistent robustness across diverse pregnancy stage data. The code is available on GitHub.

title: End-to-End Mandibular Reconstruction with Integrated Repositioning and Completion Networks
authors: Feng Lu, Chenfan Xu, Jiacheng Sun, Chi Zhang, Min Zhu, Zhiming Cui, Dinggang Shen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Patients with mandibular defects require mandibular reconstruction surgery to restore both physiological function and facial morphology. Accurate preoperative planning relies on a complete mandible in its correct anatomical position. However, existing methods primarily focus on morphological completion, without considering anatomical repositioning, which can result in significant issues if the defective mandible is left in a compensatory malposition. This paper presents a novel end-to-end framework that jointly performs mandible repositioning and morphological completion. In the repositioning stage, we predict rigid transformations to restore the displaced mandible to its anatomically correct location. The completion stage then restores the repositioned mandible to its complete morphology. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method surpasses state-of-the-art approaches, showing strong potential for clinical applications in computer-assisted craniofacial reconstruction.

title: Monolithic C-L-C Hybrid DC-DC Converter for High efficiency High-Density Direct 48V-Sub-1.8V Point-of-load Applications
authors: Guozhen Chang, Wing-Hung Ki
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: There is high demand for power converters with ultra-low voltage conversion ratio (VCR) for high-performance computing (HPC) and data centers. Recently, numerous hybrid power converter topologies have been proposed, but each has its own performance and control challenges. To arrive at an optimized solution of power delivery, this paper suggests a monolithic CLC hybrid power converter. Power loss is minimized in three aspects: (1) lowering voltage stress of switches; (2) reducing inductor DC resistance (DCR) loss; and (3) soft charging of capacitors. The converter is designed and simulated using a 0.18μm BCD process. It is compared with three popular commercial products: the 3-level, the 2C-3level, and the double-step-down (DSD) converters. The CLC converter switches at 1MHz and achieves a peak efficiency of 94.5% for 48V-1.8V conversion, and 90.3% for 48V-1V conversion, with a current density of 4.07A/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. This represents a current density improvement of 300%, and the efficiency improvement is 10% better than a traditional buck converter.

title: Real-World Tomato Leaf Disease Detection: A Comparative Study of YOLOv5–YOLOv12
authors: Hiba Tabbaa, Oumayma Ouedrhiri, Hamza Ettakaddoumi, Marouane Morfi, Ouiam Kamili, Oumaima Essaoud, Mohamed Lachgar
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum L.) production is threatened by foliar diseases that can cause severe yield and quality losses if not detected early. To support practical monitoring, this paper compares six YOLO small variants for real-world tomato disease detection using a manually annotated bounding-box dataset collected in open fields and greenhouses. Under a unified training/evaluation protocol, YOLOv5s, YOLOv8s, YOLOv9s, YOLOv10s, YOLOv11s, and YOLOv12s are benchmarked using mAP@0.5, mAP@0.5:0.95, precision, recall, and F1-score, alongside deployability indicators (speed, model size, FLOPs). The results highlight clear accuracy–efficiency trade-offs and provide guidance for selecting models for mobile/edge tomato disease monitoring.

title: A Multi-Scale Deep Learning Framework for Coastal Boundary Segmentation
authors: Marc-André Blais, Moulay A. Akhloufi
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Autonomously extracting coastal boundaries from satellite images is a critical task for coastal monitoring and management, enabling the tracking of coastal erosion with significant environmental and economic impact. Deep learning has seen increasing adoption for this task due to its ability to automatically process large volumes of remote sensing data. However, current methods remain limited by insufficient integration of global and local spatial information. In this work, a two-phase deep learning approach designed to enhance global–local perception for coastal boundary segmentation is presented. This architecture enables classical segmentation models to better capture both global structure and fine coastal features. The proposed GLoCoNet, using SegFormer MiT-B5 with a 2048–512 configuration, achieved highly accurate coastal boundary segmentation while producing finer and more coherent boundaries. It obtained an F1-score of $99.49 \%$, ePPA<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> of $12.68 \%$, ePPA<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</inf> of $71.37 \%$, an ASSD of 16.86 and HD of 431.40. Compared to the baseline singlescale SegFormer MiT-B5 (512), which achieved 99.36%, 12.84%, $66.99 \%, 33.90$ and 705.70 for the same metrics, GLoCoNet improved edge-based accuracy and reduced boundary artifacts. Qualitative visual analysis further confirmed the benefits of global–local feature integration, demonstrating cleaner boundary delineation and improved consistency across validation samples. Overall, these results highlight the effectiveness of multi-scale integration for high-resolution coastal boundary extraction using deep learning.

title: Low-Profile Vertical Shunt Resistor Integration in High-Current Power Modules
authors: Thiyu Warnakulasooriya, Jiyoon Choi, Sihoon Choi, Jun Imaoka, Masayoshi Yamamoto, Leonard Henrichs, Jan Ückerseifer, Jan Marien
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a low-profile vertical shunt resistor and its integration method in a high-current SiC power module. The proposed shunt resistor employs a parallel-pad structure and a low-resistivity alloy to achieve a resistance of 50 µΩ, while maintaining low inductance and a low temperature coefficient of resistance (TCR). The resistor is integrated into a 200 kW SiC half-bridge power module without occupying additional substrate area. Measured resistance values show strong agreement with simulated results across multiple sensing locations. Thermal cycling tests validate the reliability of the solder joints between the resistor and substrate, confirming the suitability of the proposed structure for high-current power modules.

title: Topology Optimized TE-Pass Polarizer on a Commercial Foundry
authors: Jacob M. Hiesener, Prankush Agarwal, Joshua J. Wong, Stephen E. Ralph
abstract: We experimentally validate an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 4 \mu^{2}$</tex> topology optimized TE-pass polarizer on a commercial foundry, achieving a record high transmission of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>-0.09 \text{dB}$</tex> for the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TE}_{00}$</tex> mode with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>15 \text{dB} \mathbf{T M}_{\mathbf{0 0}}$</tex> extinction over all of C-band.

title: Adaptive Intrusion Detection and Mitigation System Using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)
authors: Aya Jamal Sanad, Eman Salih Al-Shamery
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are one of the essential components of modern cybersecurity infrastructure. However, the classical signature-based system and supervised learning model lack flexibility during adaptive changes of attack behaviors and associated countermeasure strategies. This research proposes an adaptive closed-loop system model to jointly optimize the tasks of intrusion detection and attack mitigation using a reinforcement learning algorithm called Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). In this model, there are two reinforcement learning agents. The first agent is a PPO-IDS agent employing PPO for binary and multi-class traffic classification as critical component of intrusion detection. The second is the PPO-M agent using PPO to identify cost-effective mitigation strategies such as allowing traffic, rate limiting, temporary/permanent blocking, as well as deep packet inspection. The proposed model is tested using NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 dataset with standard preprocessing. The test results confirm high accuracy levels of 99.8% and 99.1% for NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 dataset, respectively. Additionally, the results confirm steady improvement of the precision of the mitigation agent with training episodes, indicating adaptive learning. This research study confirms that there is a robust cybersecurity system through adaptive optimization of multiple tasks using a single reinforcement learning model.

title: Adaptive Clinical-Aware Latent Diffusion for Multimodal Brain Image Generation and Missing Modality Imputation
authors: Rong Zhou, Houliang Zhou, Yao Su, Brian Y. Chen, Yu Zhang, Lifang He
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multimodal neuroimaging provides complementary insights for Alzheimer's disease diagnosis, yet clinical datasets frequently suffer from missing modalities. We propose ACADiff, a framework that synthesizes missing brain imaging modalities through adaptive clinical-aware diffusion. ACADiff learns mappings between incomplete multimodal observations and target modalities by progressively denoising latent representations while attending to available imaging data and clinical metadata. The framework employs adaptive fusion that dynamically reconfigures based on input availability, coupled with semantic clinical guidance via GPT-4o-encoded prompts. Three specialized generators enable bidirectional synthesis among sMRI, FDG-PET, and AV45-PET. Evaluated on ADNI subjects, ACADiff achieves superior generation quality and maintains robust diagnostic performance even under extreme 80% missing scenarios, outperforming all existing baselines. To promote reproducibility, code is available at https://github.com/rongzhou7/ACADiff.

title: Multimodal Oncology Agent for Idh1 Mutation Prediction in Low-Grade Glioma
authors: Hafsa Akebli, Adam Shephard, Vincenzo Della Mea, Nasir Rajpoot
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Low-grade gliomas frequently present IDH1 mutations that define clinically distinct subgroups with specific prognostic and therapeutic implications. This work introduces a Multimodal Oncology Agent (MOA) integrating a histology tool based on the TITAN foundation model for IDH1 mutation prediction in low-grade glioma, combined with reasoning over structured clinical and genomic inputs through PubMed, Google Search, and OncoKB. MOA reports were quantitatively evaluated on 488 patients from the TCGA-LGG cohort against clinical and histology baselines. MOA without the histology tool outperformed the clinical baseline, achieving an F1-score of 0.826 compared to 0.798. When fused with histology features, MOA reached the highest performance with an F1-score of 0.912, exceeding both the histology baseline at 0.894 and the fused histology-clinical baseline at 0.897. These results demonstrate that the proposed agent captures complementary mutation-relevant information enriched through external biomedical sources, enabling accurate IDH1 mutation prediction.

title: Segmentation and Diagnosis of Fetal Heart Ultrasound Based on Anatomical Structure Priors and Dual-Threshold Guided Semi-Supervised Learning
authors: Bo Zhai, Changqin Yan, Zhiyuan Zhao, Fangjin Deng, Feng Wei, Chenzi Wang, Kundong Mo
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of scarce labeled data, complex anatomical structures and insufficient boundary accuracy in intelligent diagnosis of fetal congenital heart disease, this paper proposes a fetal heart ultrasound segmentation and diagnosis algorithm fusing anatomical structure prior and semi-supervised learning. Its innovations include three aspects: designing an Anatomical Structure Query-based Segmentation Decoder to embed medical prior into global feature learning; proposing a View-Aware Connected Component Loss to dynamically constrain the connected domain morphology of anatomical structures and suppress false positives and structural fragmentation; putting forward a Dual-Threshold Guided Semi-Supervised Classification Learning strategy to maximize the value of unlabeled data and alleviate class imbalance and pseudo-label noise. Ablation studies on the FETUS 2026 dataset reveal that our modular framework achieves state-of-the-art performance by specializing components: the Anatomical Structure Query Decoder drives segmentation accuracy with a DSC of 74.43 and an NSD of 49.16, whereas the View-Aware Connected Component Loss optimizes the comprehensive diagnostic metric with an overall Score of 48.17, collectively demonstrating superior generalization and robustness compared to the baseline.

title: Design of Hybrid Micromixer for Biomedical Applications
authors: Halah Basim Al-Mudhafar, Qais Al-Gayem
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Microfluidic mixing is one of the core unit operations for miniaturized analysis devices. Achieving high mixing of conventional microfluidic micromixers are inherently difficult to obtain as the fluid flow is typically in the laminar flow range where molecular diffusion predominates. In this work, a numerical investigation of a microfluidic micromixer with a hybrid actuation scheme and micropillar shape and gap variation is presented.
