COMSOL Multiphysics v.6.1. is used for the purpose laminar physics to compute fluid flow governing equations, and diluted transport species to compute mixing governing equations. The simulations were carried out at different Reynolds numbers like 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40, 80, and 100 with an oscillation frequency of 10 MHz. Three micromixer designs are investigated in this study: T-mixer, a square micromixer, a hexagonal passive micromixer. With a mixing index of almost $86.7 \%$, the passive hexagonal design demonstrated the highest efficiency of all passive buildings. The hexagonal micromixer was then equipped with acoustic actuation to improve the mixing process even more. Under the same operating conditions, the active arrangement showed a notable improvement, achieving a mixing index of $99.73 \%$. The findings show that a highly effective method for microfluidic mixing in biomedical applications can be achieved by combining optimal geometry and acoustic excitation.

title: Normal-Mode Helical Antenna with Tap-Fed Impedance Step-Up for Near-Metal UHF RFID Systems
authors: Nur Syazani Bin Norman, Badrul Amin Bin Azahari, Nazihah binti Ahmad, Norsiha Zainuddin, Noorlindawaty Md Jizat, Yoshihide Yamada
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Normal-mode helical antennas (NMHAs) are unique for UHF RFID applications due to their compact size and the distinctive advantage in its simultaneous electric and magnetic current radiation characteristics. In comparison with the conventional dipole antennas, whose performance degrades severely in the presence of metal due to electric-field suppression, NMHAs have a dominant magnetic near field that is less sensitive to conductive surfaces. However, electrically short NMHAs exhibit very low input resistance, which made practical impedance is challenging, particularly for the RFID applications which require conjugate chip matching. This paper presents a compact, center tap-fed NMHA designed to achieve a controlled impedance transformation using a direct tap-feed structure by only controlling the tap dimensions. Unlike lumped matching networks or geometric modification of the helical radiator, the tap-feed approach enables systematic adjustment of the antenna input impedance while preserving the intrinsic electromagnetic behavior of the NMHA. By appropriately selecting the tap position along the helical element, the antenna input impedance can be matched to a $50 \Omega$ reference, allowing stable characterization and evaluation of near-metal performance. Full-wave simulation results demonstrate stable impedance behaviour and maintain magnetic near-field characteristics when the NMHA is placed near metal surfaces. The proposed tap-fed NMHA establishes a robust and scalable baseline for subsequent RFID near-metal studies, where the tap matching impedance can be adjusted to match with the RFID chip impedance in the future work.

title: A Hybrid Image Encryption Scheme with Wavelet Transform and Chaotic Permutation For Enhanced Security
authors: Ahwar Khan, Malik Fuad Wali Khan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: With the emergence of quantum computing, conventional cryptographic mechanisms are under serious threat. Therefore, there is a need to design more secure alternatives. We propose a hybrid image encryption scheme that is resistant to quantum attacks. Our design combines the Kyber512 key-encapsulation mechanism for securely exchanging keys, and Ascon-128 authenticated encryption for efficient symmetric encryption. For stronger security, we used the discrete wavelet transform (DWT) to decompose the image into frequency subbands and a 4D coupled logistic map to shuffle the approximation sub-band, achieving strong, quantitatively validated diffusion and confusion properties. We analyzed the scheme using extensive security evaluations, including histogram analysis, pixel correlation, information entropy, resistance against differential attacks (NPCR and UACI), structural similarity index (SSIM), and key sensitivity tests. Experimental results show that our approach successfully randomizes pixel values, decorrelates, attains near-ideal entropy ($\approx 7.999$), demonstrates high robustness against differential (NPCR $\approx 99.6 \%$ and UACI $\approx 33.5$, statistical and brute-force attacks, maintains key sensitivity, and provides strong SSIM recovery upon decryption ($\gt0.997$). Compared with recent methods, the proposed scheme achieves higher or competitive entropy and lower pixel correlation. Formal analysis confirms security under established cryptographic assumptions, and the overall complexity is linear (O(MN))) in terms of image size. The proposed design offers a practical and scalable solution for postquantum secure image transmission in sensitive domains, such as healthcare, defense, and surveillance.

title: Resilient Neural Control for Grid-Forming Microgrids: An Integrated AI-Based Approach for Virtual-Impedance Scheduling and Cyber-Attack Mitigation
authors: S. M. Talha Zaidi, Uzair Asif, Arslan Munir, Mohammad B. Shadmand, Sudip K Mazumder
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Microgrids are playing an increasingly important role in the shift toward decentralized energy systems, yet they continue to face stability and reliability challenges under fast-changing operating conditions and rising cyber threats. In this work, we develop an integrated neural control framework for grid-forming inverters (GFMIs) that enables rapid, inverter-level detection and mitigation of cyber-attacks. Each GFMI is equipped with a lightweight bidirectional long short-term memory (Bi-LSTM) network that learns the typical time-based patterns of its local power and voltage signals, enabling accurate prediction of expected virtual impedance (Z<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">v</inf>) values. A centralized residual network (ResNet) is included to generate Z<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">v</inf> references under nominal conditions, providing coordinated power-sharing support. The locally predicted Z<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">v</inf> values are then continuously monitored against the incoming Z<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">v</inf> reference commands. If the difference exceeds the threshold, the primary controller immediately switches to its locally predicted value to maintain system stability and mitigate the impact of the attack. Testing on a modified IEEE 9-bus microgrid shows that the proposed method maintains critical parameters within operational bounds and delivers fast recovery from fixed, random varying, and gradually increasing Z<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">v</inf>-targeted attacks, while supporting decentralized decision making to enhance cyber-resilience and scalability for future smart grids.

title: Investigating Label Bias and Representational Sources of Age-Related Disparities in Medical Segmentation
authors: Aditya Parikh, Sneha Das, Aasa Feragen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Algorithmic bias in medical imaging can perpetuate health disparities, yet its causes remain poorly understood in segmentation tasks. While fairness has been extensively studied in classification, segmentation remains underexplored despite its clinical importance. In breast cancer segmentation, models exhibit significant performance disparities against younger patients, commonly attributed to physiological differences in breast density. We audit the MAMA-MIA dataset, establishing a quantitative baseline of age-related bias in its automated labels, and reveal a critical Biased Ruler effect where systematically flawed labels for validation misrepresent a model's actual bias. However, whether this bias originates from lower-quality annotations (label bias) or from fundamentally more challenging image characteristics remains unclear. Through controlled experiments, we systematically refute hypotheses that the bias stems from label quality sensitivity or quantitative case difficulty imbalance. Balancing training data by difficulty fails to mitigate the disparity, revealing that younger patient cases are intrinsically harder to learn. We provide direct evidence that systemic bias is learned and amplified when training on biased, machine-generated labels, a critical finding for automated annotation pipelines. This work introduces a systematic framework for diagnosing algorithmic bias in medical segmentation and demonstrates that achieving fairness requires addressing qualitative distributional differences rather than merely balancing case counts.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>Code for the experimental framework and evaluation is made available at https://github.com/ADE-17/bias-audit-isbi2026

title: Synthetic Volumetric Data Generation Enables Zero-Shot Generalization of Foundation Models in 3D Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Satrajit Chakrabarty, Sourya Sengupta, Gopal Avinash, Ravi Soni
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Foundation models such as Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM 2) exhibit strong generalization on natural images and videos but perform poorly on medical data due to differences in appearance statistics, imaging physics, and three-dimensional structure. To address this gap, we introduce SynthFM-3D, an analytical framework that mathematically models 3D variability in anatomy, contrast, boundary definition, and noise to generate synthetic data for training promptable segmentation models without real annotations. We fine-tuned SAM 2 on 10,000 SynthFM-3D volumes and evaluated it on eleven anatomical structures across three medical imaging modalities (CT, MR, ultrasound) from five public datasets. SynthFM-3D training led to consistent and statistically significant Dice score improvements over the pretrained SAM 2 baseline, demonstrating stronger zero-shot generalization across modalities. When compared with the supervised SAM-Med3D model on unseen cardiac ultrasound data, SynthFM-3D achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-3 \times$</tex> higher Dice scores, establishing analytical 3D data modeling as an effective pathway to modality-agnostic medical segmentation.

title: Optimization Framework for Hard-Switching Transients of Power Semiconductor Devices to Minimize Switching Losses and EMC-Relevant Noise Emissions
authors: Jochen Streit, Robert Kragl, Manuel Riefer, Hadiuzzaman Syed, Karl Oberdieck, Ingmar Kallfass
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization framework for hard-switching transients of power semiconductor devices that simultaneously minimizes switching losses and electromagnetic emissions (EME) while respecting component constraints. The framework integrates a state-space simulation environment with lab-verified SiC MOSFET models, a genetic algorithm-based optimizer providing quasi-continuous gate current control through variable resistance arrays, and a novel cost function incorporating the Heisenberg-Gabor inequality for broadband EME quantification. Unlike existing approaches focusing on sequential parameter adjustment or single-frequency ringing reduction, this framework enables systematic analysis of loss-EME trade-offs with high temporal resolution. The framework reveals that optimized gate current profiles exhibit bell-shaped pulses achieving sigmoidal voltage transitions, establishing a foundation for future work, including gate loop design and power semiconductor device optimization.

title: A High-Power Compact Capacitive WPT System for EV Charging
authors: Raquel Sarabia-Soto, Dheeraj Etta, Syed Saeed Rashid, Khurram K. Afridi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Large airgap capacitive wireless power transfer (WPT) systems are increasingly achieving higher power levels, bringing their use in electric vehicle (EV) charging closer to reality. However, the high-power systems demonstrated so far are large and not suitable for deployment in EV charging applications, whether stationary or dynamic. This digest presents a compact, lightweight capacitive WPT system in which the power electronics have been reduced in size and co-packaged with the charging pad, enabling seamless integration with the EV underbody and the road. The system’s compact design is achieved by optimizing the design of the matching network and power combining components to reduce their size. These inductors and transformers are realized using optimal toroidal geometries with innovative interleaved winding designs that enable size reduction while retaining high quality factor. This approach to miniaturization also reduces electromagnetic interference without compromising efficiency. Additionally, a lightweight charging pad designed with optimized dielectric profile contributes to lowering the overall system thickness, weight, and cost. A 10-kW 6.78-MHz 12-cm airgap compact capacitive WPT system with a power transfer density greater than 150 kW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is developed. An experimental prototype based on this architecture is built and tested up to 7.1 kW, obtaining a peak efficiency of 82%.

title: Evidence-Grounded Temporal Reasoning in Operating Room Long Video Understanding
authors: Yaoqian Li, Jinpeng Li, Pheng-Ann Heng
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Operating rooms (OR) are complex, high-risk environments that demand long-horizon video understanding for intelligent surgical assistance and quality assurance. Existing domainspecific OR models and datasets mainly address short-clip or frame-level analysis, lacking the ability to reason across extended temporal contexts. In contrast, general long-video multimodal large language models (MLLMs) exhibit strong temporal reasoning but fail to capture the structured interactions and clinical semantics unique to surgical workflows. To bridge this gap, we introduce OR-LongVQA, a benchmark for long-video understanding in operating rooms, covering diverse factors and causality-centered questions. We further propose a reasoning retrieval framework based on temporal scene graph and evidence-grounded reasoning modules that retrieve relevant subgraphs and perform interpretable step-by-step inference. We compare the proposed reasoning framework with existing state-of-the-art methods on the OR-LongVQA benchmark. Our approach outperforms prior methods by achieving significant improvements in longhorizon reasoning accuracy and interpretability, demonstrating its effectiveness and reliability for clinically-oriented, structured operating room video understanding.

title: Inverse Design of Microring Resonator-Based Glucose Biosensor Using AI Diffusion Models
authors: Yasaman Torabi, Amirali Ekhteraei, Bardia Baraeinejad
abstract: Glucose detection is crucial for diabetes diagnostics. Microring resonator biosensors detect refractive index changes. We use artificial intelligence (AI) diffusion models for inverse design of the sensor geometries consistent with target spectral responses, offering potential for intelligent biosensor development.

title: A 6.78-MHz 15-cm-Air-Gap 2-kW Optimally Soft-Switched Modular Capacitive Wireless Power Transfer System for EV Charging
authors: Ayush Dixit, Asif Mushtaq Bhat, Sreyam Sinha
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an optimally soft-switched modular capacitive wireless power transfer (CWPT) system for electric vehicle (EV) charging. Multi-MHz CWPT systems must operate with zero-voltage switching (ZVS) of their inverter and rectifier transistors to avoid excessive losses and thermal instability. Two different techniques to achieve ZVS, one with an additional series inductor and the other with an additional shunt inductor, are considered. For both these techniques, the required additional inductance and dead time between switching transitions are determined optimally so as to achieve ZVS of the inverter transistors with minimized reactive power in all the modules. The efficiency implications of both the approaches are formulated, and it is shown that while efficiency remains nearly constant with varying number of modules, the shunt inductor approach consistently provides a higher efficiency. A 6.78-MHz, 2-kW, 15-cm air-gap two-module CWPT prototype is designed using the shunt inductor approach, and the desired soft switching is shown to be achieved.

title: Accelerated Reconstruction for Sparse-View CT with Angular Sinogram Blurring Arising in Fast Continuous Rotation CT Scanners Using Dykstra-Like Splitting
authors: Donghui Li, Hiroyuki Kudo, Wataru Yashiro
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Continuous-exposure X-ray imaging accelerates data acquisition by avoiding rapid source or detector switching, but both continuous exposure and fast rotation introduce angular-integration effects due to exposure characteristics and detector response limits. These effects cause severe blurring in reconstructed images or videos, and similar artifacts also arise in modern CT systems when scan speed increases for dynamic or real-time imaging. Conventional least-squares-based reconstruction methods struggle to handle sparse-view data and angular blurring simultaneously. To address this, we propose a reconstruction model within a Dykstra-like splitting framework, incorporating total variation (TV) regularization to suppress blur from continuous exposure, rapid rotation, and sparse-view conditions while accelerating convergence. By embedding a blur-compensation mechanism into the system matrix, the method effectively reduces angular-integration artifacts and preserves resolution and contrast at high scan speeds. It shows strong potential for high-speed, irreversible imaging in physics, chemistry, and materials science.

title: Digital Twin Approach for the Stator Winding Interturn fault Detection of Alternator
authors: Makarand Sudhakar Ballal, Bhimrao Sitaram Umare, Hiralal Murlidhar Suryawanshi, Rashmi Singh, Tushar G Vilhekar, Kanchan P. Ghule
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper concentrates on the approach of digital twins in alternator stator winding Interturn fault (SWIF) diagnosis. Initially, a mathematical relationship was established linking alternator dynamics to stator winding interturn faults (SWIF). A real-time 10 kW, 400 V, 50 Hz alternator model was developed in MATLAB Simulink, with a corresponding Digital Twin in ANSYS Maxwell finite element tool is considered for analysis. The models were dynamically coupled via co-simulation. SWIF detection was achieved through negative sequence current analysis, with ANSYS simulations confirming change in magnetic flux distribution proportional to fault severity. This integrated Digital Twin approach demonstrated accurate, real-time fault detection and severity assessment, validating its effectiveness for predictive maintenance and operational reliability enhancement in alternator systems under varying conditions.

title: A Multimodal Cross-Attention Pathotranscriptome Integration for Enhanced Survival Prediction of Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma
authors: Kountay Dwivedi, Amirreza Mahbod, Rupert C. Ecker, Klara Janjić
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) accounts for a major part of cancer mortality, with survival outcomes highly dependent on early diagnosis. While many approaches have been proposed for OSCC survival prediction, they often rely on unimodal data, which may be suboptimal. In this study, we introduced a unified cross-attention-based deep learning framework that integrates whole-slide histopathology images (WSIs) and transcriptomic data from OSCC patients for survival prediction. The framework employed an autoencoder for transcriptomic feature extraction and a state-of-the-art pathology foundation model-evaluated across five alternatives-to derive WSI embeddings. These embeddings were subsequently integrated using cross-attention and concatenation within a Cox proportional hazards model. The multimodal approach outperformed nearly all unimodal counterparts, achieving a maximum concordance index of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.780 \pm 0.059$</tex> with cross-attention and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.766 \pm 0.050$</tex> with concatenation. The results indicate that pathotranscriptomic integration could improve survival prediction for OSCC patients. The implementation is available on GitHub at: https://github.com/kountaydwivedi/multimodal_fusion.git.

title: Extended Zero Turn-Off Loss (ZTL) Region for SiC MOSFETs by Gate Voltage Boosting
authors: Morten Rahr Nielsen, Luke Shillaber, Ameer Janabi, Stig Munk-Nielsen, Teng Long
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Zero turn-off loss (ZTL) or near-ZTL has gained increasing interest in zero voltage switching (ZVS) topologies to minimize switching losses, as both turn-on and turn-off switching losses approach zero. ZTL can be obtained at low switching currents under ZTL switching conditions as the switching current solely charges the SiC MOSFET output capacitance, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">oss</inf>, and does not generate any joule heating in the SiC MOSFET during the turn-off switching event. Extending the ZTL switching conditions to higher switching currents can be achieved by gate voltage boosting during the turn-off switching event to obtain faster switching speeds by increasing the SiC MOSFET gate current. This paper validates, through LTspice simulations and preliminary experimental results, an extended ZTL region for SiC MOSFETs with the use of gate voltage boosting. The preliminary experimental results obtained through double pulse testing show a reduction of 10-15% in the turn-off energy under non-ideal boosting voltage.

title: Machine Learning–Based Surrogate Models for Knowledge Discovery in Multi-Stage Engineering Simulations
authors: Nischal Kumar. S, S. Elangovan, Subashini. N.J
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Analytical and empirical models are commonly employed to describe multi-stage engineering processes involving thermal, kinetic, and contact phenomena. In earlier studies, such processes were analyzed exclusively using physics-based formulations, where repeated analytical or numerical evaluations were required for parametric analysis and design exploration. No machine learning models were previously used to approximate or accelerate the simulator outputs. The present work introduces machine learning-based surrogate models developed as a continuation of a validated multi-stage engineering simulator. The underlying mathematical formulations and computational structure of the simulator are retained without modification and are treated as a deterministic knowledge source. Synthetic datasets are generated by systematically varying physically meaningful input parameters across three sequential stages: transient thermal response, diffusion-controlled coating thickness growth, and empirical contact time prediction. Stage-wise regression models are independently trained for each output variable. Multiple learning algorithms are evaluated, among which Random Forest regression consistently demonstrates superior performance across all stages. For the transient thermal stage, coefficients of determination of 0.9979 and 0.9984 are achieved for the series and lumped temperature predictions, respectively. The coating thickness surrogate attains an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value of 0.9836, while the contact time prediction achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 7 9 4}$</tex>, with low associated prediction errors. The results confirm that machine learning surrogates can accurately emulate physics-based simulator behavior while significantly reducing computational effort. The proposed framework enables rapid knowledge discovery and efficient analysis of multi-stage engineering systems, supporting effective integration of mechanical modelling with data-driven intelligence.

title: Design and Implementation of a KNX-Based Smart Home Training and Educational System
authors: Aya Alsharif, Mohammad Kouali, Jenan Abu Dayyah, Ahmed Abdou, Mohanad Rezeq, Mohammad Omar Sabri
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: KNX is one of the most commonly used and accepted home/building automation standards. However, it is not widely used in practical engineering and vocational training. This paper introduces the design, implementation, and assessment of a KNX-based smart home training and educational system that aims to bridge the gap between academic education and professional commissioning practices. The platform proposed unites KNX-certified hardware, professional configuration of ETS, structured addressing, and group communication plans, modern humanmachine interfaces, such as touch panels and voice control via a 1 Home server and Amazon Alexa. An apartment-scale prototype of the module was created to simulate real-life automation (residential) conditions, such as lighting, dimming, shading, socket control, and sensor-based automation. The system was implemented in a one-week training program and tested using a quasi-experimental pre-post design, with 18 participants. The experiments' outcomes indicate significant improvements in theoretical knowledge and real-world KNX commissioning skills. The suggested system offers a training platform that is reproducible, curriculum-ready, aligned with industry standards, and applicable to engineering education.

title: Progidiff: Prompt-Guided Diffusion-Based Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Yuan Lin, Murong Xu, Marc Hölle, Chinmay Prabhakar, Andreas Maier, Vasileios Belagiannis, Bjoern Menze, Suprosanna Shit
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Widely adopted medical image segmentation methods, although efficient, are primarily deterministic and remain poorly amenable to natural language prompts. Thus, they lack the capability to estimate multiple proposals, human interaction, and cross-modality adaption. Recently, text-toimage diffusion models have shown potential to bridge the gap. However, training them from scratch requires a large dataset-a limitation for medical image segmentation. Furthermore, they are often limited to binary segmentation and cannot be conditioned on a natural language prompt. To this end, we propose a novel framework called ProGiDiff that leverages existing image generation models for medical image segmentation purposes. Specifically, we propose a ControlNet-style conditioning mechanism with a custom encoder, suitable for image conditioning, to steer a pre-trained diffusion model to output segmentation masks. It naturally extends to a multi-class setting simply by prompting the target organ. Our experiment on organ segmentation from CT images demonstrates strong performance compared to previous methods and could greatly benefit from an expert-in-theloop setting to leverage multiple proposals. Importantly, we demonstrate that the learned conditioning mechanism can be easily transferred through low-rank, few-shot adaptation to segment MR images.

title: LLM-Powered SSH Honeypot with Behavioral Analysis and Real-Time ML-Based Attack Classification
authors: S. Metilda Florence, Abhinav U, Rashmika S
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Hackers still use Secure Shell (SSH) services to get into systems a lot since they are common and may be located on public networks. A lot of how classic intrusion detection systems work is by looking at signatures or the network as a whole. But these strategies don't usually work after an interactive session is put up to figure out what the attacker wants to do. This paper describes a behavior-driven strategy for looking at SSH assaults using honeypot log data, with an emphasis on extracting features at the session level and showing them in real time. We gather logs of interactions from a Cowrie SSH honeypot and turn them into sessions for hackers. After that, we turn them into high-dimensional behavioral feature vectors that alter based on time, language, and purpose. A real-time dashboard has these capabilities and can tell the difference between attacks that are done by bots and attacks that are done by people. The suggested approach puts a lot of attention on being modular, easy to comprehend, and easy to see how it operates. The article also discusses a proposed machine learning enhancement that would utilize labeled attack datasets to autonomously categorize attacker behavior based on identified characteristics.

title: High-Quality Synthetic Annotated Tissue Data Using Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Siddharth Srivastava, Cornelis Jan Weijer, Till Bretschneider
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We present a deep-learning based pipeline for generating high-quality synthetic instance-segmentation datasets of tissues undergoing early gastrulation in chick embryo. We create point-clouds using the Lennard-Jones potential and learn an image-to-image translation from these point-clouds to create synthetic tissue images and auxiliary flow tensors using a Generative Adversarial Network, from which the segmentation masks are derived. We evaluate the downstream utility of our synthetic data by training Cellpose and Stardist models from scratch under data replacement and data augmentation scenarios, show that the synthetic datasets effectively capture the statistical properties of the real dataset, and show that our synthetic data improves segmentation performance on a held-out test set. This approach substantially reduces expert annotation time, as late-stage gastrulation data are challenging to acquire and manually label, while similar synthetic examples can be flexibly generated from easily obtainable and annotated early-stage data. Code: https://gitlab.com/siddharthsrivastava/synthetic-tissue-data

title: Advanced Multi-Layer Framework for Distinguishing Human-Written and AI-Generated Arabic Text: A Transformer-Based Approach with Linguistic Feature Engineering
authors: Yazeed Alsarhan, Farah Abo Asba, Ayoub Alsarhan, Suhaila Abuowaida
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The emergence of a number of large Arabic language models, such as AraGPT2, Jais, CAMeLBERT, and several other versions of Arabic-optimized GPTs, has revolutionized the way in which machine-generated text is created in Arabic and has achieved new benchmarks in terms of fluency, coherence, and semantic richness. However, this represents a significant challenge for the verification of academic integrity, journalistic authenticity, and cybersecurity applications because the differences between machine-generated Arabic text and human-authored Arabic text are becoming increasingly difficult to discern. This study proposes an integrated multi-layer detection system using a combination of stylistic, statistical, morpho-syntactic and transformer-based methods to reliably identify whether Arabic text was written by humans or machines. Using a carefully compiled dataset of 26,998 samples consisting of authentic human-written texts from a variety of sources, combined with the output of state-of-theart Arabic language models, our research team identified more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5}$</tex> linguistic features, including various lexical diversity metrics, entropy measures, perplexity estimates and parts-of-speech (POS) distributions. The results of our experiments demonstrate that fine-tuned transformer architectures, specifically AraBERT, were able to identify and distinguish between human and AIgenerated Arabic texts at an average rate of 94.8% and therefore significantly outperformed traditional machine learning baseline algorithms by approximately 10%.

title: Residual-Aware Reinforcement Learning with Bayesian Transformer for Probabilistic Anomaly Detection in PV Systems
authors: Apoorva Choumal, M. Rizwan, Shatakshi Jha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Reliable detection of operational anomalies in large-scale photovoltaic plants is constrained by unavailability of labeled data data, non-stationary ramp-rate dynamics and the need for well-calibrated uncertainty estimates. This research proposes an end-to-end framework that couples residual-driven anomaly exploration with uncertainty-aware prediction to deliver probabilistic anomaly score. A ten-year, 5-min resolution dataset from 1058 kW Dessert garden, Australia solar farm is used to access the proposed framework. A deterministic gradient-boosting model is trained to estimate the photovoltaic power output using meteorological parameters. The normalized prediction residual is employed as an unsupervised anomaly indicator. Further, the anomaly identification is formulated as a sequential decision-making problem which is addressed using reinforcement learning agent. At each step the agent selects the time window and sensor subset expected to maximize residual deviation, thereby concentrating computational effort on high-risk segments. The selected sequences are processed by a Transformer encode whose decoder is trained as a Bayesian neural network via Monte-Carlo dropout. The model outputs both point prediction and epistemic variance, enabling closed-form computation of anomaly probability from prediction errors. The proposed framework attains a CRPS of 0.041 with 70% faster inference and apporx. 33% lower false alarm rate hence providing an accurate and operational viable methodology for real-time PV anomaly detection.

title: Hybrid CNN-SVM Framework for Automated Depression Detection Using Speech Analysis
authors: Vishnu Nair, Khushal Rathi, Aakash Sarkar, Vikram Kulkarni, Raminder Kaur Nagra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid CNN-SVM model that is used to detect depression based on speech using the DAIC-WOZ dataset (Opensource). All audio files were processed using Librosa, SciPy and Parselmouth to remove noise and normalize audio and extract acoustic features (including some MFCCs, spectral, prosodic and energy-based acoustic features). After data preprocessing, the CNN was trained to learn depth spectral representations and SVM was trained to classify the embeddings to improve the performance of generalization. The model achieved a rate of accuracy of 69.09%, which is a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 \%-8 \%$</tex> better result than the current methods. In general, the analysis finds a value in deep learning with machine learning in the creation of noninvasive, reliable, and scalable methods of detecting depression early.

title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation with Target-Only Margin Disparity Discrepancy
authors: Gauthier Miralles, Loïc Le Folgoc, Vincent Jugnon, Pietro Gori
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: In interventional radiology, Cone-Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) is a helpful imaging modality that provides guidance to practicians during minimally invasive procedures. CBCT differs from traditional Computed Tomography (CT) due to its limited reconstructed field of view, specific artefacts, and the intra-arterial administration of contrast medium. While CT benefits from abundant publicly available annotated datasets, interventional CBCT data remain scarce and largely unannotated, with existing datasets focused primarily on radiotherapy applications. To address this limitation, we leverage a proprietary collection of unannotated interventional CBCT scans in conjunction with annotated CT data, employing domain adaptation techniques to bridge the modality gap and enhance liver segmentation performance on CBCT. We propose a novel unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) framework based on the formalism of Margin Disparity Discrepancy (MDD), which improves target domain performance through a reformulation of the original MDD optimization framework. Experimental results on CT and CBCT datasets for liver segmentation demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in UDA, as well as in the few-shot setting.

title: Sample-Based Training Data for Effective 3D Cell Segmentation
authors: Adam Smith, Till Bretschneider
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Deep learning remains the leading choice for cell segmentation, a critical step in bioimaging analysis. Whilst deep learning models provide excellent segmentation accuracy, a large number of hand-annotated samples are required which are scarce in 3D. We propose a method for generating synthetic data for training deep learning models to augment or replace such datasets. By preserving key features in real data, we show that a standard UNet trained on synthetic data can segment single motile cells with branching filopodia with high accuracy. We also demonstrate how low-effort slice annotations can sufficiently replace volume annotations in our data generation pipeline. Overall, we provide a simple alternative to annotating large 3D datasets for training neural networks to segment cell imaging data.

title: Wirelessly Powering a Cardiac Pacemaker with a Body-Coupled TENG for Self-Powered Operation and Sensing
authors: Bowang Zhang, Xin Xia, Yunlong Zi, Ping Wang, Wei Han
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a self-powered cardiac pacemaker platform that combines body-coupled radiative triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG) wireless power transfer with passive transient parity–time (PT) symmetric sensing. A sliding freestanding triboelectric-layer (SFT) mode TENG with an integrated breakdown discharger harvests biomechanical motion as high-voltage corona discharges, modeled as an initially energized RLC resonator that acts as an antenna-like source. By optimizing the carrier to 40 MHz and leveraging human-body coupling, the radiative link achieves higher efficiency than traditional near-field wireless links. Experiments show that body-coupled transfer provides 4.3 times more energy than air-path transmission at the same distance. The harvested energy is rectified and stored, charging 4.7µF and 10µF capacitors to 17V and 7V within 13 seconds, supporting pacemaker-level power. For monitoring, the pacemaker electrodes and intracardiac blood form a variable capacitor to create an internal sensor resonator, which is inductively coupled to an external reader resonator. This coupled tank forms a transient PT-symmetric system near an exceptional point (EP). FFT analysis of the reader voltage tracks eigenfrequency coalescence and splitting, enabling battery-free detection of small capacitance changes linked to rhythm abnormalities. The proposed architecture integrates body-coupled wireless power with exceptional-point-enhanced passive sensing in a single implantable platform.

title: Identification and Assessment of Buildings Approaching the River Canals: An Integrated Geospatial Method for Conservation of Waterway Preservation
authors: Hany A. Elsalamony, Ayman Mohamed, Saeed Hamouda, Nevien Moawad, Ayman Elshenawy, Amr A. Hassanain
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The growing international problem of illegal construction and urban encroachment on central water infrastructure, such as river canals, has significant environmental and structural implications. This paper presents a reliable and integrated Deep Learning (DL) geospatial approach to accurately identify waterway borders in terms of detection, monitoring, and prioritization, as well as in addressing their potential negative environmental impacts, such as disrupting natural flow, increasing flood vulnerability, and harming aquatic ecosystems. The proposed approach integrates high-resolution remote sensing imagery, GIS, and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for precise building identification. Successive proximity and risk-based evaluation systems are used via a projected Coordinate Reference System (CRS) to profile the encroachments. A more detailed case study of one such important waterway network in Egypt the CNN achieved strong detection accuracy (F1-score: 0.92, IoU: 0.855), enabling reliable identification of using our method revealed many buildings located inside the crucial 30 meter buffer area. Concentrated analysis of 500 detected structures whose risk was based on a close distance (10 -meter radius) to a household identified as high-risk, in which actions needed to be taken immediately. Quantitative risk classification of buildings into High-Risk <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\leqslant 5 \mathrm{m})$</tex>, Medium-Risk <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5-10 \mathrm{m})$</tex>, and Low-Risk <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(10-30 \mathrm{m})$</tex> allows for prioritization in low-cost enhanced mitigation options, e.g., removal or planned relocation. This paper provides a transferable, evidence-based tool for municipal authorities and water boards to further boost their operational capacity in the maintenance of the integrity of waterscapes, which should evoke sustainable development along canal corridors.

title: Power Electronic Development Challenges for High Power Density Integrated Electric Drive Applications
authors: Shajjad Chowdhury, Himel Barua, Vandana Rallabandi, Jon Wilkins, Gui-Jia Su, Pedro Ribeiro, Brian Rowden, Burak Ozpineci, Bidzina Kekelia, Sreekant Narumanchi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The ever-increasing demand for compact, efficient, and high-performance traction drive systems for transportation applications has accelerated the development of integrated electric drives. In these systems, the electric machine and inverter are integrated within a single housing, offering significant advantages in electrical performance, volume, weight, cost, and overall system efficiency. Despite these benefits, such high levels of integration introduce a new set of challenges, particularly in power electronic design and component selection. This article presents an in-depth investigation of a highly integrated electric drive architecture with an internal stator-mounted inverter, highlighting key design considerations and trade-offs aimed exclusively at maximizing system power density.Based on a comprehensive review of the literature, the power density of a voltage-source-inverter–driven electric drive is primarily governed by the volumetric contributions of the power modules, heat sinks, and DC-link capacitors. Accordingly, this work focuses on the optimization of these three critical components to enhance the inverter’s overall power density. The proposed design achieves a power density of 100 kW/L, demonstrating the effectiveness of the presented approach for next-generation integrated electric drive systems.

title: Single-Step Dead Time Optimization Based on Gate-Source Voltage Sensing
authors: Hongming Zhao, Krishang Agarwal, Joachim Joos, Valentijn De Smedt
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel one-step dead time optimization method utilizing real-time detection of gate-source voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf>) signals. Unlike conventional adaptive methods that rely on additional high-voltage (HV) components or propagation-delayed feedback, this approach exploits the crosstalk ripples on the synchronous switch to detect the switching states of the complementary switch. The proposed closed-loop system determines the near-optimal dead time within a single switching cycle, eliminating the need for iterative convergence while significantly reducing sensing complexity. The method is experimentally validated on a 48V-to-12V synchronous buck converter operating at 100kHz using SiC MOSFETs. Experimental results demonstrate an efficiency improvement of up to 3.9% across a wide load current range, compared to a fixed 500ns dead time setting.

title: A Novel 3D Attention-Gated Fully Convolutional Network and Adaptive Optimization for Coronary Artery Segmentation
authors: Satvik Sharma, Pranav Kulkarni
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Accurate coronary artery segmentation from computed tomography angiography (CTA) is essential for quantitative cardiovascular assessment and has been a field of heavy interest in recent times. Deep learning has shown promise in automating this task, but many state-of-the-art methods are memory-intensive or rely on multi-stage pipelines, limiting clinical feasibility. In this work, we address this gap by proposing a novel 3D fully convolutional network with attention gates (FCNAG) and optimized training strategies for automated coronary artery segmentation in high resolution. The model operates on full-resolution CTA volumes, integrating multiple attention gatebased vessel enhancements, memory-efficient gradient checkpointing, and adaptive optimization with Adam and a ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler to improve convergence and preserve fine anatomical detail under hardware constraints. Experiments were conducted on the public ImageCAS dataset using 4-fold cross-validation. The proposed FCN-AG model achieves a Dice Score Coefficient of 0.8492, Hausdorff Distance of 23.67 mm, and Average Hausdorff Distance of $\mathbf{0. 6 2 3 4 ~ m m}$, demonstrating strong performance among FCN-based methods while requiring substantially less memory than other state-of-the-art approaches. These findings demonstrate that high-resolution coronary artery segmentation can be achieved with a memory-efficient architecture, supporting scalable usage in clinical workflows.

title: Battery Supercapacitor HESS for Sudden Power Delivery in Smartphone Circuits and Fast Charging
authors: Kavishka Dissanayake, Dulsha Kularatna-Abeywardana, Nitish Patel, Nihal Kularatna
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Modern smartphones face high power demands during short bursts of activity like switching on, Bluetooth transfers, Wi-Fi scanning, refreshing, scrolling, and camera flash use, which can strain lithium-ion batteries dramatically. This study introduces a hybrid energy storage system that combines a rechargeable battery and a supercapacitor. The swapping supercapacitor converter is charged by a smartphone charger, which can perform fast charging. This supercapacitor topology is used to handle rapid current requirements to the device. This system can increase efficiency in fast charging and reliability, offering a promising solution for next-generation smartphone power management.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Attention Mechanisms with EfficientNet-B2 for Deepfake Image Detection
authors: Aani Joy D, Thiyagarajan P, Selvakumar K
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Deepfake image generation techniques are generating images that seem to be real Due to this, the existing forensic detection systems are facing difficulties in detection. Attention mechanisms are integrated into convolutional neural networks to improve detection, but their effectiveness for deepfake image detection has not been studied. This paper presents a comparison of 20 attention mechanisms integrated into the EfficientNet-B2 backbone for binary classification. The evaluated mechanisms include channel-based, spatial-based, frequency-aware, coordinate-based, and globalcontext attention designs, with both exact implementations and carefully adapted variants. All models are trained and evaluated on the same large-scale dataset using the same preprocessing, optimization, and evaluation protocols. Results indicate that the architectural and functional attention mechanisms consistently outperform other attentions. Also lightweight channel attention modules offer competitive performance with reduced computational cost. The study provides a detailed understanding of attention behavior for image-based deepfake detection, which will be helpful for future research.

title: Home Location Leakage via Weather-Related Social Media Posts
authors: Akitaka Yamashita, Keishi Tajima
publish: Proceedings of the 18th ACM Web Science Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Causal Effects of Trigger Words in Social Media Discussions: A Large-Scale Case Study about UK Politics on Reddit
authors: Dimosthenis Antypas, Christian Arnold, Jose Camacho-Collados, Nedjma Ousidhoum, Carla Perez-Almendros
publish: Proceedings of the 18th ACM Web Science Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Decision Support System for Wheat Diseases Resistance Breeding Based on FAIR Principles
authors: Ayman Mohamed, Nevien Moawad, Reda Ibrahim Omara, Rasha Elnemr
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Wheat rust diseases pose severe threats to global wheat production, particularly stripe (yellow) rust (Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici), which can cause yield losses of up to 70%. In this study, 16 wheat genotypes were evaluated using the Wheat Decision Support System (WDSS). The WDSS integrates knowledge-driven decision support with FAIR-compliant data management to assess resistance against yellow, leaf, and stem rust. It provides interactive analysis of wheat varieties, rust races, and resistance gene pools through explicit rules. Curated datasets of varieties, resistance genes, and gene–race interactions were employed to validate the system. Results show that WDSS effectively distinguishes resistant and susceptible types, identifies efficient versus ineffective genes, and highlights promising genes and pyramid strategies for breeding. Compared to manual analysis, WDSS accelerates breeding recommendations and ensures consistent resistance assessment. This demonstrates its value as a straightforward methodology for evaluating rust resistance, enabling knowledge-driven breeding decisions, repeated testing of resistance scenarios, and real-time, high-efficiency strategies for durable resistance in wheat improvement programs. Data sharing is essential for advancing agricultural science and addressing global challenges. The FAIR principles are Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable which ensure datasets can be efficiently used by both humans and machines. However, implementing FAIR standards remains challenging, requiring careful attention to interoperability frameworks, access mechanisms, metadata quality, and overall data management. Strengthening these aspects will enhance collaborative research and support sustainable agricultural innovation.

title: Fusion-Based Approach For Accurate SoH Estimation of Lithium-Ion Batteries: A Review
authors: Amitava Dutta, Makarand S. Ballal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The Accurate estimation of State of Health (SoH) and Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries is critical for enhancing the safety, reliability, and performance of battery-powered systems, particularly in EVs and ESS applications. This study proposes a fusion-based diagnostics approach that integrates Coulomb Counting (CC) with Capacitance-based analysis to overcome the individual limitations of each method. CC, while widely adopted due to its simplicity and real-time applicability, on the other hand, Capacitance-based analysis provides valuable insights into electrochemical aging by correlating capacity fade with impedance or voltage response metrics. By combining these two techniques which compliments each-other, this proposed fusion methodology offers to enhance accuracy, robustness, and early fault detection capability across various aging modes and operating conditions. This review will examine the theoretical approach by understanding the foundations and comparative performance metrics of state-of-the-art fusion models. It also highlights key challenges, such as temperature dependencies, measurement noise, and data-driven model generalization, while outlining future research directions for real-time onboard implementation. The study aims to provide a consolidated knowledge base for researchers and engineers seeking advanced SoH and RUL estimation techniques for lithium-ion batteries.

title: A Constant Current Controller for a Single-Stage PFC Wireless Power Transfer Converter
authors: Saman Rezazade, Mohammed Agamy
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Series-series wireless power transfer (WPT) converters are used in a wide range of applications, such as electric vehicle chargers. Conventional WPT converters often suffer from low efficiency due to their multi-stage topology. A single-stage, power-factor-corrected WPT converter is proposed to combine the two stages and improve efficiency, while reducing the size, number of semiconductor components, and total harmonic distortion (THD). Unlike the conventional approach, which uses two separate controllers to regulate the input and output characteristics, the proposed topology uses a hybrid controller to regulate the dc-link voltage as well as the output dc current and voltage. A variable duty-cycle controller shapes the input current in continuous conduction mode (CCM), and the output dc current is maintained by variable switching-frequency control without an output LC filter. This controller provides variable gain by changing the duty ratio and switching frequency to keep a constant current (CC) for the load. This approach reduces control complexity and avoids adding a current controller on the load side. In addition, this topology significantly improves overall system efficiency by minimizing power losses, reducing the output filter size, and improving THD.

title: Reconstructing Patched or Partial Holograms to Allow for Whole Slide Imaging with a Self-Referencing Holographic Microscope
authors: Philip Groult, Julia D. Sistermanns, Ellen Emken, Oliver Hayden, Wolfgang Utschick
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The last decade has seen significant advances in computeraided diagnostics for cytological screening, mainly through the improvement and integration of scanning techniques such as whole slide imaging (WSI) and the combination with deep learning. Simultaneously, new imaging techniques such as quantitative phase imaging (QPI) are being developed to capture richer cell information with less sample preparation. So far, the two worlds of WSI and QPI have not been combined. In this work, we present a reconstruction algorithm which makes whole slide imaging of cervical smears possible by using a self-referencing three-wave digital holographic microscope. Since a WSI is constructed by combining multiple patches, the algorithm is adaptive and can be used on partial holograms and patched holograms. We present the algorithm for a single shot hologram, the adaptations to make it flexible to various inputs and show that the algorithm performs well for the tested epithelial cells.

title: Out-of-Plane Coupling of Expanded Beam Via Polymer Waveguides for CPO
authors: Jef Van Asch, Jeroen Missinne, Cheng-Jhih Luo, Joris Van Campenhout, Geert Van Steenberge
abstract: We demonstrate polymer-based ORDL-to-fiber coupling via expanded beam collimation. Combining a 45° micromirror and microlens, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> beam waist and 1.3° opening angle is achieved, resulting in 4 dB total insertion loss including mirror, microlens, fiber-to-ORDL butt coupling and ORDL propagation loss.

title: MEDLEY: Orchestrating Imperfect Segmentation Through Physics-Aware Multi-Tool Curation for Infant Brain MRI
authors: Isabelle A. T. Le, Jocelyne Bachevalier, Sarah R. Shultz, Vince Calhoun, Sergey M. Plis
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate infant brain MRI segmentation remains difficult, particularly during early infancy, when rapid myelination drives nonlinear changes in tissue contrast and pushes gray and white matter toward an emerging “isointense” regime on T1-weighted scans. Existing automatic segmentation tools exhibit predictable failure modes, and no single method performs reliably across all structures during early infancy. We present Medley, a physics-aware curation workflow that combines per-tissue T1 intensity enhancement, affine-based structural enlargement, and anatomy-informed rule-based label consolidation. Medley operates on the outputs of standard segmentation tools, correcting their systematic weaknesses rather than replacing them. We validate the workflow on a subset of T1-weighted scans from the Marcus Autism Center (manual QC) and on a subset of the BabyOpenBrains dataset (Dice and HD95), restricting analysis in this paper to infants aged 1-4 months. Within this 1-4-month subset, Medley improves the quality-control (QC) pass rate from 44% (best single tool) to 90%, increases mean Dice, and substantially reduces manual touch-up time. On the manually labeled 1-4-month BabyOpenBrains subset (17 anatomical labels), Medley achieves a macro Dice of 0.74 and a micro Dice of 0.84. This workflow produces curated labels that can facilitate downstream infant segmentation model development and enables more scalable pediatric neuroimaging studies.

title: Development and Realization of a Compact, Rugged and Highly Efficient Power Supply Unit for non-linear Pulsating Loads in High Power Radar applications
authors: Aabir Shabir Wani, Vijay Hiralal Bhosale, Nandakumar S, Suchith Rajagopal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Operation of a RADAR system requires DC power ranging from as low as a few kW to the order of MW to provide bias supply to a large number of Transmit Receive Modules (TRMs) for its desired operation. Since the readily available power is usually AC (available via State Electricity Boards or Diesel Generator Sets), it needs to be converted to DC power at the desired voltage level via power electronic converters for use by TRMs, maintaining power quality parameters for reliable operation. This is accomplished by a Power Supply Unit (PSU) which is based on DC-DC converter technology and is fed by a diode-based rectifier source at its input terminals. The developmental stages of this PSU, along with various challenges faced during the realization and testing stages, are presented in this paper. This PSU is modular in nature to allow easy scaling as per load power requirements. This PSU also offers various functional and protection features. It is a part of a transportable system; thus complies with various Military Standards (MIL-STDs) and can be operated in a wide range of temperatures and altitudes as per JSS55555 specifications. This PSU has been realized through an industry partner, qualified as per MIL-STDs, and has been successfully tested for its desired performance.

title: Overcoming Homophily Bias: Graph Anomaly Detection Based on Integrated Learning
authors: Fangjian Tao, Zhiyuan Liu, Zhi Li, Chunjie Cao, Jingzhang Sun
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Anomaly detection aims to identify patterns deviating from expected behaviors. While earlier approaches were grounded in shallow learning techniques, the advent of graph neural networks has greatly advanced graph anomaly detection (GAD). However, we observe that the GAD methods show a significant performance bias towards varying degrees of graph Homophily. we term this phenomenon as Homophily bias. The bias can lead to drastic performance fluctuations of GAD methods on the same dataset. Thus, a logical approach to counteract the Homophily bias is to harness the advantages of different methods. In this paper, we present Mul-GAD, a multi-view fusion approach based on integrated learning, to tackle the bias issue in GAD domain. This approach combines coarse-grained view fusion and fine-grained feature fusion. The former aims to harness the strength of various methods, while the latter focuses on minimizing redundancy features introduced by different methods and maximizing complementary features. As quantifying Homophily bias is challenging, we introduce graph generalizability as a novel concept to gauge its impact and formulate a quantifiable evaluation metric. We achieve the state-of-the-art detection performance and generalization in most scenarios via a series of experiments conducted on Pubmed, Amazon Computer, Amazon Photo, and Weibo. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/MulGraph-Fusion-A135.

title: Fault-Tolerant Control for Dual Three-Level Inverter Fed Five-Phase OEW-PMSM Drives Under Open-Circuit Faults
authors: Malla Mohan, Tejavathu Ramesh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines (PMSMs) are vital for numerous safety-critical applications that cannot afford operational disruptions. This paper introduces a robust control strategy designed to provide fault tolerance for Dual Three-Level Inverter (D-3LI) fed five-phase Open-End Winding PMSM (OEW-PMSM). The proposed scheme ensures that the five-phase OEW-PMSM can operate reliably even with up to two-phase failures, without the need for additional hardware reconfigurations. This capability is especially crucial in Electric Vehicle (EV) propulsion systems, where high reliability is essential. The proposed method is based on additional current cosine components added to the remaining healthy phases using current space vector diagrams. It ensures that the torque-producing magnetic motive force (MMF) remains constant under both healthy and faulty conditions. The Proposed closed-loop control employs Direct Torque Control with reduced carrier space vector modulation (DTC-RC-SVM). The simplified RC-SVM technique uses fewer carriers for generating pulses for the D-3LI. The effectiveness of the proposed fault-tolerant strategy was validated using a real-time simulator, OP5707, demonstrating that the motor maintains continuous and stable operation despite faults. The results highlight significant benefits, including reduced current and torque ripples, and improved fault-tolerant performance compared to Open-Circuit fault conditions.

title: A Scalability-Aware Industrial IoT Connectivity Framework for Adaptive Smart Data Acquisition in Cyber-Physical Manufacturing Systems
authors: M. Bharani, V. Parthipan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Smart factories are based on the abstract-cyber physical manufacturing systems, which closely combine physical industrial processes with digital intelligence, based on the constant collection of data and communication. The technologies of industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) are highly relevant to the facilitation of such integration, but due to the rapid increase of the number of connected industrial devices, there are major challenges that are associated with scalable connectivity, real-time functionality, and air-tight information gathering. To overcome the mentioned challenges, this paper suggests a scalability-conscious IIoT connectivity scheme of adaptive smart data acquisition within cyber-physical manufacturing systems. The proposed architecture collaborates in a top-down hierarchy between device, edge, and gateway and cloud layers and employs adaptive and connectivity conscious protocols that dynamically adjust the data acquisition and communication behavior based on system and network state. To achieve higher scalability and communication efficiency, distributed processing and adaptive communication are exploited and tested in a simulation based environment with varying device densities and traffic loads. There has been performance performance showing better end to end latency, throughput, ratio of delivered packets and scalability of the system than the traditional centralized IIoT connectivity architecture. These results suggest that the suggested solution is a valid and feasible solution to large scale smart manufacturing systems.

title: The Stories Behind the Incidents
authors: Lorin Hochstein
abstract: Reflections on hosting a conference track about software outage stories.

title: Movable Antenna-Enabled Integrated Sensing and Communication in Low-Altitude UAV Networks
authors: Bin Li, Pengcheng Rao, Xuedong Zhang, Xinyi Wang
abstract: This paper investigates a multiple uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system equipped with movable antenna (MA) arrays. To align with practical scenarios, we simulate the dynamic roaming of ground users and the three-dimensional deployment of UAVs in the airspace. We aim to maximize the total data rate by jointly optimizing key operational variables, including UAV trajectories, user association, antenna positions, and beamforming. This formulated problem is subject to constraints on transmission power and the sensing signal-to-noise ratio. To address the challenge of dynamically unknown state transitions due to user mobility, the original problem is decomposed into two steps and solved using different algorithms. First, we utilize the hierarchical density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (HDBSCAN) algorithm to address the ground-to-air association problem, periodically updating clusters and re-associating during training. The clustering hotspots are used to suggest flight directions for the UAVs. Second, we develop the soft actor-critic algorithm to solve the joint optimization problem of UAV trajectories, antenna positions, and beamforming. Experimental results demonstrate that UAVs equipped with MA arrays outperform those with traditional fixed antenna arrays in ISAC systems, and the proposed optimization strategy effectively enhances communication rates while ensuring sensing performance.

title: Estimation-Driven Decentralized Secondary Control of DC Microgrids with Multiple Grid-Forming Converters
authors: Chunlei Fu, Chenming Liu, Lantao Xing, Nima Tashakor, Jingyang Fang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The secondary control of DC microgrids can simultaneously achieve proper current sharing among DC grid-forming converters (GFMCs) and bus voltage restoration. To achieve these, most existing secondary controllers employ centralized or distributed architectures, where communication is inevitable. However, communication networks necessitate additional hardware and give rise to problems related to stability and security. In order to reduce cost and improve robustness, this article presents a decentralized secondary controller, which builds on the information of estimated line resistances. Notably, the proposed control scheme only requires each GFMC to measure its local output voltage and current, thereby eliminating the need for communication links. Moreover, we analyze the stability of the proposed strategy through the established small-signal model. Finally, simulation and experimental results are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme with plug-and-play capability. The results indicate that this scheme achieves an around 88.5% improvement in voltage recovery speed as compared with the conventional control methods without violating control objectives.

title: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
authors: N/A

title: Effective User-defined Keyword Spotting with Dual-stage Matching, Multi-modal Enrollment, and Continual Adaptation
authors: Zhiqi Ai, Han Cheng, Shiyi Mu, Xinnuo Li, Yongjin Zhou, Shugong Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: User-defined keyword spotting (KWS) is crucial for personalized voice interaction, yet existing methods face several challenges: (1) insufficient discriminability among confusable words, (2) performance inconsistency across speakers with varying pronunciations, and (3) high data cost to ensure reliable wake-word performance. In this paper, we introduce DMA-KWS, an efficient and robust framework for user-defined keyword spotting. First, it adopts a dual-stage matching pipeline: CTC decoding with streaming phoneme search to locate candidate segments, followed by QbyT with a phoneme matcher for fine-grained verification, enabling it to better distinguish confusable words. Next, multi-modal enrollment fuses user-specific speech with text embeddings to further improve accuracy for registered users. Finally, a parameter-efficient continual adaptation mechanism performs lightweight updates using synthetic and real data. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superior performance of DMA-KWS. On the LibriPhrase Hard subset, it achieves 97.85% AUC and 6.13% EER, reaching state-of-the-art performance. In speaker-dependent settings, DMA-KWS consistently outperforms text-only enrollment, demonstrating significant performance gains. Moreover, the proposed parameter-efficient fine-tuning mechanism adapts DMA-KWS with only 187k updated parameters, further enhancing KWS performance while ensuring suitability for on-device deployment.

title: AgeBalance: Low-Cost Lifetime Extension for SRAM-based PIM Accelerators
authors: Ning Lin, Shaocong Wang, Yaping Zhao, Yangu He, Songqi Wang, Kwunhang Wong, Rongliang Fu, Wenxing Li, Tsung-Yi Ho, Dashan Shang, Xiaojuan Qi, Xiaoming Chen, Zhongrui Wang
abstract: Although processing-in-memory (PIM) techniques have widely been used for deep neural networks (DNNs) acceleration, the inference performance of aged PIM-based accelerators remains to be investigated. This paper makes the first attempt to study Hot Carrier Injection (HCI) and Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI) aging impacts on SRAM-based DNN accelerators, which provides a novel and unified framework, termed AgeBalance for aging detection, analysis and mitigation. First, we discuss a convenient aging detection scheme. Then, we benchmark the inference accuracy drops of DNNs running on aged SRAM-based PIM accelerators. Finally, we propose a low-cost anti-aging training method without incurring additional hardware overhead on SRAM-based DNN accelerators. Extensive experimental results on MNIST, CIFAR10 and AG News datasets show that aging can cause the inference accuracy of shallow or deep DNNs to drop to about 10%, close to random guessing. The aging mitigation scheme proposed in this paper can largely restore the accuracy to the original. Moreover, the SRAM write overhead of our method is much reduced thanks to a score-based training approach, leading to a reduction of 5× to 10× writing energy compared to the traditional training method.

title: Co-creating a Community-focused Educational Knowledge Graph for Trustworthy Language Knowledge: A Case Study on Yanyuwa
authors: Lele Sha, Zhiping Liang, Zijie Zeng, Dragan Gaˇsevi´ c, Guanliang Chen, Gloria Milena Fernandez-Nieto, Yi-Shan Tsai, Zachari Swiecki, Alice Gaby, John Bradley
abstract: This study explores how educational technology can support endangered language education, with a focus on Australia's critically endangered Indigenous Yanyuwa language. Employing community-focused approaches involving Yanyuwa educators, we collaboratively pioneered an innovative educational Knowledge Graph (KG) that embeds a rich collection of language data from prior documentation and preservation efforts to assist with language learning. Our study reveals that despite the significant challenges posed by shortages in linguistic expertise and advanced computational models, a semantic KG can be built for trustworthy language knowledge retrieval by leveraging a semi-supervised semantic labeling approach. Distinct from prior KGs relying solely on technicians, we show it is feasible to include key community stakeholders at the ontological design and feedback phases, ensuring the KG is tailored to communities' knowledge needs and priorities. We further demonstrate the KG's educational utility through visualizing the retrieved sub-graph as a prototype starting point for co-designing language learning materials with teachers. Our approach is language-agnostic, and may offer a generalizable framework for empowering marginalized communities through trustworthy learning technologies.

title: Generalizable and Robust Beam Prediction for 6G Networks: An Deep-Learning Framework with Positioning Feature Fusion
authors: Yanliang Jin, Yunfan Li, Jiang Jun, Yuan Gao, Shengli Liu, Jianbo Du, Zhaohui Yang, Shugong Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Beamforming (BF) is essential for enhancing system capacity in fifth generation (5G) and beyond wireless networks, yet exhaustive beam training in ultra-massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems incurs substantial overhead. To address this challenge, we propose a deep learning–based frame work that leverages position-aware features to improve beam prediction accuracy while reducing training costs. The proposed approach uses spatial coordinate labels to supervise a position extraction branch and integrates the resulting representations with beam-domain features through a feature fusion module. A dual-branch RegNet architecture is adopted to jointly learn location-related and communication features for beam prediction. Twofusion strategies, namely adaptive and adversarial fusion, are introduced to enable efficient feature integration. The proposed framework is evaluated on datasets generated by the DeepMIMO simulator across four urban scenarios at 3.5 GHz, following 3GPP specifications, where both reference signal received power (RSRP) and user equipment (UE) location information are available. Simulation results under both in-distribution and out of-distribution settings demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently outperforms traditional baselines and achieves more accurate and robust beam prediction by effectively incorporating positioning information.

title: Terminal-Edge-Cloud Collaborative Temperature Field Reconstruction for Multi-chip IGBT Power Modules in Power Internet of Things
authors: Xingfeng Du, Yuan Yang, Jiahui Lv, Wei Xiang, Dao Hua Zhang, Qi Geng, Rui Kang, Yang Wen, Santiago Cóbreces
abstract: In multi-chip IGBT power modules, package-level failures typically manifest first as localized distortions in the copper baseplate temperature field before evolving into catastrophic faults. However, existing Power Internet of Things (Power IoT) monitoring approaches mainly rely on single-point indicators, such as on-state voltage drop or case temperature, making them inadequate for capturing spatially nonuniform thermal anomalies under sparse sensing. This paper proposes a terminal–edge–cloud collaborative method for parallel multi-module temperature-field reconstruction in Power IoT, which maps sparse thermocouple measurements to a high-resolution temperature field while meeting real-time constraints. At the terminal layer, the thermocouple array layout is optimized using a condition-number minimization criterion to maximize system observability. At the edge layer, a physics-constrained conditional generative adversarial network (PC-cGAN) is deployed on an MPSoC platform, and a multi-objective particle swarm optimization algorithm is used to automatically search network architectures and hyperparameters under resource constraints, thereby balancing reconstruction accuracy and inference latency. Experimental results demonstrate that a single edge node can monitor 4–6 power modules in parallel, with an inference latency of 11.8 ms for four-module parallel processing, achieving a 3.1× efficiency improvement over serial architectures. Using 12 sparse measurements, the proposed method achieves a root mean square error of 1.89°C, a mean absolute error of 1.52°C, and a hotspot deviation of 2.34°C; compared with CNN baselines, these metrics improve by 29.5%, 29.3%, and 32.2%, respectively, while reducing communication bandwidth by 98% relative to cloud-centric approaches. The proposed method provides an engineering-ready solution for scalable, real-time thermal-state monitoring of power-module arrays in converter systems.

title: Cortical and Cortico-Muscular Network Modulation Pattern of Eccentric and Concentric Movements in Fatigue State
authors: Yuchen Xu, Chenyang Li, Minmin Wang, Yun-Hsuan Chen, Shaomin Zhang, Mohamad Sawan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: !Lower extremity motor function deficits, combined with fatigue, are common in patients with neurological injuries. The interaction between motor deficits and fatigue further limits rehabilitation endurance and recovery.
