The experiments using the clinical dataset demonstrated the effectiveness of HyperAdAgFormer.

title: Smart Power Grid Fault Detection & Automatic Restoration System
authors: Pallavi Gayke, Pranav Ukey, Shruti Dutt, Samita Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Today's power distribution networks report large scale outages, economic loss and reduced reliability, which we seen in short circuits, line failures and over loading. What we have been using is manual fault detection and conventional grids which causes great down time and delay in recovery. We present a Smart Power Grid Fault Detection and Automatic Restoration System which puts in real time monitoring of grid parameters, which in turn leads to detection of abnormalities, determination of fault location, isolation of the faulted section and restoration of power to the healthy sections of the network. The system uses smart sensors, automatic switches and decision based control to improve performance and to guarantee continuous power supply.

title: Hierarchical Virtual Oscillator Control Based on Andronov-Hopf Oscillator for Microgrid Black Start
authors: Shumeng Wang, Uthandi Selvarasu, Minoo Mohebbifar, Mahshid Amirabadi, Yuan Li, Brad Lehman
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Conventional Virtual Oscillator Control (VOC) enables inverters to achieve fast dynamics, asymptotic stability, intrinsic synchronization, and effective power sharing. However, during practical situations such as black start, when multiple microgrids (MGs) must coordinate and reconnect to the main grid, relying on VOC alone can lead to severe inrush currents and bus voltage drops, despite guaranteed asymptotic stability and synchronization. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a hierarchical VOC structure based on the Andronov-Hopf oscillator (AHO) that maintains the dynamics of AHO on the primary level, while also enabling reliable coordination among multiple MGs, and between MGs and the main grid through secondary and tertiary levels, under realistic black start conditions.

title: EEG-VAE: A Variational Autoencoder for EEG Data Augmentation and Classification
authors: Yan Li, Zhenhuan Zhao, Zihan Chen, Yifan Zhang, Yang Yu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) is a common type of brain signal in brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), and its quality and scale significantly influence the performance of downstream EEG task models. However, manual annotation of EEG data is costly and difficult to obtain in large quantities, making data augmentation technology a critical approach to expanding EEG datasets. Among these, generative model-based data augmentation techniques can generate more realistic and diversified new data by learning the probability distribution of real data, holding promise for improving the performance of downstream EEG task models. This paper focuses on EEG data augmentation based on variational autoencoders (VAE). By integrating model principle derivation with network parameter setting, we constructed the EEG-VAE model. The quality of the generated EEG data was qualitatively evaluated through analysis of EEG topographic maps and waveform diagrams, while the reliability of the data augmentation algorithm was quantitatively verified using three metrics. Additionally, in the downstream motion intention classification task based on EEGNet, selecting an appropriate data augmentation ratio effectively improved classification accuracy. In the binary classification task, adding 25% generated data increased the average classification accuracy from 0.76 to 0.83.

title: Network Layer Security in Smart Multi-domain Integrated Learning Environments: Understanding and Mitigating Attacks
authors: Osama Firas Al-Gharaibeh, Alaa Abdullah Al-Hazaymeh, Abdallah Khaled Alfawaz, Nezar Ahmed Almoghrabi, Obaida M. Al-Hazaimeh, Ashraf A. Abu-Ein
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Important network layer threats that pose a danger to today’s IT infrastructure will be thoroughly discussed in this paper. We take into account threats like as DoS and DDoS assaults, DNS poisoning, ARP poisoning, and DHCP-based attacks including starvation and spoofing. We take a close look at each attack vector, breaking down how it works, the damage it can do, and how to stop it. Network administrators and security specialists should be aware of these vulnerabilities so that they can implement suitable defense measures.

title: An Adaptive Reinforcement Learning-Based Heap Optimization Framework for Robust Medical Image Diagnosis and Prediction (ARL-HBO)
authors: Alvin Manuel, G. Gunasekaran, T. V. Ananth, K. L. Shunmuganathan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The recent advances in deep neural networks have quickly accelerated the application of deep learning in the diagnosis of medical images and decision support systems. Nevertheless, aside from the architecture of the network, the performance of these networks is significantly dependent on the choice of hyperparameters. Inadequate hyperparameter configurations often lead to suboptimal convergence and reduced diagnostic reliability. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an Adaptive Reinforcement Learning-Based Heap Optimization (ARL-HBO) framework for robust medical image diagnosis and prediction. The proposed framework integrates reinforcement learning with an improved Heap-based Optimization algorithm, which is enriched with grey wolf optimization concepts and orthogonal learning approaches. The ARL-HBO strategy optimizes the hyperparameters of a deep residual neural network, which leads to enhanced classification accuracy, stability, and generalization. The experimental outcome reveals that the proposed strategy performs better than other strategies.

title: Compact and Efficient 3kW 1MHz GaN LLC Converter Incorporating Matrix Transformer for Data Center Power Delivery
authors: Jai Aditya Chaudhary, Kamran Kamran, Rosario Attanasio, Andrea Russo, Nicola Tecli, Gianni Vitale
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and experimental validation of a 3kW, 1MHz LLC resonant converter using GaN transistors, achieving 98.5% peak efficiency. An improved planar matrix transformer, consisting of two elemental planar transformers on a 10-layer, 2Oz PCB with an optimized winding structure, enables high power density and low losses. The study covers the LLC converter design, loss analysis, and transformer optimization in pursuit of ultra high-power density of up to 2500W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. Experimental results on a 3kW prototype confirm the high efficiency and compactness, making it ideal for advanced data center power applications

title: Deep Learning Based Landmarks Detection for Positioning Assessment in Mammography
authors: Lauriane Grondin-Reiher, Victor Patenaude, Valérie Gauvin, Francois Guibault, Lama Séoud
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Proper breast positioning is critical for reliable mammographic screening, yet remains a major source of diagnostic variability and exam failure. This study proposes an interpretable, landmark-based framework for the automated evaluation of the Adequate amount of pectoral muscle criterion in mediolateral oblique mammograms. Our approach combines a DeepLabv3+ model for pectoral muscle segmentation with a Gaussian U-Net for nipple localization, followed by a geometric decision algorithm assessing positioning adequacy from the relative locations of the predicted landmarks. The method was trained and evaluated on 2274 annotated MLO images derived from a multicenter audit, with a test subset independently labeled by three expert radiographers to account for inter-rater variability. The proposed system achieved a balanced accuracy of 0.88 (specificity of 0.92, sensitivity of 0.84), outperforming both classification-based CNN baselines and previous landmark detection models. Mean localization errors were within expert variability (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5-3.0 ~\text{mm}$</tex> for pectoral landmarks, 2.0 mm for nipple). These results highlight that anatomically grounded, explainable models can deliver robust and clinically meaningful positioning assessments even in imbalanced and subjective datasets.

title: Enhanced Complex Vector Control of Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines with Resistive Voltage Drop Compensation
authors: Kunal Layek, Arijit Banerjee
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: Complex vector control of permanent magnet synchronous motors can substantially reduce the adverse effects of inverter delays by enabling controller design without cross-coupling feedforward compensation. In this paper, the impact of resistance drops and their compensation is analyzed for complex vector model-based control systems, and it is found that the inverter delay can introduce instability in the current vector control loop. To address this issue, a resistance-compensated complex current vector controller is proposed, which incorporates the stator resistance voltage drop directly into the plant model and designs the controller accordingly. This approach achieves the desired dynamic response while preventing instability. The effectiveness of the proposed control strategy is validated through MATLAB simulations.

title: Decomposing Pathological Features into Patho-Spatial Graphs for Skin Lesion Diagnosis
authors: Guowei Dai, Caixia Dong, Jiangbo Zhang, Yu Wang, Duwei Dai
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Existing deep learning models for skin lesion diagnosis often operate as black boxes, lacking an explicit understanding of the pathological structures topology, which contradicts the diagnostic logic of dermatologists. To address this, we introduce DermPrism, a novel multi-stage framework. At its core is the Patho-Spatial Graph (PSG) Block, which decomposes the visual representation into a structured pathological graph. This is achieved by a Pathological Characteristic Point (PCP) Miner that identifies diagnostically salient points, a Hierarchical Graph Connector that builds a multi-scale topological map, and a Geometric Learner that reasons over this graph. By explicitly modeling the where and how of pathological features, DermPrism bridges the critical gap between pixel-level recognition and high-level clinical reasoning. Experiments demonstrate that our topology-aware, decompositional approach sets a new state-of-the-art in diagnostic accuracy and provides significantly enhanced interpretability.

title: AI Generalisation Gap in Comorbid Sleep Disorder Staging
authors: Saswata Bose, Suvadeep Maiti, Shivam Kumar Sharma, Mythirayee S, Tapabrata Chakraborti, Srijitesh Rajendran, Raju S. Bapi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate sleep staging is essential for diagnosing OSA and hypopnea in stroke patients. Although PSG is reliable, it is costly, labor-intensive, and manually scored. While deep learning enables automated EEG-based sleep staging in healthy subjects, our analysis shows poor generalization to clinical populations with disrupted sleep. Using Grad-CAM interpretations, we systematically demonstrate this limitation. We introduce iSLEEPS, a newly clinically annotated ischemic stroke dataset (to be publicly released), and evaluate a SE-ResNet plus bidirectional LSTM model for single-channel EEG sleep staging. As expected, cross-domain performance between healthy and diseased subjects is poor. Attention visualizations, supported by clinical expert feedback, show the model focuses on physiologically uninformative EEG regions in patient data. Statistical and computational analyses further confirm significant sleep architecture differences between healthy and ischemic stroke cohorts, highlighting the need for subject-aware or disease-specific models with clinical validation before deployment. A summary of the paper and the code is available at https://himalayansaswatabose.github.io/iSLEEPS_Explainability.github.io/

title: Graph-Fista-Net: An Interpretable Graph Unrolling Network for Fast Compressed Sensing MRI
authors: Aromal C. J, Sumit Datta
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reconstructing high quality magnetic resonance (MR) images from compressed sensing (CS) data remains a difficult problem, especially when only a small portion of k-space is sampled. In this work, we introduce Graph-FISTA-Net, an interpretable unrolled network designed for fast CS-MRI reconstruction. Instead of following the usual fixed iteration order in FISTA based models, our model learns a directed acyclic graph that connects different stages of the optimization process. Each node captures how information is shared between iterations through a temperature-controlled adjacency matrix, enabling the network to discover its own data-driven momentum pattern. Entropy and sparsity regularizations are incorporated to maintain stability and ensure monotone convergence. Experiments with T1-weighted brain magnetic resonance data (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-40 \%$</tex> sampling) show consistent improvements in PSNR and SSIM over FISTA-Net without increasing inference time. Visualizations of the learned graph reveal clear and interpretable update behaviors during reconstruction.

title: Faster 4D Flow MRI Scan with 3D Arbitrary-Scale Super-Resolution
authors: Simon Perrin, Sébastien Levilly, Harold Mouchère, Jean-Michel Serfaty
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: 4D Flow MRI enables comprehensive hemodynamic assessment but remains constrained by prolonged acquisition times (10-15 min) and limited spatial resolution, hindering clinical deployment. Conventional super-resolution (SR) methods aim to enhance the original spatial resolution without accounting for acquisition time. This study investigates arbitrary-scale super-resolution (ASSR) frameworks applied to reduced-time acquisitions to recover clinical-grade resolution. Two generalisable ASSR architectures - LIIF and LTE - originally designed for 2D natural imagery, are reformulated for 3D volumetric data and trained exclusively on in-vivo measurements. To suppress background noise reconstruction, a Weighted Mean Frequencies (WMF)-based loss function is introduced, emphasising regions of high pulsatility. Furthermore, a physics-based regularisation term enforcing mass conservation is incorporated, analogous to PINN. Quantitative evaluation demonstrates that both ASSR models markedly outperform linear interpolation and a CFDpretrained SURFR-Net baseline. The LTE model exhibits superior in-scale fidelity, whereas LIIF demonstrates enhanced generalisation across unseen scaling factors.

title: Privacy-Preserving and Scalable Blockchain-Based Certificate Authentication Using Zero-Knowledge Rollups and AI-Based Trust Scoring
authors: Vallamkonda Jyothi, Dama Rishitha Naidu, Challa Sravan Kumar, Dunna Lalith Krishna Kanth, Chinthapalli Martin Rich
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The accelerated digitalization of academic qualifications requires certificate authentication systems with not only the capability not to be tampered with, but also privacy-assuring and scalable. Although certificate verification using blockchain guarantees immutability and transparency, current solutions have high transaction costs, low scalability, and a loosely applied guarantee of privacy. In this research, proposes a new Privacy-Preserving and Scalable Blockchain-Based Certificate Authentication System, which combines Zero-Knowledge (ZK) rollups with an AI-based system of trust scoring. ZK-rollups save a lot of gas through batching certificate transactions and generating succinct cryptographic proofs, which enhances throughput and makes operational costs less. Zero-knowledge proofs can be used to facilitate privacy by providing the selective disclosure so that the verifiers can verify the validity of the certificates without access to sensitive personal information. Also, trust scoring model is an AI-based model that dynamically analyzes the actions of the validators to identify anomalies, collusion, and malicious actions. The experimental analysis shows significant reduction in transaction costs (as much as 80 percent), lower verification latency as well as resilience against Sybil and coordinated attacks. The suggested model provides a privacy-conscious, secure and scalable decentralized certificate authentication model.

title: NEUSEG: Interpretable Unsupervised GM/WM Segmentation in Brain Histopathology Using Nuclei Morphometrics
authors: Hyung Seok Roh, Noah Capp, Daniel T. Ohm, David J. Irwin, James C. Gee, Min Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We present NEUSEG, a fully automated, interpretable, and unsupervised pipeline for gray/white matter (GM/WM) segmentation in brain histopathology whole-slide images (WSIs). GM/WM segmentation in WSIs is challenging due to their gigapixel-scale resolution and substantial variability in staining, regional morphology, and underlying pathology. To address this, NEUSEG combines nuclei morphometrics with a two-component Gaussian Mixture Model and refines boundaries using a Conditional Random Field and morphological post-processing. In comparison with a supervised CNN baseline, NEUSEG achieved comparable accuracy on in-distribution slides and superior robustness under out-of-distribution conditions. Across 252 WSIs with expert-annotated regions of interest (ROIs), median annotation-to-contour distances were 32.89 μm for the GMBG boundary and 129.79 μm for the GM-WM boundary, with no significant difference across stains or pathology types and only minor regional variation. Percent area occupied between expertand segmentation-derived ROIs showed near-perfect agreement (r=0.987). NEUSEG is lightweight, CPU-operable, and processes each WSI within minutes, enabling scalable and robust GM/WM segmentation across heterogeneous histopathology datasets.

title: Scale-Cascaded Diffusion Models for Super-Resolution in Medical Imaging
authors: Darshan Thaker, Mahmoud Mostapha, Radu Miron, Shihan Qiu, Mariappan Nadar
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Diffusion models have been increasingly used as strong generative priors for solving inverse problems such as superresolution in medical imaging. However, these approaches typically utilize a diffusion prior trained at a single scale, ignoring the hierarchical scale structure of image data. In this work, we propose to decompose images into Laplacian pyramid scales and train separate diffusion priors for each frequency band. We then develop an algorithm to perform super-resolution that utilizes these priors to progressively refine reconstructions across different scales. Evaluated on brain, knee, and prostate MRI data, our approach both improves perceptual quality over baselines and reduces inference time through smaller coarse-scale networks. Our framework unifies multiscale reconstruction and diffusion priors for medical image super-resolution.

title: Adaptive Digital Control Addressing Inductance and Bus Voltage Variation in PFC for Energy Storage Systems (ESS)
authors: Marco Torrisi, Sebastiano Messina, Angelo Giordano, Mario Cacciato
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents digital adaptive current control of PFC to address variable boost inductance and variable bus voltage. The proposed approach is particularly suitable for Energy Storage Systems (ESS) and PFC applications in general. Digital control is implemented through a simple proportional integrator (PI) regulator, and Bode stability analysis is conducted for different inductances and bus voltages. Then, a low processing and low memory MCU implementation is presented. The effectiveness of the proposed adaptive control is validated in a SiC-based 7-kW PFC platform for ESS, showing significant steady-state performance improvements (THDi, PF, and efficiency) over traditional methods with fixed control parameters. Finally, transient tests demonstrate the robustness of the proposed control also in dynamic conditions.

title: Circuit-Balanced Flyback Converter Eliminating the Need for an Isolated Auxiliary Supply for Gate Driver IC
authors: Naoya Miyata, Yuta Imaeda, Silumin Sandaru Seneneyake, Sihoon Choi, Yu Yonezawa, Jun Imaoka, Masayoshi Yamamoto, Hayato Nakamura, Naoki Yoshikawa, Masashi Sawada
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Wide-bandgap devices enable high-frequency operation, though their fast switching generates common-mode (CM) noise. Circuit balancing reduces CM noise in flyback converters by maintaining circuit symmetry. However, the balanced structure causes the source potential of the switching devices to fluctuate, requiring the gate-driver IC to use an isolated auxiliary supply. This not only diminishes the advantages of the flyback converter but also introduces additional noise paths. This paper proposes a balanced flyback converter that eliminates the need for an isolated auxiliary supply by reusing the surge energy absorbed by the snubber circuit. The proposed method is validated through experiments, confirming stable gate-drive operation and effective CM noise reduction.

title: Boundary-Aware Adversarial Filtering for Reliable Diagnosis Under Extreme Class Imbalance
authors: Yanxuan Yu, Michael S. Hughes, Julien Lee, Jiacheng Zhou, Andrew F. Laine
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We study classification under extreme class imbalance where recall and calibration are both critical: for example, in medical diagnosis scenarios. We propose AF-SMOTE, a mathematically motivated augmentation framework that first synthesizes minority points and then filters them by an adversarial discriminator and a boundary utility model. We prove that, under mild assumptions on the decision boundary smoothness and class-conditional densities, our filtering step monotonically improves a surrogate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F}_{\beta}$</tex> (for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta \geq 1$</tex>) while not inflating Brier score. On MIMIC-IV proxy label prediction and canonical fraud detection benchmarks, AF-SMOTE attains higher recall and average precision than strong oversampling baselines (SMOTE, ADASYN, Borderline-SMOTE, SVM-SMOTE), and yields the best calibration. We further validate these gains across multiple additional datasets beyond MIMIC-IV. Our successful application of AF-SMOTE to a healthcare dataset using a proxy label demonstrates in a disease-agnostic way its practical value in clinical situations, where missing true positive cases in rare diseases can have severe consequences.

title: Disease Progression and Subtype Modeling for Combined Discrete and Continuous Input Data
authors: Sterre de Jonge, Elisabeth J. Vinke, Meike W. Vernooij, Daniel C. Alexander, Alexandra L. Young, Esther E. Bron
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Disease progression modeling provides a robust framework to identify long-term disease trajectories from short-term biomarker data. It is a valuable tool to gain a deeper understanding of diseases with a long disease trajectory, such as Alzheimer's disease. A key limitation of most disease progression models is that they are specific to a single data type (e.g., continuous data), thereby limiting their applicability to heterogeneous, real-world datasets. To address this limitation, we propose the Mixed Events model, a novel disease progression model that handles both discrete and continuous data types. This model is implemented within the Subtype and Stage Inference (SuStaIn) framework, resulting in Mixed-SuStaIn, enabling subtype and progression modeling. We demonstrate the effectiveness of Mixed-SuStaIn through simulation experiments and realworld data from the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative, showing that it performs well on mixed datasets. The code is available at: https://github.com/ucl-pond/pySuStaIn.

title: Three-Port Microconverter Integrating PV Module and Battery to DC-Bus for Reduced Intermittency
authors: Georgios Salagiannis, Emmanuel Tatakis
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Module-level photovoltaic (PV) integration through microconverters is increasingly adopted in distributed power-generation systems. To mitigate the inherent variability of PV sources, batteries are typically interfaced through independent converters. Recent advances have explored unified architectures that combine PV and battery interfaces within compact three-port converters; however, existing solutions often compromise between voltage gain, component count, and efficiency. This work presents a three-port microconverter with an integrated battery, enabling the realization of PV supermodules that deliver continuous power with significantly reduced intermittency. The proposed topology achieves high voltage gain suitable for direct connection to a 380 V DC bus while efficiently processing up to 800 W from modern PV modules. The integrated battery port provides bidirectional power flow up to the nominal rating, enhancing system reliability under fluctuating irradiance. Both maximum power point tracking and output-voltage regulation are implemented, ensuring seamless mode transitions. Experimental validation of a 800 W laboratory prototype confirms stable operation across all modes and demonstrates nominal efficiencies of 97.29, 96.09 and 95.65% for Modes I, II and III accordingly, substantiating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Generative Artificial Intelligence for Intelligent Decision Support in Organizational Management: A Data-Driven Framework
authors: Satvik V Khara, Gaurav D Tivari, Sujal Patel, Jayani Nathvani, Rishi Amrutiya, Dhruv Chavda
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Significant changes in the organizational decision-making process have been witnessed considering the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology. In this context, the emergence of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) has been recognized as an emerging technology with the capability to generate insights, suggestions and predictions on the basis of massive data sets. Considering the organizational decision-making process, the traditional process involves the study of history, which might lead to a time gap or incorrect information in the changing environment. This research study aims to propose a data-driven approach to integrate Generative Artificial Intelligence with the organizational decision support systems to enhance the quality of organizational decisionmaking processes at the strategic, operational and tactical levels. This study will be specific to the use of generative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> in the context of knowledge extraction, scenario development and prediction which are critical components of the organizational decision support systems. To demonstrate the potential of the proposed approach, a conceptual architecture has been developed to showcase the potential offered by generative AI for transforming conventional decision support systems into intelligent systems. The proposed approach is validated through simulated scenarios using actual enterprise data and its effectiveness for improving decision quality, efficiency and organizational responsiveness. The results shows the potential benefits offered by the proposed approach for integrating generative AI into management systems for improving decision accuracy and reducing operational uncertainty. This research has significant implications for the emerging domain of AIbased management systems and offers insights for organizations seeking opportunities for leveraging intelligent technologies for decision support.

title: Improving User Experience in Culinary Exploration Website Through Contextual User Interface Approach
authors: Siska Lewandi, Lidia Tanaya, Anderies
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Foodierate was used as a representative baseline system for evaluating design improvements, chosen for its typical features commonly found in culinary exploration platforms, which tend to increase cognitive load and trigger the Split-Attention Effect. To address this issue, a Contextual User Interface (CUI) design was implemented into Peek & Map, a culinary exploration platform. This study developed and evaluated the UX quality of Peek & Map which implements CUI compared to the static baseline through a structured mixed-method approach. 47 participants completed a within-subjects scenario, and a heuristic analysis based on Nielsen's Usability Heuristics was combined with the User Experience Questionnaire (UEQ) to evaluate and generate the comparative data. While the expert heuristic review provided the technical “diagnosis, the UEQ captured experiential “symptoms”. The results demonstrate that across all six dimensions of UEQ, Peek & Map with CUI achieved an excellent UX score (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\bar{x} \geq 2.1$</tex>) while the baseline performed poorly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\bar{x} \approx-1.1$</tex> to -1.3). A Paired Samples t-test confirmed highly significant differences between both of CUI and Baseline (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta M=3.15-\mathbf{3. 4 4}, p<0.001$</tex>) with extremely large effect sizes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{d} \geq 1.68$</tex>). These results show empirical evidence of Peek & Map that implements CUI as an effective mechanism for reducing cognitive load and validate that for linking UX deficits to specific design flaws, an integrated Symptoms-Diagnosis framework is suitable.

title: CLTextRank: A Contrastive Learning Enhanced TextRank Model for Keyword Extraction in Power System Service Orders
authors: Hao Zhang, Guangdong Wang, Dongdong Zhu, Tianrun Yu, Jiayin Bai, Yunkun Cao, Haizhou Du
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Keyword generation is a fine-grained task in the field of natural language processing. The generated keyword phrases can be applied to various practical scenarios such as text classification, text clustering, and semantic analysis. Work order data is characterized by high volume, diversity, and real-time nature. It implicitly contains customer needs and service expectations, which are valuable for improving and managing business operations. However, power companies currently analyze work order data mainly at a statistical level, without deeply mining or organizing the latent information. To address this issue, this paper proposes a keyword phrase extraction method called CLTextRank (Contrastive Learning TextRank), which integrates a contrastive learning framework with TextRank. Under the scenario of processing power work order data, this method aims to extract key information by removing formatted and fixed textual patterns, while retaining useful content that reflects users’ actual problems. The extracted information can then be used to populate and enrich general response templates for service orders.

title: A Novel Low-Voltage Ride-Through Control for Bidirectional Megawatt Charging System with Grid Support Capability
authors: Stephen Ambe Chengu, Nikolaos Gatsis, Miltiadis Alamaniotis, Sara Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The rapid deployment of megawatt charging systems (MCS) for heavy-duty electric vehicles (HDEVs) poses challenges to grid stability, especially during voltage sags. Conventional chargers operate at fixed power factor and disconnect when the point of connection voltage drops below 0.7 pu [1], causing abrupt load withdrawal that worsens instability and disrupts protection. This paper proposes a low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) control strategy for bidirectional megawatt-scale chargers with reactive power support. The method coordinates front-end and back-end converters to reduce active power and inject reactive current during voltage sag conditions, accelerating voltage recovery and enhancing AC-side protection. The proposed control strategy is implemented and tested on the IEEE 13-bus feeder in MATLAB/Simulink under both charger-induced sags and grid faults. Results show that the proposed controller sustains charger operation and enables megawatt-scale stations to act as stabilizing resources, strengthening grid resiliency during disturbances.

title: Magnetics Integrated Design for CNR Point-of-Load Converter
authors: Zehui Li, Zhongqiu Zhang, Qishan Pan, Haoyu Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In data center point-of-load applications, current ninefold rectifier (CNR) converter offers obvious advantages over conventional current doubler rectifier (CDR), including zero-voltage switching (ZVS) and enhanced output current capability. However, the CNR typically employs three discrete transformers, resulting in a bulky design that compromises power density. Given the demanding requirements of data centers — particularly large voltage conversion ratios and high output currents — efficient magnetic design is crucial for achieving both high efficiency and high power density. To address this challenge, this paper presents a magnetic integration scheme tailored for the CNR converter, which substantially reduces transformer core volume. A 24 V-to-0.8 V, 180 A, 500 kHz CNR prototype incorporating the integrated magnetics is designed and experimentally validated. The prototype demonstrates a peak efficiency of 93.7% and maintains 89.6% efficiency at full load.

title: Multimodal Cough Detection and Classification with Machine Learning: Research Gaps and Future Directions
authors: Noor E. Hadi, Ghaidaa A. Khalid, Ali A. Al-Naji
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Cough is a primary symptom of many acute and chronic respiratory diseases and serves as an important indicator of disease progression. Conventional clinical assessments rely on observation or patient self-reporting, which limits continuous monitoring. This review systematically analyzes twenty-seven recent studies on AI-based cough detection and classification systems. The reviewed approaches are categorized into four groups: audio-only systems, sensor-based systems, multimodal fusion frameworks, and hybrid deep learning architectures. Each category is evaluated in terms of feature extraction strategies, classification models, dataset characteristics, performance metrics, and deployment feasibility. The analysis reveals that while audio-based systems dominate due to accessibility, they remain vulnerable to environmental noise and privacy concerns. Sensor-based and multimodal approaches improve robustness but face limitations related to dataset scale and real-world validation. Hybrid architectures demonstrate strong performance by capturing both temporal and spectral characteristics; however, most models are validated on restricted or laboratory-controlled datasets. Key research gaps include limited demographic diversity, generalization challenges, data imbalance, real-time implementation constraints, and integration barriers with clinical infrastructures. Future research should prioritize large-scale annotated datasets, energy-efficient edge-AI deployment, and privacy-preserving frameworks to enable scalable clinical and home-based respiratory monitoring.

title: Analysis and Design of Integrated Magnetic Components Using Magnetic Shunts for a Dual Active Bridge Converter
authors: Alberto Cárcamo, Sayan Paul, Ashwini Kumar Dubey, Dragan Maksimovic, Luca Corradini, Aitor Vázquez
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Integrated Magnetic Components (IMC) combine multiple discrete magnetic elements into a single device to reduce size and improve power density. This work explores the use of magnetic shunts to integrate the series inductance into a planar transformer of a Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter. This integration allows the control of the leakage inductance of the transformer, while reducing the size of the magnetic devices. A fully interleaved winding arrangement is employed, which minimizes power losses from the proximity effect. A general analysis of the Integrated Magnetic Component with Magnetic Shunts (IMCMS) magnetic circuit is developed, deriving a simplified model to accurately estimate the leakage inductance. Furthermore, it explores the magnetic flux behavior of the component, describing the effects of adding the magnetic shunts and the considerations to prevent saturation. A power analysis is presented, comparing this proposal with a conventional design composed of a transformer and an external inductor. Experimental validation is provided using a DAB converter operating over an input voltage range of 35–50 V and an output voltage range of 180–300 V and a maximum power of 1.1 kW.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Startup Incubation and Acceleration: A Global Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Walid Dalal, Dounia Rabhi, Marouane Ikira
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: This study presents a global bibliometric review of published, peer-reviewed scientific literature on artificial intelligence (AI), startup incubation and acceleration. Data were compiled from 310 peer-reviewed publications in the Web of Science from 2016 until 2025. Publication trends, collaboration networks and themes were analysed with Bibliometrix (R) and VOSviewer. Since 2019, there has been exponential growth in AI-related research. Five research groups or clusters have been identified: innovation based on AI technology; digital transformation; machine learning; sustainability; and entrepreneurial ecosystems. The leading countries in terms of output were found to be the U.S, China and Italy, indicating that many institutions originating from major research universities have participated in the research. Findings reveal a movement away from conventional resource-based incubation models to AI-enabled, data-driven, and globally networked ecosystems, in which intelligent systems are used to optimize mentoring, venture selection, and innovation forecasting. Despite this rapid growth, the research area remains fragmented and geographically concentrated. This study contributes to the entrepreneurship and innovation disciplines by providing a map of the theoretical and conceptual landscape of AI-focused startup incubation and acceleration.

title: Design Optimization of Ferrite Based Axial Flux PM Machine for Low-Speed Applications
authors: Deepak Kumar, Himanshu Misra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper investigates the viability of ferrite magnets in low-speed Axial Flux Permanent Magnet (AFPM) machines by benchmarking their performance against a dimensionally equivalent NdFeB-based AFPM. Both machines adopt a 12-slot, 10-pole topology with double-layer Fractional Slot Concentrated Windings (FSCW). The NdFeB reference design is rated at 4.5 kW and 600 RPM, representing a compact, high-torque, low-speed electric drive. Ferrite magnets, valued for their thermal stability and cost-effectiveness, are explored as sustainable alternatives to rare-earth magnets, particularly in low-speed applications where their lower remanence can be offset through geometric optimization.A comprehensive performance evaluation including no-load and full-load simulations is conducted for both machines. A multi-objective cost function is formulated to optimize key design variables such as magnet thickness, airgap length, and yoke dimensions. The function simultaneously minimizes mass, maximizes torque, and incorporates penalty terms to enforce efficiency and flux density constraints. Over 250 design variants are evaluated, and Pareto solutions are extracted to analyze trade-offs between electromagnetic performance and lightweight design. The results demonstrate that, despite inherent limitations in magnetic strength, ferrite-based AFPM can be an alternative to low speed drives.

title: Cross-View User Identification for Vision-Aided 3D Beam Alignment in UAV-to-Vehicle MmWave Networks
authors: Foad Barghikar, Foroogh S. Tabataba, Mehdi Naderi Soorki
abstract: In highly stochastic scenarios, like mobile vehicles in dense urban environments, frequent 5G-NR beam management introduces high overhead due to exhaustive beamspace searches. Vision-aided wireless communication offers a promising solution by extracting channel semantic information, such as propagation and target direction, from visual data captured by onboard cameras to execute channel-related tasks with lower overhead. However, it requires accurate target user identification among other objects in the imaging scene. Traditional methods rely on visual and beamforming data, inheriting beam management delays. We propose a user identification method for UAV-to-vehicle networks using multimodal sensor data (vehicle compass, UAV/vehicle-view images) fed into a non-shared-weights Siamese neural network to identify the target vehicle among non-transmitting/non-receiving ones. We generate a synthetic dataset to train this model for bridging the gap between the ground and aerial views to match radio and visual domains. In vehicle-crowded scenarios, our approach achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$99.15\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> identification accuracy and significantly reduces latency compared to traditional methods. We also introduce a solution for optimally aligning beam pairs without pilot transmission overhead, making codebook-based beamforming unnecessary. Our vision-based beam alignment enhances beamforming gain and removes in-band beam management overhead, resulting in a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$43.4\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> average improvement in achievable rate across various traditional system configurations.

title: An Efficient CFAR Detector for Burr Type-XII Distributed SAR Ground Clutter
authors: Dheeren Ku Mahapatra, Sourabh Paul, Alejandro C. Frery, Lakshi Prosad Roy
abstract: In this paper, a CFAR target detection algorithm is proposed for Burr TypeXII-distributed Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) ground clutter amplitude data. We employ method of log-cumulants (MoLC) to estimate the clutter model parameters. A closed-form expression for the CFAR detection threshold is formulated. Furthermore, we derive a closed-form expression for the detection probability for the proposed CFAR-BurrTypeXII detector.We present the variation of Pd with the clutter model parameters representing scene heterogeneity and the ROC curve to illustrate the performance of CFAR detection. The proposed CFAR-BurrTypeXII detector is tested on MSTAR (Moving and Stationary Target Acquisition and Recognition) data to assess the detection accuracy. Experimental results illustrate the superior detection accuracy of the proposed detector over CFAR-LGN, CFAR-WBL, CFAR-K, CFAR-G0 detectors.

title: Cyber Security Challenges: A Strategic Model for the Software Development Organizations
authors: Kausar-Nasreen Khattak, Sabahat, Naeem Iqbal, Zeeshan, Jaeyoung Choi
abstract: Security is the process of providing protection to the software development industry to mitigate various kinds of threats such as cyber-attacks. Cyber-attacks are increasing rapidly at an unprecedented rate in the software development industry. Due to rapid increases in cyber-attacks, well-reputed Software Development Organizations (SDO) and government institutions are facing challenges related to vulnerabilities to cyber-attacks and potential threats. Furthermore, cyber-attacks are evolving; therefore, it is challenging to enable cyber security approaches to assure security on continuous observation. Thus, SDO are facing serious problems such as cyber theft, malware attacks, insider’s threats, etc. Therefore, there is a need to investigate challenges related to cyber security affecting SDO; how these cyber-attacks harm and negatively effects the organizations in context of software development and how to mitigate with these evolving cyber-attacks enabling organizations for secure software development. Hence, in our proposed research study, a deliberate and articulate attempt is made by developing a Cyber Security Challenges in Software Development (CSC-SD) to identify challenges negatively effecting the organizations during the development of software’s. Furthermore, our proposed research uses a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) to identify the challenging factors of the cyber-attacks impacting organizations in the context of the SDO by developing a search string based on the defined research questions. The SLR is performed on 77 research articles selected based on the inclusion/exclusion and quality criteria from different databases, for instance, Google Scholar, IEEE, Springer, ACM, ResearchGate, and ScienceDirect. Based on the conducted SLR, 11 potential challenges are identified and investigated in order to facilitate the SDO to avoid or mitigate the potential threats posed by cyber-attacks. The identified potential challenges include cyber-attack exposure, knowledge deficiency, framework gaps, technical support limitations, disaster preparedness gaps, resource constraints, confidentiality and trust deficiency, management and measurement gaps, unauthorized access risk, development execution gaps, and quality/reliability concerns. The findings of our research study are intended to substantially aid SDO in fending off the threats posed by cyberattacks. In this way, it will also enable and encourage SDO towards secure software development. Furthermore, the findings of our research study analysis highlight both the similarities and differences in identified 11 potential challenges across various organizations, databases, and research methodologies. Moreover, our proposed research study provides a roadmap to conduct future research with the basic premise to formulate a CSC-SD model.

title:
SMGF
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    -YOLACT: a Spatial response, Mask vector Generation-Focus-Fusion instance segmentation for head-neck lymph nodes
authors: Tao Zhou, Wenwen Chai, Huiling Lu, Defang Chang, Qitao Liu, Kaixiong Chen, Zhe Zhang, XiaoYing Jia, Yong Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Head-neck lymph nodes are important pathways for the metastasis of cervical malignant tumors. Accurate segmentation of the lesion is of great significance for clinical diagnosis and treatment evaluation. There are some problems in anchor boxes-based instance segmentation caused by differences in lesion size and vague boundary, such as redundant anchor boxes, high computational cost, weak coupling ability between instance masks and spatial positions, and limited perception ability. The SMGF<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-YOLACT model with "Heatmap better than Box Mask" is proposed. The model revolves around 4 questions "How to use the hot zones of heatmap to guide the instance mask vector?", "How to generate the instance mask vector?" ,"How to focus the instance mask vector?" and "How to fuse instance mask vectors?". Firstly, the model introduces spatial heatmap to replace anchor boxes for location, enhancing the context awareness ability; Secondly, in the heatmap high-response region, candidate mask vectors are generated based on the heatmap guidance mechanism. The candidate mask vectors are optimized by using Mask Regression and Semantic Aggregation mechanisms to enhance the consistency between instance masks and instance object; Thirdly, Top-K and Top-L focusing strategies are designed to compress the redundant mask vector set and focus on the boundary region; Finally, the model fuses the mask vector and the prototype mask to obtain semantically consistent and clear boundary instance mask. The validity of the model is verified on the head-neck lymph nodes dataset, with mAP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">det</sub> and mAP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">seg</sub> reaching 40.2% and 43.4% respectively, and the inference speed reaching 36FPS. The generalization ability of the model is evaluated on the BraTS brain tumor dataset, with mAP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">det</sub> and mAP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">seg</sub> reaching 65.0% and 64.3% respectively, and the inference speed reaching 44FPS. The results show that the model outperforms the existing methods in terms of accuracy, efficiency and robustness, which is of positive significance for computer-aided diagnosis.

title: Robust Control of Affine Nonlinear Systems Using an Energy-Efficient Gray-Box Framework: A Practical Validation
authors: Alireza Nezamzadeh, Mohammadreza Esmaeilidehkordi, Nikolai Drigal
abstract: Hysteresis nonlinearities, parametric uncertainties, and external disturbances substantially impair the performance and energy efficiency of piezoelectric actuators (PEAs), which are extensively utilized in high-precision positioning and energy-sensitive actuation systems. Conventional model-based control strategies depend on accurate hysteresis modeling and parameter identification, which are often difficult to accomplish and may deteriorate under varying operating conditions. By contrast, data-driven methods usually focus on tracking performance without making sure that closed-loop stability or energy-aware operation is always present. This paper introduces a novel gray-box modeling and control framework designed to address the challenges associated with piezoelectric actuator systems. It integrates a brain emotional learning-based intelligent controller (BELBIC) with a fuzzy extended state observer (FESO). The BELBIC component learns about unknown nonlinear dynamics in real time, eliminating the need for explicit hysteresis modeling or precise parameter identification. Simultaneously, the FESO estimates unmeasured system states and aggregates disturbances related to hysteresis as they occur. This approach enables effective disturbance compensation without requiring hysteresis inversion.Acomprehensive Lyapunov-based stability analysis is conducted for the entire BELBIC–FESO closed-loop system, considering the interactions between the learning mechanism, observer dynamics, and control law. Results from both simulations and tests on a real piezoelectric actuator show that it can track accurately, reduce hysteresis effectively, be more resistant to uncertainties, and use less energy. This is shown by lower control oscillations and optimized actuation effort.

title: Development of a Mixed-Signal Readout ASIC for 3D Position Sensitive CZT Detector
authors: Hao Chang, Zirui Yang, Yan Huang, Xutao Zheng, Jiahuan Zhu, Yang Tian, Yulan Li, Feng Liu, Hua Feng, Wei Shen, Ming Zeng
abstract: Three-dimensional position-sensitive Cadmium-Zinc-Telluride (3D-CZT) detectors are promising in astrophysics. However, the specific space environment and scientific observation requirements impose numerous challenges on the readout electronics, such as achieving low noise, high timing precision and count rate, alongside the need to balance high integration with low power consumption. Thus, we propose the MPT3128, a 128-channel mixed-signal application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) for 3D-CZT detectors. Each channel integrates a low-noise charge-sensitive amplifier (CSA), fast and slow shapers, a 12-bit ADC (shared between two channels), and a time-to-digital converter (TDC) with a resolution of 312.5 ps. Specifically, for cathode timing, it features an adjustable fast-shaper peaking time and a triple-threshold timing scheme. Experimental results demonstrate that the analog front-end achieves low timing jitter (∼2 ns even at 4.5 fC charge input), low electronic noise (202 e- rms), and high energy resolution (5.7 keV FWHM at 511 keV), while featuring fully digital output and control interfaces, facilitating system integration. Furthermore, with all channels active, the triggering rate reaches 55 kHz per channel. With relatively low power consumption (6.34 mW/channel), it is a promising solution for advanced radiation applications.

title: Modeling Atmospheric Attenuation in Vertical FSO Links: An Altitude-Aware Piecewise Framework
authors: Eylem Erdogan, Mohammed Elamassie, Ibrahim Altunbas, Gunes Karabulut Kurt, Murat Uysal, Halim Yanikomeroglu
abstract: Free-space optical (FSO) communication is emerging as a key backhaul technology for next-generation vertical heterogeneous networks (VHetNets), whose architecture spans satellites, high-altitude platform stations (HAPSs), autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs), and terrestrial nodes. Along these vertical and slant paths, optical beams traverse successive atmospheric layers that may contain clouds, fog, rain, and aerosols, conditions that conventional single-coefficient Beer–Lambert (BL) models typically handle only in isolation. Instead of such simplified formulas, we present a unified attenuation model that incorporates aerosols, fog, rain, cloud layers, and drizzle, accounts for the zenith angle, and provides a holistic estimate of the cumulative power loss across atmospheric layers. Numerical results show several-decibel attenuation variations across representative weather scenarios, while the difference between the proposed model predictions and the layer-resolved Moderate Resolution Atmospheric Transmission (MODTRAN) simulations remains within 1 dB, thereby validating the accuracy of the proposed model and its practical relevance for VHetNet link budget studies.

title: Miniature On-Chip Dual-Band Balanced Power Divider With Input Common-Mode Noise Absorption
authors: Yo-Shen Lin, Meng-Ping Chueh, Wei-Chih Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: A miniature on-chip dual-band balanced power divider design based on bridged T-coils and thin-film resistors (TFRs) is proposed, which is capable of absorbing the input common-mode noise. As a proof of concept, a 10-/24-GHz prototype implemented with a GaAs-based integrated passive device (IPD) process is demonstrated. It features a very compact circuit size of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.83\times 2.61$</tex-math> </inline-formula> mm, and the corresponding electrical size is only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.128\lambda _{0} \times 0.087\lambda _{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> at 10 GHz. Measured results demonstrate differential-mode insertion loss of 3.99/6.07 dB and common-mode rejection of 48.4/33 dB at 10/24 GHz, respectively. Notably, the proposed design achieves high common-mode suppression with more than 98.6% of the input common-mode noise being absorbed at both operating frequencies.

title: An Expert Trajectory Guided Online Energy Management Strategy for Aviation Fuel Cell Hybrid Power System
authors: Rui Ma, Feier Meng, Zhirui Guo, Haoran Ma, Wentao Jiang, Yang Zhou
abstract: To meet the higher power output and real-time control requirements of aviation fuel cell hybrid power system, an online energy management strategy (EMS) for multienergy sources becomes essential. A reinforcement learning (RL) based online EMS is proposed in the article, which integrates expert trajectory guidance with autonomous exploration mechanism. During the offline phase, the strategy mimics power allocation actions based on trajectories generated by the dimension-reduction dynamic programming (DRDP) strategy. Subsequently, the trained agent is deployed online for performance validation. Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test results demonstrate that compared with demo-guided TD3 and demo-guided SAC, the proposed strategy can effectively reduce hydrogen consumption rate by 1.34%–2.45%, suppress the state of charge (SOC) fluctuations, and enhance operational efficiency by 2.43%–5.56%. While inheriting global optimization capabilities, the proposed strategy significantly enhances rapid response and adaptive capacity under complex operational conditions, which significantly improves the economic performance and operational stability of aviation fuel cell hybrid power systems.

title: Characterization of Sub-6-GHz Carbon Nanotube Transistor-Based Resistive Mixer
authors: Simin He, Yanwen Wu, Zhengshun Li, Defu Wang, Lian-Mao Peng
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents the fabrication and characterization of carbon nanotube (CNT) transistors and demonstrates their feasibility for high-linearity sub-6-GHz single-ended resistive mixer applications. Randomly oriented CNT FETs with a gate length of 200 nm were realized, delivering a current-gain cutoff frequency of 9.6 GHz and a maximum oscillation frequency of 21.9 GHz. To evaluate their performance in practical RF environments, the CNT FETs were integrated onto a Rogers 4003 PCB via wire bonding, enabling characterization under realistic interconnect and fixture parasitics without de-embedding. Under zero-bias operation, the proposed gate-pumped resistive mixer achieves a conversion gain (CG) of −17.8–−21 dB over the 1.4–5 GHz band. An input third-order intercept point (IIP3) of 16 dBm is obtained, indicating good linearity. These results demonstrate the feasibility of CNT FETs for passive mixer applications in sub-6-GHz RF front ends.

title: Bilateral Neurodynamic-Reconstructed Olfactory Model for Agricultural Product Recognition in Electronic Noses
authors: Yatao Cheng, Jinxia Liu, Yan Shi, Yanwei Wang, Hong Men
abstract: Electronic noses (E-noses) are intelligent identification systems inspired by biological olfactory perception mechanisms. However, data captured by conventional E-noses primarily represent mechanistic expressions of odor properties, and existing analytical methods fail to fully replicate the neurodynamic characteristics inherent in biological olfactory systems. To address the limitations of the classical KIII computational model in simulating bilateral synergistic perception, this study systematically reconstructed the topological structure and dynamic equations to establish the Bilateral Olfactory Neural Model (BONM), incorporating recent neuroanatomical findings on bilateral interaction mechanisms. Integrating the BONM module into the E-nose data processing pipeline facilitates a transition from simple pattern recognition to biomimetic perceptual processing. Self-similarity and chaotic dynamic indices, combined with small-world properties, were innovatively introduced to quantitatively validate the efficacy of BONM in simulating complex neural dynamics. Experimental odor signals from rice, eggs, soybeans, apples, and peanuts were collected using a PEN3 to construct a large-scale dataset. Results demonstrate that the BONM-integrated recognition system significantly outperforms the original KIII model in terms of feature robustness and compatibility with diverse classification algorithms, while maintaining high classification accuracy. This work provides novel insights into the structural evolution and biomimetic evaluation of olfactory models and offers an effective technical solution for developing high-performance, intelligent gas identification engineering systems.

title: FIRE-C2: Environment-Triggered Backdoor Attacks in Wireless IoT Networks
authors: Rouaa Naim, Hams Gelban, Ahmed Badawy, Saeed Salem, Mahmoud Barhamgi
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The proliferation of IoT devices has expanded the attack surface of cyber–physical systems, enabling stealthy backdoor attacks that remain inactive until triggered by specific environmental conditions. Existing network security datasets lack the granularity, multi-modal visibility, and physical realism required to study such threats or to support the development of advanced machine learning methods for intrusion detection. To address this gap, we introduce FIRE-C2, an open-source ns-3–based simulation and dataset generation framework that models a smart-building fire monitoring environment targeted by an environmentally triggered, bidirectional command-and-control (C2) backdoor. The framework integrates physical fire dynamics, wireless communication behavior, sensor readings, and multi-stage covert channels, producing synchronized packet traces, node states, wireless metrics, and ground-truth labels for the complete backdoor lifecycle. The attack model combines temperature-triggered activation, timing-modulated beacons, LSB-based steganographic perturbations, and a downlink command channel for remote control. This design yields a realistic and difficult-to-detect adversarial pattern. FIRE-C2 enables a wide range of machine learning studies, including anomaly detection, temporal modeling, cross-layer inference, and packet-level classification under controlled and reproducible conditions. We describe the system design, dataset structure, and baseline ML benchmarks, demonstrating that FIRE-C2 serves as a comprehensive research testbed for securing IoT and Cyber-Physical System (CPS) deployments against stealthy, context-dependent backdoor attacks. The complete simulation codebase is publicly released to enable researchers to generate custom datasets with configurable topologies, attacker populations, and covert channel parameters.

title: Improving Rock Detection Accuracy by using Vision-Language Models with Textual–Visual Prompt Alignment
authors: Vincenzo Carletti, Antonio Greco, Alessia Saggese, Camilla Spingola, Bruno Vento
abstract: The reliable detection of unconventional obstacles, such as rocks, is essential for ensuring safe and continuous railway operations. However, this task is challenging due to the visual heterogeneity of rocks and the scarcity of annotated railway data. Existing vision-based systems often rely on conventional classifiers, whose generalization ability is limited in this setting. In this work, we introduce Textual-Visual Alignment for Rock Detection (TVA-Rock), a parameter-efficient textual-visual alignment framework that leverages the representational strength of vision-language models for rock detection within a two-stage pipeline comprising track segmentation and patch-wise classification. TVA-Rock employs a sequential textual-visual prompt alignment strategy, in which textual prompts are first optimized to encode task-relevant semantics, and subsequently used to guide visual prompt tuning, yielding rock-aware visual representations without modifying backbone weights. This sequential design stabilizes multimodal optimization, mitigates overfitting in data-scarce regimes, and enables efficient inference by discarding the textual branch after training. Extensive experiments on realistic onboard-camera samples show that TVA-Rock outperforms existing approaches, that are also more complex, achieving state-of-the-art performance in identifying small and visually irregular rocks. Ablation studies further validate the incremental contribution of textual prompting, visual prompting, and architectural generalization across different VLM backbones. These results demonstrate the potential of the proposed method as a robust and efficient strategy for the detection of rocks on railway tracks.

title: Reliability Characterization of GaN HEMTs under Extreme Temperature Cycling for Space Power Applications
authors: Dilip Rana, Purushottam Khadka, Tian Qiu, Zheyu Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Gallium nitride (GaN) high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) are attracting increasing attention for future space power applications due to their ability to operate at cryogenic temperatures. While GaN reliability is gradually being explored, studies specifically focused on space applications, where operating temperatures can fluctuate significantly from cryogenic to high temperatures, remain largely unexplored. This paper investigates the thermal cycling reliability of commercial 200 V GaN HEMTs by applying thermal cycles between 50 K and 400 K, mimicking lunar day-night cycle, and performing characterization throughout the stress cycles. The characterization data were analyzed to track parameter shifts and cycles to failure, which were also used to perform Weibull fitting and quantify uncertainty ranges. The results reveal negligible gradual degradation followed by abrupt failure, a behavior not commonly reported in prior GaN thermal cycling studies. The observed failure pattern was further examined and found to involve two distinct events: drain-source breakdown and gate control loss, while freewheeling conduction remained nearly unaffected. In addition, a supplementary test was performed to evaluate the effects of both characterization and thermal cycling on device failures, and the observations are reported.

title: Smart Soil Nutrient Mapping and Fertilizer Recommendation System
authors: G Shanmuka, J Sashank, G Bhavan Sai, G Kamalesh, R Rajesh
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Fertility of soils closely linked to agricultural yield because it helps to determine the balance of soil nutrients. Overuse or underuse of fertilizers can lead towards low crop production, soil erosion and soil pollution. In this paper an Internet-of-Things (IoT) based system is suggested that calculates the soil nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium levels with the help of an NPK sensor, that will be working using an ESP32 microcontroller. The data obtained is sent to MySQL cloud database for analysis. The results obtained are represented pictorially through proper visualization diagrams which will help in displaying of good, medium, and high nutrient levels. It also provides ideas on fertilizer use. This allows correct and calculated management of fertilizers, which will reduce wastage and keep soil healthy.

title: Concise Review on AI-Integrated Cluster Computing for Big Data Processing
authors: Shaymaa Q. Abdali, Ali Mahdi Ali Al-Salim, Ahmed A. Al-Moadhen
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: This review examines how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can be integrated with cluster computing to support efficient big data processing in Local Area Network (LAN) environments. The paper synthesizes prior work on LANresident cluster architecture, Hadoop and Spark-based processing frameworks, AI-enhanced analytics, and AI-assisted cluster management. It highlights how earlier cluster concepts such as Single System Image (SSI), resource coordination, and fault tolerance continue to inform modern distributed big data systems. The review also analyzes the role of Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), MapReduce, and Spark in supporting scalable storage and parallel execution, while identifying limitations related to static replication, network bottlenecks, and disk-intensive execution. In addition, it examines how AI is increasingly used not only on cluster platforms for analytics, but also for cluster optimization through predictive scheduling, parameter tuning, and adaptive control. Comparative findings show that Spark is generally better suited to iterative machine learning workloads because of its in-memory execution model, whereas MapReduce remains relevant for stable batch processing. The review further addresses security and privacy through encryption, access control, auditing, and privacy-preserving distributed learning. Overall, the surveyed literature shows that AI-integrated LAN clusters provide a practical foundation for scalable, secure, and increasingly autonomous big data systems, while revealing important open challenges in workload diversity, networkaware optimization, energy efficiency, and closed-loop intelligence.

title: A Hybrid Prophet-XGBoost Model for Weekly Leptospirosis Forecasting in the Philippines
authors: Jaymart G. Latigay, Alex C. Gonzaga
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Epidemiological signals, such as weekly cases of Leptospirosis ($y[t]$), function as non-stationary discrete-time signal that follows predictable seasonal patterns frequently disrupted by unpredictable spikes caused by several external environmental factors, most notably severe weather, especially for the Philippines. Traditional forecasting methods tend to oversmooth this signal, effectively capturing the long-term trend but missing sharp peaks that define an outbreak. To address this, we propose a novel Hybrid Prophet-XGBoost Residual Learning Framework. The architecture splits the signal $\mathbf{y}[\mathbf{t}]$ into two main parts: a stable seasonal baseline and complex error signals modeled by Prophet and XGBoost, respectively. By training XGBoost to learn the relationship between environmental triggers and these residuals, the system recovers outbreak intensity that the standard model misses. Rolling-origin validation shows that this hybrid approach significantly outperforms statistical baselines, improving MAE by roughly 30% and RMSE by and 23%, and is the only model that remained stable when evaluated during peak outbreaks. SHAP analysis corroborates this and shows that the model successfully isolates meaningful patterns, achieving high accuracy without sacrificing interpretability.

title: Control of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator based Wind Energy Generation with Solar PV Connected to 3-Phase Grid for EV charging
authors: Sanjay Kumar, Shailendra Kumar, Bhim Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid renewable energy-based micro-grid charging infrastructure for electric vehicle (EV), incorporating a three-phase grid-tied solar photovoltaic array (SPVA) and a permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG) driven by a wind turbine. A key contribution is the development of a control scheme for grid side voltage source converter (GSVSC) to regulate the common DC bus voltage at 400 V and ensure dynamic power sharing between sources using feed forward (FF). The system employs maximum power point tracking (MPPT) to optimize energy extraction from both solar and wind sources. Proposed control strategy includes primary and secondary control to improve grid integration, DC bus voltage regulation, and power quality under IEEE 519 standard. Field oriented control (FOC) is applied to the machine side voltage sources converter (MSVSC) for efficient PMSG operation. Proposed control approach applied to DC voltage regulation, grid power integrated charging infrastructure, and hybrid operating modes. It ensures reliability microgrid operation and enables an ample amount of control. Complete system is modeled in MATLAB/Simulink, and simulation results validate effectiveness of the proposed approach in both transient and steady state conditions under grid-connected and hybrid modes.

title: Multipulse Rectifier Fed Reconfigurable Interleaved Buck Converter for Electrified Hydrogen Systems
authors: Anakapalli Vasu Deva, Deepak Ronanki, Hani Vahedi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper proposes the design and analysis of a multipulse rectifier fed multi-channel reconfigurable interleaved buck converter (IBC) for electrified hydrogen generation systems. Unlike existing topologies, this configuration utilizes a 24-pulse rectifier in the AC-DC stage to minimise grid current total harmonic distortion (THD) below 5% without any control circuit and additional filters on the grid side. In the DC-DC stage, a multi-channel reconfigurable interleaved buck converter (IBC) is employed to reduce the electrolyser current ripple and enhance lifetime. Further, a new control scheme is implemented that selectively activates the required number of channels based on the power demand for efficient and reliable operation. A dual-loop control architecture comprising an inner current loop and outer voltage loop is implemented to regulate electrolyser voltage and current. The proposed system configuration, along with the selective reconfiguration scheme based on power demand, is validated using MATLAB simulations and experimental studies from a 1.2 kW laboratory prototype of the IBC stage.

title: Design and Implementation of an Integrated AES–Blockchain Framework for Secure IoT Networks
authors: Neaam R. Abdulgabbar, Salah Abdulghani Alabady
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) exposes vast amounts of sensitive information to devices with limited resources, which then spread across insecure networks. This situation leads to serious security risks, such as data manipulation, hacking, and an undesirable over-reliance on centralized storage. Sensitive data security applications should not rely on traditional cloud infrastructures, as these are inherently opaque and suffer from centralized vulnerabilities. In this study, the researcher presents a unified model combining Advanced Encryption Stamping (AES) and blockchain technology to provide encryption for IoT data during storage and transmission. The system ensures confidentiality using AES encryption with IoT-level password-generated keys before network connection. This encrypted data is sent to lightweight smart contracts on the Ethereum platform for storage on the blockchain, making it immutable. Importantly, the plaintext and encryption keys are not stored on the blockchain. This differs from existing frameworks that only store hashes or metadata while maintaining complete cryptographic datasets on the blockchain. The system was deployed in a privately hosted blockchain environment using Remix, Ganache, and MetaMask. Experiments showed that the size of the blocked cryptographic data is directly proportional to the amount of gas used; the infrastructure cost approximately 65,074 gas, and the average expansion cost was 975.6 gas per kilobyte. End-to-end payload recovery achieved a 100 % success rate while maintaining low transaction latency and limited computational costs. Overall, the results demonstrate that the proposed architecture is viable and flexible and provides verifiable cryptographic storage in IoT systems.

title: A Multi-Modal Transformer for Ultrasound-Guided Diffuse Optical Tomography
authors: Seongkwon Yu, Seok-Hwan Oh, Yuqing Liang, Jimin Lee, Bumjun Koh, Hyeon-Min Bae
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Diffuse Optical Tomography (DOT) is a non-invasive imaging modality that estimates tissue optical properties using near-infrared (NIR) light. Conventional DOT reconstruction methods suffer from poor spatial resolution and instability due to the ill-posed inverse problem. In this work, we propose MDOT-former, a multi-modal transformer that integrates B-mode ultrasound (US) imaging with DOT to enhance reconstruction accuracy. The proposed model employs a multimodal cross-attention transformer to extract morphological features from US images and improve the localization and quantification of optical properties. To facilitate model training, we introduce an in-silico simulation framework that synthesizes paired DOT and US data. We evaluate MDOT-former using both in-silico and ex-vivo experiments, demonstrating a significant improvement over state-of-the-art (SOTA) learning-based algorithms. MDOT-former achieves 95% lesion identification sensitivity and improves both structural and quantitative accuracy in lesion detection. The results highlight the potential of multi-modal deep learning in advancing DOT reconstruction for biomedical imaging applications.

title: Method for Identifying Abnormal Ship Behavior in Bridge Area Waters
authors: Yuantan Lin, Xiao Liu, Chaofeng Wang, Xingya Zhao
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: With the increasing number of bridge constructions, the collision risk between ships in bridge area waters has significantly escalated. Early identification of abnormal ship behavior is critical for accident prevention. This study addresses the constrained navigational environment in bridge waters by proposing a method for detecting abnormal ship behavior. Based on navigation regulations and safety requirements, the method focuses on three core elements—ship position, speed, and course—and establishes quantitative models as follows: Positional anomalies are identified by defining bridge risk zones and grounding hazard zones, integrating ship-specific parameters to delineate non-navigable waters. Speed anomalies are detected by calculating dynamic thresholds using safe distances from preceding ships and the required bridge passage width under wind/current influences.
